3964    status.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
3965    status.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=8)
3966    status.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=180)
3967    status.add_argument("--full", action="store_true", help="Render the full dashboard")
3968    status.add_argument("--json", action="store_true")
3969    status.set_defaults(func=cmd_status)
3970
3971    health = sub.add_parser("health")
3972    health.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=8)
3973    health.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=180)
3974    health.set_defaults(func=cmd_health)
3975
3976    history = sub.add_parser("history")
3977    history.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
3978    history.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=80)
3979    history.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=260)
3980    history.add_argument("--full", action="store_true")
3981    history.add_argument("--json", action="store_true")
3982    history.set_defaults(func=cmd_history)
3983
3984    events = sub.add_parser("events")
3985    events.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
3986    events.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=80)
3987    events.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=260)
3988    events.add_argument("--full", action="store_true")
3989    events.add_argument("--follow", action="store_true")
3990    events.add_argument("--interval", type=float, default=2.0)
3991    events.add_argument("--json", action="store_true")
3992    events.set_defaults(func=cmd_events)
3993
3994    dashboard = sub.add_parser("dashboard", aliases=["dash"])
3995    dashboard.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
3996    dashboard.add_argument("--interval", type=float, default=2.0)
3997    dashboard.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=12)
3998    dashboard.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=260)
3999    dashboard.add_argument("--no-follow", dest="follow", action="store_false")
4000    dashboard.add_argument("--no-clear", dest="clear", action="store_false")
4001    dashboard.set_defaults(func=cmd_dashboard, follow=True, clear=True)
4002
4003    start = sub.add_parser("start")
4004    start.add_argument("--poll-seconds", type=float, default=0.0)
4005    start.add_argument("--fake", action="store_true", help="Use deterministic fake model responses")
4006    start.add_argument("--quiet", action="store_true", help="Write fewer daemon log lines")
4007    start.add_argument("--log-file")
4008    start.set_defaults(func=cmd_start)
4009
4010    stop = sub.add_parser("stop")
4011    stop.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*", help="Optional job title/id to pause instead of stopping the daemon")
4012    stop.add_argument("--wait", type=float, default=5.0)
4013    stop.set_defaults(func=cmd_stop)
4014
4015    restart = sub.add_parser("restart")
4016    restart.add_argument("--poll-seconds", type=float, default=0.0)
4017    restart.add_argument("--wait", type=float, default=5.0)
4018    restart.add_argument("--fake", action="store_true", help="Use deterministic fake model responses")
4019    restart.add_argument("--quiet", action="store_true", help="Write fewer daemon log lines")
4020    restart.add_argument("--log-file")
4021    restart.set_defaults(func=cmd_restart)
4022
4023    browser_dashboard = sub.add_parser("browser-dashboard")
4024    browser_dashboard.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=4848)
4025    browser_dashboard.add_argument("--foreground", action="store_true")
4026    browser_dashboard.add_argument("--stop", action="store_true")
4027    browser_dashboard.add_argument("--log-file")
4028    browser_dashboard.set_defaults(func=cmd_browser_dashboard)
4029
4030    autostart = sub.add_parser("autostart")
4031    autostart.add_argument("action", choices=["install", "status", "uninstall"])
4032    autostart.add_argument("--poll-seconds", type=float, default=5.0)
4033    autostart.add_argument("--quiet", action="store_true")
4034    autostart.set_defaults(func=cmd_autostart)
4035
4036    service = sub.add_parser("service")
4037    service.add_argument("action", choices=["install", "status", "uninstall"])
4038    service.add_argument("--poll-seconds", type=float, default=0.0)
4039    service.add_argument("--quiet", action="store_true")
4040    service.set_defaults(func=cmd_service)
4041
4042    artifacts = sub.add_parser("artifacts")
4043    artifacts.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4044    artifacts.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=25)
4045    artifacts.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=220)
4046    artifacts.add_argument("--paths", action="store_true", help="Show full artifact paths")
4047    artifacts.set_defaults(func=cmd_artifacts)
4048
4049    artifact = sub.add_parser("artifact")
4050    artifact.add_argument("artifact_id_or_path", nargs="+")
4051    artifact.add_argument("--job", dest="job_id")
4052    artifact.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=12000)
4053    artifact.set_defaults(func=cmd_artifact)
4054
4055    lessons = sub.add_parser("lessons")
4056    lessons.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4057    lessons.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=25)
4058    lessons.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=220)
4059    lessons.set_defaults(func=cmd_lessons)
4060
4061    learn = sub.add_parser("learn")
4062    learn.add_argument("--job", dest="job_id")
4063    learn.add_argument("--category", default="operator_preference")
4064    learn.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=220)
4065    learn.add_argument("lesson", nargs="+")
4066    learn.set_defaults(func=cmd_learn)
4067
4068    findings = sub.add_parser("findings")
4069    findings.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4070    findings.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=25)
4071    findings.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=220)
4072    findings.add_argument("--json", action="store_true")
4073    findings.set_defaults(func=cmd_findings)
4074
4075    tasks = sub.add_parser("tasks")
4076    tasks.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4077    tasks.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=25)
4078    tasks.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=220)
4079    tasks.add_argument("--status", nargs="+")
4080    tasks.add_argument("--json", action="store_true")
4081    tasks.set_defaults(func=cmd_tasks)
4082
4083    roadmap = sub.add_parser("roadmap")
4084    roadmap.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4085    roadmap.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=25)
4086    roadmap.add_argument("--features", type=int, default=3)
4087    roadmap.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=220)
4088    roadmap.add_argument("--json", action="store_true")
4089    roadmap.set_defaults(func=cmd_roadmap)
4090
4091    experiments = sub.add_parser("experiments")
4092    experiments.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4093    experiments.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=25)
4094    experiments.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=220)
4095    experiments.add_argument("--status", nargs="+")
4096    experiments.add_argument("--json", action="store_true")
4097    experiments.set_defaults(func=cmd_experiments)
4098
4099    sources = sub.add_parser("sources")
4100    sources.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4101    sources.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=25)
4102    sources.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=220)
4103    sources.add_argument("--json", action="store_true")
4104    sources.set_defaults(func=cmd_sources)
4105
4106    memory = sub.add_parser("memory")
4107    memory.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4108    memory.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=10)
4109    memory.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=260)
4110    memory.set_defaults(func=cmd_memory)
4111
4112    metrics = sub.add_parser("metrics")
4113    metrics.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4114    metrics.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=220)
4115    metrics.set_defaults(func=cmd_metrics)
4116
4117    usage = sub.add_parser("usage")
4118    usage.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4119    usage.add_argument("--json", action="store_true")
4120    usage.set_defaults(func=cmd_usage)
4121
4122    logs = sub.add_parser("logs", aliases=["outputs", "output"])
4123    logs.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4124    logs.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=25)
4125    logs.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true")
4126    logs.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=4000)
4127    logs.set_defaults(func=cmd_logs)
4128
4129    activity = sub.add_parser("activity", aliases=["feed", "tail"])
4130    activity.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4131    activity.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=20)
4132    activity.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=180)
4133    activity.add_argument("--follow", action="store_true")
4134    activity.add_argument("--interval", type=float, default=2.0)
4135    activity.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true")
4136    activity.add_argument("--paths", action="store_true", help="Show full artifact paths")
4137    activity.set_defaults(func=cmd_activity)
4138
4139    updates = sub.add_parser("updates", aliases=["update", "outcomes", "outcome"])
4140    updates.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4141    updates.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=5)
4142    updates.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=180)
4143    updates.add_argument("--paths", action="store_true", help="Show full artifact paths")
4144    updates.set_defaults(func=cmd_updates)
4145
4146    watch = sub.add_parser("watch")
4147    watch.add_argument("job_id", nargs="+")
4148    watch.add_argument("--interval", type=float, default=2.0)
4149    watch.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=20)
4150    watch.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true")
4151    watch.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=4000)
4152    watch.add_argument("--no-follow", dest="follow", action="store_false")
4153    watch.set_defaults(func=cmd_watch, follow=True)
4154
4155    run_one = sub.add_parser("run-one")
4156    run_one.add_argument("job_id", nargs="+")
4157    run_one.add_argument("--fake", action="store_true", help="Use a deterministic fake model response")
4158    run_one.set_defaults(func=cmd_run_one)
4159
4160    work = sub.add_parser("work")
4161    work.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4162    work.add_argument("--steps", type=int, default=5)
4163    work.add_argument("--poll-seconds", type=float, default=0.5)
4164    work.add_argument("--fake", action="store_true", help="Use deterministic fake model responses")
4165    work.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true", help="Print step inputs and outputs")
4166    work.add_argument("--dashboard", action="store_true", help="Render a dashboard snapshot after each step")
4167    work.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=12)
4168    work.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=4000)
4169    work.add_argument("--continue-on-error", action="store_true")
4170    work.set_defaults(func=cmd_work)
4171
4172    run = sub.add_parser("run")
4173    run.add_argument("job_id", nargs="*")
4174    run.add_argument("--poll-seconds", type=float, default=0.0)
4175    run.add_argument("--interval", type=float, default=2.0)
4176    run.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=20)
4177    run.add_argument("--chars", type=int, default=180)
4178    run.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true")
4179    run.add_argument("--paths", action="store_true")
4180    run.add_argument("--fake", action="store_true", help="Use deterministic fake model responses")
4181    run.add_argument("--quiet", action="store_true", help="Write fewer daemon log lines")
4182    run.add_argument("--log-file")
4183    run.add_argument("--no-follow", action="store_true", help="Start daemon and return without tailing activity")
4184    run.set_defaults(func=cmd_run)
4185
4186    digest = sub.add_parser("digest")
4187    digest.add_argument("job_id", nargs="+")
4188    digest.set_defaults(func=cmd_digest)
4189
4190    daily_digest = sub.add_parser("daily-digest")
4191    daily_digest.add_argument("--day", help="YYYY-MM-DD. Defaults to today.")
4192    daily_digest.set_defaults(func=cmd_daily_digest)
4193
4194    daemon = sub.add_parser("daemon")
4195    daemon.add_argument("--once", action="store_true", help="Run at most one job step and exit")
4196    daemon.add_argument("--fake", action="store_true", help="Use deterministic fake model responses")
4197    daemon.add_argument("--poll-seconds", type=float, default=0.0)
4198    daemon.add_argument("--quiet", action="store_true", help="Do not print foreground progress lines")
4199    daemon.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true", help="Print model-visible job state and step results")
4200    daemon.set_defaults(func=cmd_daemon)
4201
4202    doctor = sub.add_parser("doctor")
4203    doctor.add_argument("--check-model", action="store_true", help="Also call the local model /models endpoint")
4204    doctor.set_defaults(func=cmd_doctor)
4205
4206    return parser
4207
4208
4209def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> None:
4210    argv = sys.argv[1:] if argv is None else argv
4211    if not argv:
4212        cmd_home(argparse.Namespace(history_limit=12))
4213        return
4214    parser = build_parser()
4215    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
4216    args.func(args)
4217
4218
4219if __name__ == "__main__":
4220    main()
nipux_cli/compression.py 198 lines 
   1"""Deterministic rolling memory summaries for long-running jobs."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5from nipux_cli.db import AgentDB
   6from nipux_cli.operator_context import active_prompt_operator_entries
   7
   8
   9def _clip_text(value: object, limit: int) -> str:
  10    text = " ".join(str(value or "").split())
  11    if len(text) <= limit:
  12        return text
  13    return text[: max(0, limit - 3)].rstrip() + "..."
  14
  15
  16def refresh_memory_index(db: AgentDB, job_id: str, *, max_steps: int = 8, max_artifacts: int = 8) -> str:
  17    """Write a compact, artifact-referenced job memory entry.
  18
  19    This is deliberately deterministic. A local model can later improve the
  20    prose, but the daemon should always have a cheap compaction path that runs
  21    after every step and survives model failures.
  22    """
  23
  24    job = db.get_job(job_id)
  25    steps = db.list_steps(job_id=job_id)[-max_steps:]
  26    artifacts = db.list_artifacts(job_id, limit=max_artifacts)
  27    artifact_refs = [artifact["id"] for artifact in artifacts]
  28    metadata = job.get("metadata") if isinstance(job.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
  29    operator_messages = metadata.get("operator_messages") if isinstance(metadata.get("operator_messages"), list) else []
  30    active_operator = [
  31        entry
  32        for entry in active_prompt_operator_entries(operator_messages)
  33        if str(entry.get("mode") or "steer") in {"steer", "follow_up"}
  34    ][-5:]
  35    operator_notes = [
  36        entry for entry in operator_messages
  37        if isinstance(entry, dict)
  38        and str(entry.get("mode") or "steer") == "note"
  39    ][-3:]
  40
  41    lines = [
  42        f"Job lifecycle status: {job['status']}",
  43        f"Objective: {job['objective']}",
  44        "",
  45        "Active operator context:",
  46    ]
  47    if not active_operator and not operator_notes:
  48        lines.append("- none")
  49    for entry in active_operator:
  50        lines.append(
  51            f"- {entry.get('mode') or 'steer'} {entry.get('event_id') or ''}: "
  52            f"{_clip_text(entry.get('message') or '', 300)}"
  53        )
  54    for entry in operator_notes:
  55        lines.append(f"- note {entry.get('event_id') or ''}: {_clip_text(entry.get('message') or '', 300)}")
  56
  57    lines.extend([
  58        "",
  59        "Recent steps:",
  60    ])
  61    if not steps:
  62        lines.append("- none")
  63    for step in steps:
  64        tool = f" tool={step['tool_name']}" if step.get("tool_name") else ""
  65        summary = step.get("summary") or step.get("error") or ""
  66        lines.append(f"- #{step['step_no']} {step['kind']} {step['status']}{tool}: {_clip_text(summary, 280)}")
  67
  68    lines.extend(["", "Recent artifacts:"])
  69    if not artifacts:
  70        lines.append("- none")
  71    for artifact in artifacts:
  72        title = artifact.get("title") or artifact["id"]
  73        summary = artifact.get("summary") or ""
  74        lines.append(f"- {artifact['id']} {_clip_text(title, 120)} ({artifact['type']}): {_clip_text(summary, 240)}")
  75
  76    tasks = _metadata_list(metadata, "task_queue")
  77    findings = _metadata_list(metadata, "finding_ledger")
  78    sources = _metadata_list(metadata, "source_ledger")
  79    experiments = _metadata_list(metadata, "experiment_ledger")
  80    roadmap = metadata.get("roadmap") if isinstance(metadata.get("roadmap"), dict) else {}
  81    pending_measurement = (
  82        metadata.get("pending_measurement_obligation")
  83        if isinstance(metadata.get("pending_measurement_obligation"), dict)
  84        and metadata.get("pending_measurement_obligation")
  85        and not metadata.get("pending_measurement_obligation", {}).get("resolved_at")
  86        else {}
  87    )
  88
  89    lines.extend(["", "Durable progress ledgers:"])
  90    lines.append(
  91        "- "
  92        + ", ".join(
  93            [
  94                f"tasks={len(tasks)}",
  95                f"findings={len(findings)}",
  96                f"sources={len(sources)}",
  97                f"experiments={len(experiments)}",
  98                f"roadmap={'yes' if roadmap else 'no'}",
  99            ]
 100        )
 101    )
 102    for task in _rank_tasks(tasks)[:4]:
 103        lines.append(
 104            "- task "
 105            f"{task.get('status') or 'open'} "
 106            f"{_clip_text(task.get('title') or '', 120)} "
 107            f"contract={task.get('output_contract') or '?'}"
 108        )
 109    for experiment in experiments[-3:]:
 110        metric = ""
 111        if experiment.get("metric_value") not in (None, ""):
 112            metric = (
 113                f" metric={experiment.get('metric_name') or 'value'}="
 114                f"{experiment.get('metric_value')}{experiment.get('metric_unit') or ''}"
 115            )
 116        lines.append(
 117            "- experiment "
 118            f"{experiment.get('status') or 'planned'} "
 119            f"{_clip_text(experiment.get('title') or '', 120)}{metric}"
 120        )
 121    if pending_measurement:
 122        candidates = pending_measurement.get("metric_candidates")
 123        candidate_text = "; ".join(str(item) for item in candidates[:3]) if isinstance(candidates, list) else ""
 124        lines.append(
 125            "- pending_measurement "
 126            f"step=#{pending_measurement.get('source_step_no') or '?'} "
 127            f"tool={pending_measurement.get('tool') or '?'} "
 128            f"{_clip_text(candidate_text or pending_measurement.get('summary') or '', 220)}"
 129        )
 130    for finding in findings[-3:]:
 131        lines.append(f"- finding {_clip_text(finding.get('name') or finding.get('title') or '', 140)}")
 132    for source in sources[-3:]:
 133        score = source.get("usefulness_score")
 134        lines.append(f"- source {_clip_text(source.get('source') or '', 140)} score={score if score is not None else '?'}")
 135    if roadmap:
 136        lines.append(
 137            "- roadmap "
 138            f"{roadmap.get('status') or 'planned'} "
 139            f"{_clip_text(roadmap.get('title') or 'Roadmap', 140)} "
 140            f"current={_clip_text(roadmap.get('current_milestone') or '', 120)}"
 141        )
 142
 143    usage = db.job_token_usage(job_id)
 144    if int(usage.get("calls") or 0) > 0:
 145        lines.extend(["", "Model usage:"])
 146        latest_prompt = _compact_count(usage.get("latest_prompt_tokens"))
 147        latest_total = _compact_count(usage.get("latest_total_tokens"))
 148        lines.append(
 149            "- "
 150            + ", ".join(
 151                [
 152                    f"calls={usage.get('calls') or 0}",
 153                    f"total_tokens={_compact_count(usage.get('total_tokens'))}",
 154                    f"output_tokens={_compact_count(usage.get('completion_tokens'))}",
 155                    f"latest_context={latest_prompt}",
 156                    f"latest_total={latest_total}",
 157                    f"estimated_calls={usage.get('estimated_calls') or 0}",
 158                ]
 159            )
 160        )
 161
 162    return db.upsert_memory(
 163        job_id=job_id,
 164        key="rolling_state",
 165        summary="\n".join(lines).strip(),
 166        artifact_refs=artifact_refs,
 167    )
 168
 169
 170def _metadata_list(metadata: dict, key: str) -> list[dict]:
 171    values = metadata.get(key)
 172    if not isinstance(values, list):
 173        return []
 174    return [value for value in values if isinstance(value, dict)]
 175
 176
 177def _rank_tasks(tasks: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
 178    status_rank = {"active": 0, "open": 1, "blocked": 2, "validating": 3, "done": 4, "skipped": 5}
 179    return sorted(
 180        tasks,
 181        key=lambda task: (
 182            status_rank.get(str(task.get("status") or "open"), 9),
 183            -int(task.get("priority") or 0),
 184            str(task.get("title") or ""),
 185        ),
 186    )
 187
 188
 189def _compact_count(value: object) -> str:
 190    try:
 191        number = int(float(value or 0))
 192    except (TypeError, ValueError):
 193        number = 0
 194    if number >= 1_000_000:
 195        return f"{number / 1_000_000:.1f}M"
 196    if number >= 1_000:
 197        return f"{number / 1_000:.1f}K"
 198    return str(number)
nipux_cli/config.py 201 lines 
   1"""Configuration for the Nipux long-running agent runtime."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import os
   6from dataclasses import dataclass, field
   7from pathlib import Path
   8from typing import Any
   9
  10import yaml
  11
  12
  13DEFAULT_OPENROUTER_MODEL = "qwen/qwen3.6-27b"
  14DEFAULT_MODEL = DEFAULT_OPENROUTER_MODEL
  15DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
  16DEFAULT_API_KEY_ENV = <redacted>
  17DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LENGTH = 262_144
  18
  19
  20def get_agent_home() -> Path:
  21    """Return the Nipux agent home directory."""
  22
  23    value = os.environ.get("NIPUX_HOME", "").strip()
  24    return Path(value).expanduser() if value else Path.home() / ".nipux"
  25
  26
  27def load_env_file(path: str | Path) -> None:
  28    """Load KEY=value pairs from a local env file without overriding the shell."""
  29
  30    env_path = Path(path).expanduser()
  31    if not env_path.exists():
  32        return
  33    for raw_line in env_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
  34        line = raw_line.strip()
  35        if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
  36            continue
  37        key, value = line.split("=", 1)
  38        key = key.strip()
  39        value = value.strip().strip("\"'")
  40        if key and key not in os.environ:
  41            os.environ[key] = value
  42
  43
  44@dataclass(frozen=True)
  45class ModelConfig:
  46    model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL
  47    base_url: str = DEFAULT_BASE_URL
  48    api_key_env: str = DEFAULT_API_KEY_ENV
  49    context_length: int = DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LENGTH
  50    request_timeout_seconds: float = 120.0
  51    input_cost_per_million: float | None = None
  52    output_cost_per_million: float | None = None
  53
  54    @property
  55    def api_key(self) -> str:
  56        return os.environ.get(self.api_key_env, "")
  57
  58
  59@dataclass(frozen=True)
  60class RuntimeConfig:
  61    home: Path = field(default_factory=get_agent_home)
  62    max_step_seconds: int = 600
  63    max_steps_per_run: int = 1
  64    artifact_inline_char_limit: int = 12_000
  65    daily_digest_enabled: bool = True
  66    daily_digest_time: str = "08:00"
  67
  68    @property
  69    def state_db_path(self) -> Path:
  70        return self.home / "state.db"
  71
  72    @property
  73    def jobs_dir(self) -> Path:
  74        return self.home / "jobs"
  75
  76    @property
  77    def logs_dir(self) -> Path:
  78        return self.home / "logs"
  79
  80    @property
  81    def digests_dir(self) -> Path:
  82        return self.home / "digests"
  83
  84
  85@dataclass(frozen=True)
  86class EmailConfig:
  87    enabled: bool = False
  88    smtp_host: str = ""
  89    smtp_port: int = 587
  90    username: str = ""
  91    password_env: str = "NIPUX_EMAIL_PASSWORD"
  92    from_addr: str = ""
  93    to_addr: str = ""
  94    use_tls: bool = True
  95
  96    @property
  97    def password(self) -> str:
  98        return os.environ.get(self.password_env, "")
  99
 100
 101@dataclass(frozen=True)
 102class AppConfig:
 103    runtime: RuntimeConfig = field(default_factory=RuntimeConfig)
 104    model: ModelConfig = field(default_factory=ModelConfig)
 105    email: EmailConfig = field(default_factory=EmailConfig)
 106
 107    def ensure_dirs(self) -> None:
 108        self.runtime.home.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 109        self.runtime.jobs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 110        self.runtime.logs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 111        self.runtime.digests_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 112
 113
 114def _as_dict(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
 115    return value if isinstance(value, dict) else {}
 116
 117
 118def _optional_float(value: Any) -> float | None:
 119    if value in (None, ""):
 120        return None
 121    return float(value)
 122
 123
 124def load_config(path: str | Path | None = None) -> AppConfig:
 125    """Load config.yaml, falling back to the default OpenAI-compatible provider."""
 126
 127    home = get_agent_home()
 128    load_env_file(home / ".env")
 129    cfg_path = Path(path).expanduser() if path else home / "config.yaml"
 130    raw: dict[str, Any] = {}
 131    if cfg_path.exists():
 132        loaded = yaml.safe_load(cfg_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) or {}
 133        raw = _as_dict(loaded)
 134
 135    runtime_raw = _as_dict(raw.get("runtime"))
 136    model_raw = _as_dict(raw.get("model"))
 137    email_raw = _as_dict(raw.get("email"))
 138
 139    runtime_home = Path(runtime_raw.get("home") or home).expanduser()
 140    runtime = RuntimeConfig(
 141        home=runtime_home,
 142        max_step_seconds=int(runtime_raw.get("max_step_seconds", 600)),
 143        max_steps_per_run=int(runtime_raw.get("max_steps_per_run", 1)),
 144        artifact_inline_char_limit=int(runtime_raw.get("artifact_inline_char_limit", 12_000)),
 145        daily_digest_enabled=bool(runtime_raw.get("daily_digest_enabled", True)),
 146        daily_digest_time=str(runtime_raw.get("daily_digest_time") or "08:00"),
 147    )
 148    model = ModelConfig(
 149        model=str(model_raw.get("name") or model_raw.get("model") or DEFAULT_MODEL),
 150        base_url=str(model_raw.get("base_url") or DEFAULT_BASE_URL).rstrip("/"),
 151        api_key_env=str(model_raw.get("api_key_env") or DEFAULT_API_KEY_ENV),
 152        context_length=int(model_raw.get("context_length", DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LENGTH)),
 153        request_timeout_seconds=float(model_raw.get("request_timeout_seconds", 120.0)),
 154        input_cost_per_million=_optional_float(model_raw.get("input_cost_per_million")),
 155        output_cost_per_million=_optional_float(model_raw.get("output_cost_per_million")),
 156    )
 157    email = EmailConfig(
 158        enabled=bool(email_raw.get("enabled", False)),
 159        smtp_host=str(email_raw.get("smtp_host") or ""),
 160        smtp_port=int(email_raw.get("smtp_port", 587)),
 161        username=str(email_raw.get("username") or ""),
 162        password_env=str(email_raw.get("password_env") or "NIPUX_EMAIL_PASSWORD"),
 163        from_addr=str(email_raw.get("from_addr") or ""),
 164        to_addr=str(email_raw.get("to_addr") or ""),
 165        use_tls=bool(email_raw.get("use_tls", True)),
 166    )
 167    return AppConfig(runtime=runtime, model=model, email=email)
 168
 169
 170def default_config_yaml(
 171    *,
 172    model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL,
 173    base_url: str = DEFAULT_BASE_URL,
 174    api_key_env: str = DEFAULT_API_KEY_ENV,
 175    context_length: int = DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LENGTH,
 176) -> str:
 177    """Return a starter config file for an OpenAI-compatible model server."""
 178
 179    return (
 180        "model:\n"
 181        f"  name: {model}\n"
 182        f"  base_url: {base_url.rstrip('/')}\n"
 183        f"  api_key_env: {api_key_env}\n"
 184        f"  context_length: {context_length}\n"
 185        "  input_cost_per_million: null\n"
 186        "  output_cost_per_million: null\n"
 187        "runtime:\n"
 188        "  max_step_seconds: 600\n"
 189        "  max_steps_per_run: 1\n"
 190        "  artifact_inline_char_limit: 12000\n"
 191        "  daily_digest_enabled: true\n"
 192        "  daily_digest_time: \"08:00\"\n"
 193        "email:\n"
 194        "  enabled: false\n"
 195        "  smtp_host: \"\"\n"
 196        "  smtp_port: 587\n"
 197        "  username: \"\"\n"
 198        "  password_env: NIPUX_EMAIL_PASSWORD\n"
 199        "  from_addr: \"\"\n"
 200        "  to_addr: \"\"\n"
 201    )
nipux_cli/daemon.py 599 lines 
   1"""Daemon runner for restartable background jobs."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import contextlib
   6import fcntl
   7import hashlib
   8import json
   9import os
  10import signal
  11import time
  12from dataclasses import dataclass
  13from datetime import datetime, timezone
  14from email.utils import parsedate_to_datetime
  15from functools import lru_cache
  16from pathlib import Path
  17from typing import Any
  18
  19from nipux_cli.config import AppConfig, load_config
  20from nipux_cli.db import AgentDB
  21from nipux_cli.digest import write_daily_digest
  22from nipux_cli.scheduling import job_deferred_until, job_is_deferred
  23
  24
  25class DaemonAlreadyRunning(RuntimeError):
  26    pass
  27
  28
  29RUNTIME_CODE_FILES = (
  30    "artifacts.py",
  31    "browser.py",
  32    "browser_web.py",
  33    "compression.py",
  34    "config.py",
  35    "daemon.py",
  36    "db.py",
  37    "digest.py",
  38    "llm.py",
  39    "operator_context.py",
  40    "progress.py",
  41    "source_quality.py",
  42    "templates.py",
  43    "tools.py",
  44    "worker.py",
  45)
  46
  47
  48@lru_cache(maxsize=1)
  49def current_runtime_fingerprint() -> dict[str, Any]:
  50    """Return a stable fingerprint for code that affects daemon behavior."""
  51
  52    from nipux_cli import __version__
  53    from nipux_cli.tools import DEFAULT_REGISTRY
  54    from nipux_cli.worker import SYSTEM_PROMPT, WORKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION
  55
  56    tool_schema = DEFAULT_REGISTRY.openai_tools()
  57    tool_schema_hash = hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(tool_schema, sort_keys=True, default=str).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
  58    prompt_hash = hashlib.sha256(SYSTEM_PROMPT.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
  59    code_fingerprint = _runtime_code_fingerprint()
  60    payload = {
  61        "nipux_version": __version__,
  62        "worker_protocol": WORKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
  63        "tool_schema_hash": tool_schema_hash[:16],
  64        "prompt_hash": prompt_hash[:16],
  65        "code_hash": code_fingerprint["code_hash"],
  66        "code_mtime": code_fingerprint["code_mtime"],
  67        "tool_count": len(DEFAULT_REGISTRY.names()),
  68    }
  69    payload["runtime_hash"] = hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:16]
  70    return payload
  71
  72
  73@lru_cache(maxsize=1)
  74def _runtime_code_fingerprint() -> dict[str, Any]:
  75    package_dir = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
  76    digest = hashlib.sha256()
  77    mtimes: list[float] = []
  78    for name in RUNTIME_CODE_FILES:
  79        path = package_dir / name
  80        if not path.exists():
  81            continue
  82        digest.update(name.encode("utf-8"))
  83        data = path.read_bytes()
  84        digest.update(hashlib.sha256(data).digest())
  85        mtimes.append(path.stat().st_mtime)
  86    return {
  87        "code_hash": digest.hexdigest()[:16],
  88        "code_mtime": max(mtimes) if mtimes else 0,
  89    }
  90
  91
  92def runtime_stale(metadata: dict[str, Any] | None) -> bool:
  93    if not isinstance(metadata, dict):
  94        return False
  95    recorded = metadata.get("runtime")
  96    if not isinstance(recorded, dict):
  97        return True
  98    return recorded.get("runtime_hash") != current_runtime_fingerprint().get("runtime_hash")
  99
 100
 101def _parse_lock_metadata(raw: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
 102    raw = raw.strip()
 103    if not raw:
 104        return {}
 105    try:
 106        parsed = json.loads(raw)
 107        return parsed if isinstance(parsed, dict) else {"raw": raw}
 108    except json.JSONDecodeError:
 109        return {"raw": raw}
 110
 111
 112def daemon_lock_status(path: str | Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
 113    """Return whether another process currently holds the daemon lock."""
 114
 115    path = Path(path)
 116    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 117    with path.open("a+", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
 118        handle.seek(0)
 119        metadata = _parse_lock_metadata(handle.read())
 120        try:
 121            fcntl.flock(handle.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_EX | fcntl.LOCK_NB)
 122        except BlockingIOError:
 123            stale = runtime_stale(metadata)
 124            return {
 125                "running": True,
 126                "lock_path": str(path),
 127                "metadata": metadata,
 128                "stale": stale,
 129                "current_runtime": current_runtime_fingerprint(),
 130                "detail": "daemon lock is held",
 131            }
 132        with contextlib.suppress(OSError):
 133            fcntl.flock(handle.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_UN)
 134    return {
 135        "running": False,
 136        "lock_path": str(path),
 137        "metadata": metadata,
 138        "stale": False,
 139        "current_runtime": current_runtime_fingerprint(),
 140        "detail": "daemon lock is free",
 141    }
 142
 143
 144@contextlib.contextmanager
 145def single_instance_lock(path: str | Path):
 146    """Hold an exclusive non-blocking daemon lock for this state directory."""
 147
 148    path = Path(path)
 149    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 150    with path.open("w+", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
 151        try:
 152            fcntl.flock(handle.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_EX | fcntl.LOCK_NB)
 153        except BlockingIOError as exc:
 154            raise DaemonAlreadyRunning(f"Another nipux daemon holds {path}") from exc
 155        payload = {
 156            "pid": os.getpid(),
 157            "started_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 158            "runtime": current_runtime_fingerprint(),
 159        }
 160        handle.seek(0)
 161        handle.truncate()
 162        handle.write(json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True))
 163        handle.flush()
 164        try:
 165            yield handle
 166        finally:
 167            fcntl.flock(handle.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_UN)
 168
 169
 170def update_lock_metadata(handle, **patch: Any) -> None:
 171    handle.seek(0)
 172    metadata = _parse_lock_metadata(handle.read())
 173    metadata.update(patch)
 174    handle.seek(0)
 175    handle.truncate()
 176    handle.write(json.dumps(metadata, sort_keys=True))
 177    handle.flush()
 178
 179
 180def append_daemon_event(config: AppConfig, event: str, **fields: Any) -> Path:
 181    """Append a small daemon event that the CLI can tail without parsing stdout."""
 182
 183    config.ensure_dirs()
 184    path = config.runtime.logs_dir / "daemon-events.jsonl"
 185    payload = {
 186        "at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 187        "event": event,
 188        **fields,
 189    }
 190    with path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
 191        handle.write(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n")
 192    return path
 193
 194
 195def read_daemon_events(config: AppConfig, *, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
 196    path = config.runtime.logs_dir / "daemon-events.jsonl"
 197    if not path.exists():
 198        return []
 199    lines = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace").splitlines()[-limit:]
 200    events: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 201    for line in lines:
 202        try:
 203            parsed = json.loads(line)
 204        except json.JSONDecodeError:
 205            events.append({"event": "unparseable", "raw": line})
 206            continue
 207        if isinstance(parsed, dict):
 208            events.append(parsed)
 209    return events
 210
 211
 212def fake_step_llm():
 213    from nipux_cli.llm import LLMResponse, ScriptedLLM, ToolCall
 214
 215    nonce = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
 216    return ScriptedLLM([
 217        LLMResponse(tool_calls=[
 218            ToolCall(
 219                name="write_artifact",
 220                arguments={
 221                    "title": "daemon-fake-step",
 222                    "type": "text",
 223                    "summary": "Fake daemon step",
 224                    "content": f"This is a fake daemon worker step.\n\nnonce: {nonce}",
 225                },
 226            )
 227        ])
 228    ])
 229
 230
 231@dataclass
 232class Daemon:
 233    config: AppConfig
 234    db: AgentDB
 235
 236    @classmethod
 237    def open(cls, config: AppConfig | None = None) -> "Daemon":
 238        config = config or load_config()
 239        config.ensure_dirs()
 240        return cls(config=config, db=AgentDB(config.runtime.state_db_path))
 241
 242    @property
 243    def lock_path(self) -> Path:
 244        return self.config.runtime.home / "agentd.lock"
 245
 246    def close(self) -> None:
 247        self.db.close()
 248
 249    def next_runnable_job(self) -> dict | None:
 250        """Return the next runnable job by priority/age.
 251
 252        UI focus is intentionally not used here. Focus is for the operator's
 253        chat view; the daemon should keep all runnable jobs advancing.
 254        """
 255
 256        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
 257        for job in self.db.list_jobs(statuses=["queued", "running"]):
 258            if job_is_deferred(job, now=now):
 259                continue
 260            return job
 261        return None
 262
 263    def idle_sleep_seconds(self, *, poll_seconds: float, now: datetime | None = None) -> float:
 264        """Return the next idle sleep, capped by the nearest deferred job wake."""
 265
 266        fallback = max(5.0, poll_seconds)
 267        now = (now or datetime.now(timezone.utc)).astimezone(timezone.utc)
 268        due_times: list[datetime] = []
 269        for job in self.db.list_jobs(statuses=["queued", "running"]):
 270            due = job_deferred_until(job, now=now)
 271            if due is not None:
 272                due_times.append(due)
 273        if not due_times:
 274            return fallback
 275        wait_seconds = min((due - now).total_seconds() for due in due_times)
 276        return max(0.5, min(fallback, wait_seconds))
 277
 278    def run_once(self, *, fake: bool = False, verbose: bool = False):
 279        from nipux_cli.worker import run_one_step
 280
 281        job = self.next_runnable_job()
 282        if job is None:
 283            return None
 284        if verbose:
 285            print(f"thinking job={job['id']} title={job['title']} kind={job['kind']}", flush=True)
 286            print(f"objective: {job['objective']}", flush=True)
 287        llm = fake_step_llm() if fake else None
 288        return run_one_step(job["id"], config=self.config, db=self.db, llm=llm)
 289
 290    def send_due_daily_digest(self, *, now: datetime | None = None) -> dict | None:
 291        if not self.config.runtime.daily_digest_enabled:
 292            return None
 293        now = now or datetime.now()
 294        if not _is_digest_due(now, self.config.runtime.daily_digest_time):
 295            return None
 296        day = now.date().isoformat()
 297        target = self.config.email.to_addr or "dry-run"
 298        if self.db.digest_exists(day=day, target=target):
 299            return None
 300        return write_daily_digest(self.config, self.db, day=day)
 301
 302    def run_forever(
 303        self,
 304        *,
 305        fake: bool = False,
 306        poll_seconds: float = 30.0,
 307        quiet: bool = False,
 308        verbose: bool = False,
 309        max_iterations: int | None = None,
 310    ) -> None:
 311        consecutive_failures = 0
 312        iterations = 0
 313        with single_instance_lock(self.lock_path) as lock_handle:
 314            previous_sigterm = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
 315            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _raise_keyboard_interrupt)
 316            recovered = self.db.mark_interrupted_running(reason="daemon recovered abandoned running work from a previous process")
 317            append_daemon_event(
 318                self.config,
 319                "daemon_started",
 320                pid=os.getpid(),
 321                fake=fake,
 322                poll_seconds=poll_seconds,
 323                recovered_steps=recovered["steps"],
 324                recovered_runs=recovered["runs"],
 325                runtime=current_runtime_fingerprint(),
 326            )
 327            if recovered["steps"] or recovered["runs"]:
 328                append_daemon_event(self.config, "stale_work_recovered", **recovered)
 329            if not quiet:
 330                print(f"nipux daemon started; db={self.config.runtime.state_db_path}", flush=True)
 331            try:
 332                while True:
 333                    iterations += 1
 334                    update_lock_metadata(
 335                        lock_handle,
 336                        last_heartbeat=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 337                        last_state="checking",
 338                        consecutive_failures=consecutive_failures,
 339                        runtime=current_runtime_fingerprint(),
 340                    )
 341
 342                    try:
 343                        digest = self.send_due_daily_digest()
 344                        if digest:
 345                            append_daemon_event(self.config, "daily_digest", **digest)
 346                            if not quiet:
 347                                print(f"daily_digest {json.dumps(digest, ensure_ascii=False)}", flush=True)
 348                        result = self.run_once(fake=fake, verbose=verbose and not quiet)
 349                    except Exception as exc:
 350                        consecutive_failures += 1
 351                        payload = _exception_payload(exc)
 352                        update_lock_metadata(
 353                            lock_handle,
 354                            last_heartbeat=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 355                            last_state="error",
 356                            last_error=payload["error"],
 357                            last_error_type=payload["error_type"],
 358                            consecutive_failures=consecutive_failures,
 359                            runtime=current_runtime_fingerprint(),
 360                        )
 361                        append_daemon_event(self.config, "daemon_error", **payload, consecutive_failures=consecutive_failures)
 362                        if not quiet:
 363                            print(
 364                                f"daemon_error type={payload['error_type']} error={payload['error'][:240]}",
 365                                flush=True,
 366                            )
 367                        _sleep_or_stop(_exception_backoff(exc, poll_seconds, consecutive_failures), max_iterations, iterations)
 368                        if max_iterations is not None and iterations >= max_iterations:
 369                            return
 370                        continue
 371
 372                    if result is None:
 373                        update_lock_metadata(
 374                            lock_handle,
 375                            last_heartbeat=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 376                            last_state="idle",
 377                            runtime=current_runtime_fingerprint(),
 378                        )
 379                        idle_sleep = self.idle_sleep_seconds(poll_seconds=poll_seconds)
 380                        if not quiet:
 381                            print(f"idle; sleeping {idle_sleep:g}s", flush=True)
 382                        _sleep_or_stop(idle_sleep, max_iterations, iterations)
 383                    else:
 384                        consecutive_failures = consecutive_failures + 1 if result.status == "failed" else 0
 385                        update_lock_metadata(
 386                            lock_handle,
 387                            last_heartbeat=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 388                            last_state="step",
 389                            last_job_id=result.job_id,
 390                            last_run_id=result.run_id,
 391                            last_step_id=result.step_id,
 392                            last_status=result.status,
 393                            last_tool=result.tool_name,
 394                            last_error="" if result.status != "failed" else str(result.result.get("error") or ""),
 395                            last_error_type="" if result.status != "failed" else str(result.result.get("error_type") or ""),
 396                            consecutive_failures=consecutive_failures,
 397                            runtime=current_runtime_fingerprint(),
 398                        )
 399                        detail = result.result.get("error") or result.result.get("artifact_id") or result.result.get("content", "")
 400                        append_daemon_event(
 401                            self.config,
 402                            "step",
 403                            job_id=result.job_id,
 404                            run_id=result.run_id,
 405                            step_id=result.step_id,
 406                            status=result.status,
 407                            tool=result.tool_name,
 408                            detail=str(detail)[:500],
 409                            consecutive_failures=consecutive_failures,
 410                        )
 411                        if not quiet:
 412                            print(
 413                                f"step job={result.job_id} run={result.run_id} step={result.step_id} "
 414                                f"status={result.status} tool={result.tool_name or '-'} detail={str(detail)[:240]}",
 415                                flush=True,
 416                            )
 417                            if verbose:
 418                                print(json.dumps(result.result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)[:8000], flush=True)
 419                        sleep_seconds = (
 420                            _step_failure_backoff(result, poll_seconds, consecutive_failures)
 421                            if result.status == "failed"
 422                            else max(0.0, poll_seconds)
 423                        )
 424                        _sleep_or_stop(sleep_seconds, max_iterations, iterations)
 425                    if max_iterations is not None and iterations >= max_iterations:
 426                        return
 427            except KeyboardInterrupt:
 428                interrupted = self.db.mark_interrupted_running(reason="daemon stopped during active work")
 429                update_lock_metadata(
 430                    lock_handle,
 431                    last_heartbeat=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 432                    last_state="stopped",
 433                    consecutive_failures=consecutive_failures,
 434                    runtime=current_runtime_fingerprint(),
 435                )
 436                append_daemon_event(self.config, "daemon_stopped", pid=os.getpid(), interrupted_steps=interrupted["steps"], interrupted_runs=interrupted["runs"])
 437                if not quiet:
 438                    print("nipux daemon stopped", flush=True)
 439            finally:
 440                signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, previous_sigterm)
 441
 442
 443def _is_digest_due(now: datetime, configured_time: str) -> bool:
 444    try:
 445        hour_text, minute_text = configured_time.split(":", 1)
 446        hour = int(hour_text)
 447        minute = int(minute_text)
 448    except ValueError:
 449        hour, minute = 8, 0
 450    return (now.hour, now.minute) >= (hour, minute)
 451
 452
 453def _raise_keyboard_interrupt(signum, frame) -> None:
 454    raise KeyboardInterrupt
 455
 456
 457def _exception_payload(exc: Exception) -> dict[str, str]:
 458    return {
 459        "error": str(exc),
 460        "error_type": type(exc).__name__,
 461    }
 462
 463
 464def _failure_backoff(poll_seconds: float, consecutive_failures: int) -> float:
 465    base = max(1.0, poll_seconds)
 466    return min(60.0, base * min(8, max(1, consecutive_failures)))
 467
 468
 469def _step_failure_backoff(result: Any, poll_seconds: float, consecutive_failures: int) -> float:
 470    """Return a retry delay for failed worker steps.
 471
 472    Worker LLM/provider failures are recorded as failed steps rather than
 473    escaping as daemon exceptions, so they need the same throttling path here.
 474    This keeps bad credentials, quota limits, and provider throttles from
 475    flooding the timeline while preserving the forever loop.
 476    """
 477
 478    fallback = _failure_backoff(poll_seconds, consecutive_failures)
 479    text = _step_failure_text(result)
 480    if _is_provider_config_text(text):
 481        return max(fallback, 300.0)
 482    if _is_rate_limit_text(text):
 483        return max(fallback, 60.0)
 484    return fallback
 485
 486
 487def _step_failure_text(result: Any) -> str:
 488    payload = getattr(result, "result", None)
 489    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
 490        return str(result)
 491    parts = [
 492        payload.get("error"),
 493        payload.get("error_type"),
 494        payload.get("detail"),
 495        payload.get("message"),
 496    ]
 497    return " ".join(str(part) for part in parts if part)
 498
 499
 500def _exception_backoff(exc: Exception, poll_seconds: float, consecutive_failures: int) -> float:
 501    fallback = _failure_backoff(poll_seconds, consecutive_failures)
 502    if not _is_rate_limit_error(exc):
 503        return fallback
 504    retry_after = _retry_after_seconds(exc)
 505    if retry_after is None:
 506        return max(fallback, 10.0)
 507    return max(fallback, min(300.0, retry_after))
 508
 509
 510def _is_rate_limit_error(exc: Exception) -> bool:
 511    status_code = getattr(exc, "status_code", None)
 512    if status_code == 429:
 513        return True
 514    return _is_rate_limit_text(f"{type(exc).__name__} {exc}")
 515
 516
 517def _is_rate_limit_text(text: str) -> bool:
 518    lowered = text.lower()
 519    return (
 520        "429" in lowered
 521        or "rate limit" in lowered
 522        or "ratelimit" in lowered
 523        or "too many requests" in lowered
 524        or "temporarily over capacity" in lowered
 525    )
 526
 527
 528def _is_provider_config_text(text: str) -> bool:
 529    lowered = text.lower()
 530    return (
 531        "401" in lowered
 532        or "403" in lowered
 533        or "authentication" in lowered
 534        or "permissiondenied" in lowered
 535        or "permission denied" in lowered
 536        or "invalid api key" in lowered
 537        or ("api key" in lowered and ("missing" in lowered or "invalid" in lowered))
 538        or "key limit exceeded" in lowered
 539        or "insufficient quota" in lowered
 540        or "quota exceeded" in lowered
 541        or "billing" in lowered
 542        or "credits" in lowered
 543    )
 544
 545
 546def _retry_after_seconds(exc: Exception) -> float | None:
 547    headers = _exception_headers(exc)
 548    for key, value in headers.items():
 549        normalized = key.lower()
 550        if normalized in {"retry-after", "x-ratelimit-reset", "x-rate-limit-reset"}:
 551            parsed = _parse_retry_after(value)
 552            if parsed is not None:
 553                return parsed
 554    return None
 555
 556
 557def _exception_headers(exc: Exception) -> dict[str, str]:
 558    response = getattr(exc, "response", None)
 559    headers = getattr(response, "headers", None)
 560    if headers:
 561        return {str(key): str(value) for key, value in dict(headers).items()}
 562    return {}
 563
 564
 565def _parse_retry_after(value: str) -> float | None:
 566    text = str(value).strip()
 567    if not text:
 568        return None
 569    with contextlib.suppress(ValueError):
 570        number = float(text)
 571        if number > 10_000_000_000:
 572            number = number / 1000
 573        if number > 1_000_000_000:
 574            return max(0.0, number - time.time())
 575        return max(0.0, number)
 576    with contextlib.suppress(ValueError, TypeError, OSError):
 577        parsed = parsedate_to_datetime(text)
 578        if parsed.tzinfo is None:
 579            parsed = parsed.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
 580        return max(0.0, parsed.timestamp() - time.time())
 581    return None
 582
 583
 584def _sleep_or_stop(seconds: float, max_iterations: int | None, iterations: int) -> None:
 585    if max_iterations is not None and iterations >= max_iterations:
 586        return
 587    time.sleep(seconds)
 588
 589
 590def _focused_job_id(config: AppConfig) -> str | None:
 591    path = config.runtime.home / "shell_state.json"
 592    if not path.exists():
 593        return None
 594    try:
 595        parsed = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 596    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
 597        return None
 598    job_id = parsed.get("focus_job_id") if isinstance(parsed, dict) else None
 599    return job_id if isinstance(job_id, str) and job_id else None
nipux_cli/dashboard.py 493 lines 
   1"""Operator-facing dashboard state and rendering."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5from collections import Counter
   6from datetime import datetime, timezone
   7from pathlib import Path
   8from textwrap import shorten
   9from typing import Any
  10
  11from nipux_cli.config import AppConfig
  12from nipux_cli.daemon import daemon_lock_status
  13from nipux_cli.db import AgentDB
  14from nipux_cli.operator_context import active_prompt_operator_entries
  15from nipux_cli.scheduling import job_deferred_until
  16from nipux_cli.tools import DEFAULT_REGISTRY
  17
  18
  19def collect_dashboard_state(
  20    db: AgentDB,
  21    config: AppConfig,
  22    *,
  23    job_id: str | None = None,
  24    limit: int = 12,
  25) -> dict[str, Any]:
  26    """Build a serializable snapshot for status and dashboard commands."""
  27
  28    jobs = db.list_jobs()
  29    selected = _select_focus_job(db, jobs, job_id)
  30    job_cards = [_job_card(db, job) for job in jobs]
  31    focus = _focus_state(db, selected, limit=limit) if selected else None
  32    return {
  33        "generated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
  34        "daemon": daemon_lock_status(config.runtime.home / "agentd.lock"),
  35        "runtime": {
  36            "home": str(config.runtime.home),
  37            "state_db": str(config.runtime.state_db_path),
  38            "logs_dir": str(config.runtime.logs_dir),
  39            "model": config.model.model,
  40            "base_url": config.model.base_url,
  41            "tool_count": len(DEFAULT_REGISTRY.names()),
  42        },
  43        "jobs": job_cards,
  44        "focus": focus,
  45    }
  46
  47
  48def render_dashboard(state: dict[str, Any], *, width: int = 120, chars: int = 260) -> str:
  49    """Render a compact terminal dashboard."""
  50
  51    width = max(72, min(width, 160))
  52    line = "-" * width
  53    runtime = state["runtime"]
  54    daemon = state["daemon"]
  55    focus = state.get("focus")
  56    generated_at = _compact_time(state["generated_at"])
  57    daemon_text = _daemon_text(daemon)
  58    lines = [
  59        "Nipux CLI Dashboard".ljust(width - len(generated_at)) + generated_at,
  60        line,
  61        f"daemon: {daemon_text}",
  62        f"model: {runtime['model']} | endpoint: {runtime['base_url']} | tools: {runtime['tool_count']}",
  63        f"home: {runtime['home']}",
  64        "trace: model-visible state, tool calls, outputs, artifacts, and errors. Hidden chain-of-thought is not exposed.",
  65        line,
  66        "Jobs",
  67    ]
  68    jobs = state.get("jobs") or []
  69    if not jobs:
  70        lines.append("  no jobs yet")
  71    else:
  72        lines.append("  title                         state      kind            steps  artifacts  last action")
  73        for job in jobs[:12]:
  74            latest = job.get("latest_step") or {}
  75            last_action = _one_line(latest.get("summary") or latest.get("error") or "-", 42)
  76            display_state = _job_state_text(job, bool(daemon.get("running")))
  77            lines.append(
  78                f"  {_one_line(job['title'], 29):<29} {display_state:<10} {job['kind']:<15} "
  79                f"{job['step_count']:>5} {job['artifact_count']:>10}  {last_action}"
  80            )
  81    if focus:
  82        lines.extend(_render_focus(focus, width=width, chars=chars, daemon_running=bool(daemon.get("running"))))
  83    return "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n"
  84
  85
  86def render_overview(state: dict[str, Any], *, width: int = 100) -> str:
  87    """Render a human-sized status view for the interactive shell."""
  88
  89    width = max(72, min(width, 120))
  90    runtime = state["runtime"]
  91    daemon = state["daemon"]
  92    focus = state.get("focus")
  93    jobs = state.get("jobs") or []
  94    latest_step = ((focus or {}).get("recent_steps") or [{}])[-1] if focus else {}
  95    lines = [
  96        "Nipux Status",
  97        "=" * min(width, 96),
  98        f"daemon: {_daemon_health_text(daemon, latest_step=latest_step)}",
  99        f"model: {runtime['model']}",
 100        f"jobs: {len(jobs)} total | tools: {runtime['tool_count']} | home: {runtime['home']}",
 101    ]
 102    if not focus:
 103        lines.append("focus: no job yet")
 104        lines.append("")
 105        lines.append("next: create \"your objective\" --title \"name\"")
 106        return "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n"
 107
 108    job = focus["job"]
 109    counts = focus["counts"]
 110    artifacts = focus.get("artifacts") or []
 111    metadata = job.get("metadata") if isinstance(job.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 112    operator = (metadata.get("last_operator_message") if isinstance(metadata, dict) else None) or {}
 113    agent_update = (metadata.get("last_agent_update") if isinstance(metadata, dict) else None) or {}
 114    lesson = (metadata.get("last_lesson") if isinstance(metadata, dict) else None) or {}
 115    findings = metadata.get("finding_ledger") if isinstance(metadata.get("finding_ledger"), list) else []
 116    sources = metadata.get("source_ledger") if isinstance(metadata.get("source_ledger"), list) else []
 117    tasks = metadata.get("task_queue") if isinstance(metadata.get("task_queue"), list) else []
 118    experiments = metadata.get("experiment_ledger") if isinstance(metadata.get("experiment_ledger"), list) else []
 119    active_operator = _active_operator_messages(metadata)
 120    pending_measurement = metadata.get("pending_measurement_obligation") if isinstance(metadata.get("pending_measurement_obligation"), dict) else {}
 121    lines.extend([
 122        "",
 123        f"focus: {job['title']}",
 124        (
 125            f"state: {_job_state_text(job, bool(daemon.get('running')))} | "
 126            f"worker: {_worker_text(job, bool(daemon.get('running')))} | kind: {job['kind']} | "
 127            f"steps: {counts['steps']} | artifacts: {counts['artifacts']} | failures: {counts['failed_steps']}"
 128        ),
 129        f"learning: findings={len(findings)} | sources={len(sources)} | tasks={len(tasks)} | experiments={len(experiments)} | lessons={counts.get('lessons', 0)} | reflections={counts.get('reflections', 0)}",
 130        f"objective: {_one_line(job['objective'], width - 11)}",
 131    ])
 132    if active_operator:
 133        lines.append(f"operator context: {len(active_operator)} active | {_one_line(active_operator[-1].get('message') or '', width - 28)}")
 134    if pending_measurement:
 135        lines.append(f"measurement: pending from step #{pending_measurement.get('source_step_no') or '?'}")
 136    if latest_step:
 137        tool = latest_step.get("tool_name") or latest_step.get("kind") or "-"
 138        status = latest_step.get("status") or "-"
 139        summary = latest_step.get("summary") or latest_step.get("error") or "-"
 140        lines.append(f"latest: #{latest_step.get('step_no')} {status} {tool}: {_one_line(summary, width - 22)}")
 141    if artifacts:
 142        artifact = artifacts[0]
 143        lines.append(f"latest artifact: {artifact.get('title') or artifact['id']}")
 144    if operator:
 145        lines.append(f"last steering: {_one_line(operator.get('message') or '', width - 15)}")
 146    if agent_update:
 147        lines.append(f"agent note: {_one_line(agent_update.get('message') or '', width - 12)}")
 148    if lesson:
 149        lines.append(f"latest lesson: {_one_line(lesson.get('lesson') or '', width - 16)}")
 150    lines.extend([
 151        "",
 152        "commands: activity | updates | findings | tasks | sources | memory | metrics | work --steps 3 | start | stop",
 153    ])
 154    return "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n"
 155
 156
 157def _select_focus_job(db: AgentDB, jobs: list[dict[str, Any]], job_id: str | None) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
 158    if job_id:
 159        return db.get_job(job_id)
 160    for status in ("running", "queued", "paused", "failed", "completed"):
 161        for job in jobs:
 162            if job.get("status") == status:
 163                return job
 164    return jobs[0] if jobs else None
 165
 166
 167def _job_card(db: AgentDB, job: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
 168    steps = db.list_steps(job_id=job["id"])
 169    artifacts = db.list_artifacts(job["id"], limit=500)
 170    runs = db.list_runs(job["id"], limit=500)
 171    return {
 172        "id": job["id"],
 173        "status": job["status"],
 174        "kind": job["kind"],
 175        "title": job["title"],
 176        "updated_at": job["updated_at"],
 177        "step_count": _step_count(steps),
 178        "run_count": len(runs),
 179        "failed_steps": sum(1 for step in steps if step.get("status") == "failed"),
 180        "artifact_count": len(artifacts),
 181        "latest_step": _public_step(steps[-1]) if steps else None,
 182    }
 183
 184
 185def _focus_state(db: AgentDB, job: dict[str, Any], *, limit: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
 186    steps = db.list_steps(job_id=job["id"])
 187    runs = db.list_runs(job["id"], limit=limit)
 188    artifacts = db.list_artifacts(job["id"], limit=limit)
 189    memory = db.list_memory(job["id"])
 190    metadata = job.get("metadata") if isinstance(job.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 191    lessons = metadata.get("lessons") if isinstance(metadata.get("lessons"), list) else []
 192    findings = metadata.get("finding_ledger") if isinstance(metadata.get("finding_ledger"), list) else []
 193    sources = metadata.get("source_ledger") if isinstance(metadata.get("source_ledger"), list) else []
 194    tasks = metadata.get("task_queue") if isinstance(metadata.get("task_queue"), list) else []
 195    experiments = metadata.get("experiment_ledger") if isinstance(metadata.get("experiment_ledger"), list) else []
 196    reflections = metadata.get("reflections") if isinstance(metadata.get("reflections"), list) else []
 197    active_operator = _active_operator_messages(metadata)
 198    tool_counts = Counter(step.get("tool_name") or step.get("kind") or "unknown" for step in steps)
 199    blocked = [step for step in steps if str(step.get("error") or "").endswith("blocked") or "blocked" in str(step.get("summary") or "")]
 200    return {
 201        "job": {
 202            "id": job["id"],
 203            "title": job["title"],
 204            "kind": job["kind"],
 205            "status": job["status"],
 206            "objective": job["objective"],
 207            "updated_at": job["updated_at"],
 208            "metadata": job.get("metadata") or {},
 209        },
 210        "counts": {
 211            "steps": _step_count(steps),
 212            "runs": len(db.list_runs(job["id"], limit=1000)),
 213            "artifacts": len(db.list_artifacts(job["id"], limit=1000)),
 214            "failed_steps": sum(1 for step in steps if step.get("status") == "failed"),
 215            "blocked_steps": len(blocked),
 216            "findings": len(findings),
 217            "sources": len(sources),
 218            "tasks": len(tasks),
 219            "experiments": len(experiments),
 220            "active_operator_messages": len(active_operator),
 221            "lessons": len(lessons),
 222            "reflections": len(reflections),
 223        },
 224        "tool_counts": dict(tool_counts.most_common(8)),
 225        "recent_runs": [_public_run(run) for run in runs],
 226        "recent_steps": [_public_step(step) for step in steps[-limit:]],
 227        "artifacts": [_public_artifact(artifact) for artifact in artifacts],
 228        "memory": [
 229            {
 230                "key": entry.get("key"),
 231                "summary": entry.get("summary"),
 232                "artifact_refs": entry.get("artifact_refs") or [],
 233                "updated_at": entry.get("updated_at"),
 234            }
 235            for entry in memory[:4]
 236        ],
 237        "lessons": [
 238            {
 239                "at": entry.get("at"),
 240                "category": entry.get("category") or "memory",
 241                "lesson": entry.get("lesson") or "",
 242                "confidence": entry.get("confidence"),
 243            }
 244            for entry in lessons[-5:]
 245            if isinstance(entry, dict)
 246        ],
 247        "findings": findings[-8:],
 248        "tasks": tasks[-12:],
 249        "experiments": experiments[-12:],
 250        "active_operator_messages": active_operator[-12:],
 251        "sources": sources[-8:],
 252        "reflections": reflections[-4:],
 253    }
 254
 255
 256def _render_focus(focus: dict[str, Any], *, width: int, chars: int, daemon_running: bool) -> list[str]:
 257    job = focus["job"]
 258    counts = focus["counts"]
 259    lines = [
 260        "-" * width,
 261        f"Focus Job: {job['title']} | state {_job_state_text(job, daemon_running)} | {job['kind']}",
 262        f"objective: {_one_line(job['objective'], width - 11)}",
 263        (
 264            f"counts: steps={counts['steps']} runs={counts['runs']} artifacts={counts['artifacts']} "
 265            f"failed_steps={counts['failed_steps']} blocked_steps={counts['blocked_steps']}"
 266        ),
 267        f"learning: findings={counts.get('findings', 0)} sources={counts.get('sources', 0)} tasks={counts.get('tasks', 0)} experiments={counts.get('experiments', 0)} lessons={counts.get('lessons', 0)} reflections={counts.get('reflections', 0)}",
 268        f"tool mix: {_tool_mix(focus.get('tool_counts') or {})}",
 269    ]
 270    active_operator = focus.get("active_operator_messages") or []
 271    if active_operator:
 272        lines.append(f"operator context: {len(active_operator)} active | {_one_line(active_operator[-1].get('message') or '', chars)}")
 273    pending_measurement = (job.get("metadata") or {}).get("pending_measurement_obligation") if isinstance(job.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 274    if isinstance(pending_measurement, dict) and pending_measurement:
 275        lines.append(f"measurement obligation: pending from step #{pending_measurement.get('source_step_no') or '?'}")
 276    lines.extend(["", "Recent Steps"])
 277    recent_steps = focus.get("recent_steps") or []
 278    if not recent_steps:
 279        lines.append("  no steps recorded")
 280    for step in recent_steps:
 281        error = f" | error={_one_line(step['error'], 70)}" if step.get("error") else ""
 282        lines.append(
 283            f"  #{step['step_no']:<4} {step['status']:<9} {step.get('tool_name') or step['kind']:<18} "
 284            f"{_one_line(step.get('summary') or '-', chars)}{error}"
 285        )
 286        args = step.get("arguments") or {}
 287        if args:
 288            lines.append(f"       args: {_one_line(_compact_value(args), chars)}")
 289    lines.append("")
 290    lines.append("Artifacts")
 291    artifacts = focus.get("artifacts") or []
 292    if not artifacts:
 293        lines.append("  no artifacts yet")
 294    for artifact in artifacts[:8]:
 295        title = artifact.get("title") or artifact["id"]
 296        lines.append(f"  {artifact['created_at']} {artifact['type']} {title}")
 297        if artifact.get("summary"):
 298            lines.append(f"       {_one_line(artifact['summary'], chars)}")
 299    lessons = focus.get("lessons") or []
 300    if lessons:
 301        lines.append("")
 302        lines.append("Lessons")
 303        for lesson in lessons:
 304            lines.append(f"  {lesson.get('category') or 'memory'}: {_one_line(lesson.get('lesson') or '', chars)}")
 305    findings = focus.get("findings") or []
 306    if findings:
 307        lines.append("")
 308        lines.append("Recent Findings")
 309        for finding in findings[-5:]:
 310            lines.append(f"  {_one_line(finding.get('name') or 'unknown', 48)} score={finding.get('score')} {finding.get('category') or ''}")
 311    tasks = focus.get("tasks") or []
 312    if tasks:
 313        lines.append("")
 314        lines.append("Task Queue")
 315        for task in tasks[-6:]:
 316            lines.append(f"  {task.get('status') or 'open':<7} p={task.get('priority') or 0:<3} {_one_line(task.get('title') or 'untitled', 56)}")
 317    sources = focus.get("sources") or []
 318    if sources:
 319        lines.append("")
 320        lines.append("Recent Sources")
 321        for source in sources[-5:]:
 322            lines.append(f"  {_one_line(source.get('source') or 'unknown', 48)} score={source.get('usefulness_score')} findings={source.get('yield_count') or 0}")
 323    memory = focus.get("memory") or []
 324    if memory:
 325        lines.append("")
 326        lines.append("Compact Memory")
 327        for entry in memory:
 328            refs = ", ".join(entry.get("artifact_refs") or [])
 329            suffix = f" refs={refs}" if refs else ""
 330            lines.append(f"  {entry['key']}: {_one_line(entry.get('summary') or '', chars)}{suffix}")
 331    return lines
 332
 333
 334def _public_run(run: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
 335    return {
 336        "id": run["id"],
 337        "status": run["status"],
 338        "started_at": run["started_at"],
 339        "ended_at": run.get("ended_at"),
 340        "model": run.get("model"),
 341        "error": run.get("error"),
 342    }
 343
 344
 345def _public_step(step: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
 346    input_data = step.get("input") if isinstance(step.get("input"), dict) else {}
 347    args = input_data.get("arguments") if isinstance(input_data.get("arguments"), dict) else {}
 348    return {
 349        "id": step["id"],
 350        "step_no": step["step_no"],
 351        "kind": step["kind"],
 352        "status": step["status"],
 353        "tool_name": step.get("tool_name"),
 354        "started_at": step["started_at"],
 355        "ended_at": step.get("ended_at"),
 356        "summary": _clean_step_summary(step.get("summary")),
 357        "error": step.get("error"),
 358        "arguments": args,
 359    }
 360
 361
 362def _public_artifact(artifact: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
 363    return {
 364        "id": artifact["id"],
 365        "created_at": artifact["created_at"],
 366        "type": artifact["type"],
 367        "title": artifact.get("title"),
 368        "summary": artifact.get("summary"),
 369        "path": artifact["path"],
 370    }
 371
 372
 373def _step_count(steps: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> int:
 374    numbers = [int(step.get("step_no") or 0) for step in steps]
 375    return max(numbers, default=0)
 376
 377
 378def _active_operator_messages(metadata: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
 379    messages = metadata.get("operator_messages") if isinstance(metadata.get("operator_messages"), list) else []
 380    prompt_entries = active_prompt_operator_entries(messages)
 381    return [
 382        entry for entry in messages
 383        if isinstance(entry, dict)
 384        and entry in prompt_entries
 385        and str(entry.get("mode") or "steer") in {"steer", "follow_up"}
 386    ]
 387
 388
 389def _daemon_text(daemon: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
 390    metadata = daemon.get("metadata") or {}
 391    if daemon.get("running"):
 392        pid = metadata.get("pid") or "unknown"
 393        started = metadata.get("started_at") or "unknown start"
 394        stale = " stale-runtime" if daemon.get("stale") else ""
 395        return f"running pid={pid}{stale} started={started}"
 396    return "stopped"
 397
 398
 399def _daemon_health_text(daemon: dict[str, Any], *, latest_step: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> str:
 400    if not daemon.get("running"):
 401        return "stopped (job will not advance until you run: start)"
 402    metadata = daemon.get("metadata") or {}
 403    heartbeat = metadata.get("last_heartbeat")
 404    status = "running"
 405    if daemon.get("stale"):
 406        status = "running stale-runtime"
 407    if heartbeat:
 408        age = _age_seconds(heartbeat)
 409        if age is not None:
 410            status += f" | heartbeat {int(age)}s ago"
 411            running_step = latest_step or {}
 412            if age > 120 and running_step.get("status") == "running":
 413                tool = running_step.get("tool_name") or running_step.get("kind") or "step"
 414                step_age = _age_seconds(running_step.get("started_at") or "")
 415                if step_age is not None:
 416                    status += f" | busy #{running_step.get('step_no')} {tool} for {int(step_age)}s"
 417                else:
 418                    status += f" | busy #{running_step.get('step_no')} {tool}"
 419            elif age > 120:
 420                status += " (stale)"
 421    failures = metadata.get("consecutive_failures")
 422    if failures:
 423        status += f" | consecutive failures: {failures}"
 424    tool = metadata.get("last_tool")
 425    step_status = metadata.get("last_status")
 426    if tool or step_status:
 427        status += f" | last: {step_status or '?'} {tool or '-'}"
 428    if metadata.get("last_error"):
 429        status += f" | error: {_one_line(metadata.get('last_error'), 48)}"
 430    return status
 431
 432
 433def _worker_text(job: dict[str, Any], daemon_running: bool) -> str:
 434    status = str(job.get("status") or "")
 435    if status in {"paused", "completed", "cancelled", "failed"}:
 436        return status
 437    if job_deferred_until(job):
 438        return "waiting"
 439    return "active" if daemon_running and status in {"running", "queued"} else "idle"
 440
 441
 442def _job_state_text(job: dict[str, Any], daemon_running: bool) -> str:
 443    status = str(job.get("status") or "")
 444    if status in {"running", "queued"}:
 445        if job_deferred_until(job):
 446            return "waiting"
 447        return "advancing" if daemon_running else "open"
 448    return status or "unknown"
 449
 450
 451def _age_seconds(value: str) -> float | None:
 452    try:
 453        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(value.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
 454    except ValueError:
 455        return None
 456    return max(0.0, (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - parsed.astimezone(timezone.utc)).total_seconds())
 457
 458
 459def _compact_time(value: str) -> str:
 460    try:
 461        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(value.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
 462    except ValueError:
 463        return value
 464    return parsed.astimezone().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
 465
 466
 467def _one_line(value: Any, width: int) -> str:
 468    text = " ".join(str(value).split())
 469    return shorten(text, width=max(8, width), placeholder="...")
 470
 471
 472def _clean_step_summary(summary: Any) -> str:
 473    text = " ".join(str(summary or "").split())
 474    if text.startswith("write_artifact saved ") and " at /" in text:
 475        return text.split(" at /", 1)[0]
 476    return text
 477
 478
 479def _compact_value(value: Any) -> str:
 480    if isinstance(value, dict):
 481        parts = [f"{key}={value[key]!r}" for key in sorted(value)]
 482        return ", ".join(parts)
 483    return str(value)
 484
 485
 486def _tool_mix(tool_counts: dict[str, int]) -> str:
 487    if not tool_counts:
 488        return "none"
 489    return ", ".join(f"{name}:{count}" for name, count in tool_counts.items())
 490
 491
 492def resolve_artifact_path(path: str | Path) -> str:
 493    return str(Path(path).expanduser())
nipux_cli/db.py 2403 lines 
   1"""SQLite state store for the Nipux agent."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import json
   6import random
   7import re
   8import sqlite3
   9import threading
  10import time
  11import uuid
  12from datetime import datetime, timezone
  13from pathlib import Path
  14from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, TypeVar
  15
  16from nipux_cli.metric_format import format_metric_value
  17
  18T = TypeVar("T")
  19
  20SCHEMA_VERSION = 1
  21
  22SCHEMA_SQL = """
  23CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
  24    version INTEGER NOT NULL
  25);
  26
  27CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS jobs (
  28    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  29    title TEXT NOT NULL,
  30    objective TEXT NOT NULL,
  31    kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'generic',
  32    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued',
  33    priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  34    cadence TEXT,
  35    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  36    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  37    metadata_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'
  38);
  39
  40CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS job_runs (
  41    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  42    job_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES jobs(id),
  43    status TEXT NOT NULL,
  44    started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  45    ended_at TEXT,
  46    model TEXT,
  47    config_hash TEXT,
  48    score REAL,
  49    error TEXT
  50);
  51
  52CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS steps (
  53    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  54    job_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES jobs(id),
  55    run_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES job_runs(id),
  56    step_no INTEGER NOT NULL,
  57    kind TEXT NOT NULL,
  58    status TEXT NOT NULL,
  59    tool_name TEXT,
  60    started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  61    ended_at TEXT,
  62    summary TEXT,
  63    input_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  64    output_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  65    error TEXT
  66);
  67
  68CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS artifacts (
  69    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  70    job_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES jobs(id),
  71    run_id TEXT,
  72    step_id TEXT,
  73    type TEXT NOT NULL,
  74    path TEXT NOT NULL,
  75    sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
  76    title TEXT,
