 260    return str(payload.get("id") or getattr(response, "id", "") or "")
nipux_cli/metric_format.py 17 lines 
   1"""Small formatting helpers for measured worker results."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5from typing import Any
   6
   7
   8def format_metric_value(name: Any, value: Any, unit: Any = "") -> str:
   9    """Return a readable metric string such as ``score=0.82`` or ``tokens=4200 tokens``."""
  10
  11    metric_name = str(name or "metric").strip() or "metric"
  12    metric_value = str(value).strip()
  13    metric_unit = str(unit or "").strip()
  14    if not metric_unit:
  15        return f"{metric_name}={metric_value}"
  16    separator = "" if metric_unit.startswith(("%", "/", "°")) else " "
  17    return f"{metric_name}={metric_value}{separator}{metric_unit}"
nipux_cli/operator_context.py 83 lines 
   1"""Generic filtering for operator messages that enter worker context."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import re
   6from typing import Any
   7
   8
   9CONVERSATION_ONLY_PATTERNS = [
  10    re.compile(r"(?i)^\s*(hi|hello|hey|yo|thanks|thank you|ok|okay|cool|nice|great|hello\?)\s*[.!?]*\s*$"),
  11    re.compile(r"(?i)^\s*(how('?s| is) it going|what('?s| is) going on|any updates?|status|jobs|ls|help|clear|exit|quit)\s*[.!?]*\s*$"),
  12    re.compile(r"(?i)^\s*(how('?s| is) it going)\??\s*(have you got|any)\s+(any\s+)?(improvements?|updates?|results?)\s*(yet)?\s*[.!?]*\s*$"),
  13    re.compile(r"(?i)^\s*(run|start|stop|pause|resume|cancel|work|status|jobs|clear|exit|quit|help)\s+\d+\s*$"),
  14]
  15
  16ACTIONABLE_PATTERNS = [
  17    re.compile(
  18        r"(?i)\b("
  19        r"avoid|because|benchmark|change|constraint|correct|do not|don't|dont|fix|focus|instead|instruction|"
  20        r"measure|must|need|never|only|prefer|priority|remember|should|target|use|wrong"
  21        r"|prioriti[sz]e)\b"
  22    ),
  23    re.compile(r"[\"'`][^\"'`]{2,}[\"'`]"),
  24]
  25
  26
  27def operator_entry_is_active(entry: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
  28    mode = str(entry.get("mode") or "steer").strip().lower().replace("-", "_")
  29    return (
  30        mode in {"steer", "follow_up"}
  31        and not entry.get("acknowledged_at")
  32        and not entry.get("superseded_at")
  33    )
  34
  35
  36def operator_entry_is_prompt_relevant(entry: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
  37    mode = str(entry.get("mode") or "steer").strip().lower().replace("-", "_")
  38    message = str(entry.get("message") or "").strip()
  39    if not message:
  40        return False
  41    if mode == "note":
  42        return not _conversation_only(message)
  43    if mode not in {"steer", "follow_up"}:
  44        return False
  45    if entry.get("acknowledged_at") or entry.get("superseded_at"):
  46        return False
  47    return _actionable(message)
  48
  49
  50def active_prompt_operator_entries(messages: list[Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
  51    return [
  52        entry
  53        for entry in messages
  54        if isinstance(entry, dict)
  55        and operator_entry_is_prompt_relevant(entry)
  56    ]
  57
  58
  59def inactive_prompt_operator_ids(messages: list[Any]) -> list[str]:
  60    ids: list[str] = []
  61    for entry in messages:
  62        if not isinstance(entry, dict):
  63            continue
  64        if not operator_entry_is_active(entry):
  65            continue
  66        if operator_entry_is_prompt_relevant(entry):
  67            continue
  68        event_id = str(entry.get("event_id") or "")
  69        if event_id:
  70            ids.append(event_id)
  71    return ids
  72
  73
  74def _conversation_only(message: str) -> bool:
  75    text = " ".join(message.split())
  76    return any(pattern.search(text) for pattern in CONVERSATION_ONLY_PATTERNS)
  77
  78
  79def _actionable(message: str) -> bool:
  80    text = " ".join(message.split())
  81    if _conversation_only(text):
  82        return False
  83    return any(pattern.search(text) for pattern in ACTIONABLE_PATTERNS)
nipux_cli/planning.py 358 lines 
   1"""Generic initial planning primitives for long-running jobs."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import re
   6from typing import Any
   7
   8
   9_PROFILE_TERMS: dict[str, set[str]] = {
  10    "measured": {
  11        "accelerate",
  12        "benchmark",
  13        "compare",
  14        "decrease",
  15        "faster",
  16        "improve",
  17        "increase",
  18        "latency",
  19        "measure",
  20        "metric",
  21        "optimize",
  22        "performance",
  23        "reduce",
  24        "score",
  25        "speed",
  26        "test",
  27        "throughput",
  28    },
  29    "deliverable": {
  30        "article",
  31        "deck",
  32        "doc",
  33        "document",
  34        "draft",
  35        "guide",
  36        "memo",
  37        "outline",
  38        "paper",
  39        "presentation",
  40        "report",
  41        "spec",
  42        "write",
  43    },
  44    "monitor": {
  45        "alert",
  46        "check",
  47        "observe",
  48        "periodic",
  49        "reporting",
  50        "track",
  51        "watch",
  52        "monitor",
  53    },
  54    "implementation": {
  55        "automate",
  56        "build",
  57        "change",
  58        "code",
  59        "debug",
  60        "deploy",
  61        "fix",
  62        "implement",
  63        "install",
  64        "repair",
  65        "run",
  66        "setup",
  67    },
  68    "research": {
  69        "analyze",
  70        "compare",
  71        "explore",
  72        "find",
  73        "investigate",
  74        "learn",
  75        "map",
  76        "research",
  77        "review",
  78        "summarize",
  79        "survey",
  80    },
  81}
  82_PROFILE_PRIORITY = {
  83    "measured": 0,
  84    "monitor": 1,
  85    "implementation": 2,
  86    "deliverable": 3,
  87    "research": 4,
  88}
  89
  90
  91def objective_profiles(objective: str) -> list[str]:
  92    """Infer generic work profiles from an objective without binding to a domain."""
  93
  94    tokens = set(re.findall(r"[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+", objective.lower()))
  95    scores: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
  96    for profile, terms in _PROFILE_TERMS.items():
  97        score = len(tokens & terms)
  98        if score:
  99            scores.append((score, profile))
 100    if not scores:
 101        return ["general"]
 102    scores.sort(key=lambda item: (-item[0], _PROFILE_PRIORITY.get(item[1], 99), item[1]))
 103    profiles = [profile for _score, profile in scores[:2]]
 104    return profiles or ["general"]
 105
 106
 107def initial_plan_for_objective(objective: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
 108    objective_text = " ".join(objective.split())
 109    profiles = objective_profiles(objective_text)
 110    tasks = _initial_tasks_for_profiles(profiles)
 111    questions = _initial_questions_for_profiles(profiles)
 112    return {
 113        "status": "needs_operator_review",
 114        "summary": _initial_summary_for_profiles(profiles),
 115        "profile": profiles[0],
 116        "profiles": profiles,
 117        "tasks": tasks,
 118        "questions": questions,
 119        "objective": objective_text,
 120    }
 121
 122
 123def initial_task_contract(task_title: str) -> dict[str, str]:
 124    lowered = task_title.lower()
 125    if any(word in lowered for word in ("baseline", "benchmark", "compare", "experiment", "measure", "metric", "test")):
 126        return {
 127            "output_contract": "experiment",
 128            "acceptance_criteria": "A baseline, result, comparison, or explicit blocked measurement is recorded.",
 129            "evidence_needed": "Experiment record with metric, environment or inputs, result direction, and next action.",
 130            "stall_behavior": "Record why measurement is blocked and create the smallest follow-up task that can obtain it.",
 131        }
 132    if any(word in lowered for word in ("draft", "deliverable", "document", "paper", "report", "write")):
 133        return {
 134            "output_contract": "report",
 135            "acceptance_criteria": "A durable draft, report, or deliverable section is saved with its evidence status.",
 136            "evidence_needed": "Saved output plus cited evidence, assumptions, gaps, or review notes.",
 137            "stall_behavior": "Save the partial output, record the gap, and create the next evidence or revision task.",
 138        }
 139    if any(word in lowered for word in ("validate", "review", "decide", "criteria", "constraint", "success")):
 140        return {
 141            "output_contract": "decision",
 142            "acceptance_criteria": "The decision, validation result, or success criteria are explicit.",
 143            "evidence_needed": "Operator context, durable notes, milestone validation, or task/roadmap updates.",
 144            "stall_behavior": "Ask for the missing constraint or record a reversible assumption and continue.",
 145        }
 146    if any(word in lowered for word in ("monitor", "watch", "track", "check", "observe")):
 147        return {
 148            "output_contract": "monitor",
 149            "acceptance_criteria": "A check cadence, signal, state change, or next observation time is recorded.",
 150            "evidence_needed": "Monitor result, status update, deferred follow-up, or recorded blocker.",
 151            "stall_behavior": "Defer the job until the next useful check or pivot to a diagnostic task.",
 152        }
 153    if any(word in lowered for word in ("act", "apply", "build", "change", "deploy", "fix", "implement", "install", "run")):
 154        return {
 155            "output_contract": "action",
 156            "acceptance_criteria": "The action produces an observable durable change or a clear blocker.",
 157            "evidence_needed": "Tool result plus file, artifact, ledger, task, roadmap, or experiment update.",
 158            "stall_behavior": "Record the blocker and open a smaller follow-up action.",
 159        }
 160    if "clarify" in lowered or "criteria" in lowered or "constraint" in lowered:
 161        return {
 162            "output_contract": "decision",
 163            "acceptance_criteria": "Success criteria, constraints, and first branches are explicit.",
 164            "evidence_needed": "Operator context, durable notes, or an updated roadmap/task queue.",
 165            "stall_behavior": "Ask for the missing constraint or record a decision with the best current assumption.",
 166        }
 167    if "map" in lowered or "research" in lowered or "branch" in lowered:
 168        return {
 169            "output_contract": "research",
 170            "acceptance_criteria": "At least one viable branch is selected and low-value branches are avoided.",
 171            "evidence_needed": "Source notes, branch rationale, source ledger entries, or saved research output.",
 172            "stall_behavior": "Record a low-yield lesson and pivot to a different branch.",
 173        }
 174    if "collect" in lowered or "evidence" in lowered or "save" in lowered or "output" in lowered:
 175        return {
 176            "output_contract": "artifact",
 177            "acceptance_criteria": "A durable output is saved and linked to the task or ledger.",
 178            "evidence_needed": "Artifact, file output, finding record, source record, or experiment record.",
 179            "stall_behavior": "Record what evidence is missing and create the next evidence-producing task.",
 180        }
 181    if "reflect" in lowered or "memory" in lowered or "continue" in lowered:
 182        return {
 183            "output_contract": "monitor",
 184            "acceptance_criteria": "Progress is evaluated from durable deltas and the next branch is chosen.",
 185            "evidence_needed": "Reflection, lesson, task update, roadmap validation, or experiment comparison.",
 186            "stall_behavior": "Record a blocker or pivot when no durable delta was produced.",
 187        }
 188    return {
 189        "output_contract": "action",
 190        "acceptance_criteria": "The task produces an observable durable change.",
 191        "evidence_needed": "Tool result plus artifact, ledger, task, roadmap, or experiment update.",
 192        "stall_behavior": "Record a blocker and open a smaller follow-up task.",
 193    }
 194
 195
 196def initial_roadmap_for_objective(*, title: str, objective: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
 197    profiles = objective_profiles(objective)
 198    execute_contract = _primary_execution_contract(profiles)
 199    return {
 200        "title": title,
 201        "status": "planned",
 202        "objective": objective,
 203        "scope": (
 204            "Initial roadmap generated from the objective and inferred generic work profile "
 205            f"({', '.join(profiles)}). Refine this as evidence and operator context arrive."
 206        ),
 207        "current_milestone": "Clarify and frame the work",
 208        "validation_contract": (
 209            "Each milestone needs observable evidence that its acceptance criteria were met, "
 210            "or a recorded blocker plus follow-up tasks."
 211        ),
 212        "milestones": [
 213            {
 214                "title": "Clarify and frame the work",
 215                "status": "planned",
 216                "priority": 10,
 217                "goal": "Turn the objective into concrete success criteria and constraints.",
 218                "acceptance_criteria": "Success criteria and first branches are explicit.",
 219                "evidence_needed": "Operator context, planning notes, or a recorded task queue.",
 220                "features": [{"title": "Capture success criteria", "status": "planned", "output_contract": "decision"}],
 221            },
 222            {
 223                "title": "Execute first durable branches",
 224                "status": "planned",
 225                "priority": 8,
 226                "goal": "Produce artifacts, findings, actions, or measurements that advance the objective.",
 227                "acceptance_criteria": "At least one branch produces durable evidence.",
 228                "evidence_needed": "Saved outputs, ledger updates, action results, or experiment records.",
 229                "features": [
 230                    {
 231                        "title": "Run the first evidence-producing branch",
 232                        "status": "planned",
 233                        "output_contract": execute_contract,
 234                    }
 235                ],
 236            },
 237            {
 238                "title": "Validate and continue",
 239                "status": "planned",
 240                "priority": 6,
 241                "goal": "Check results against acceptance criteria and create follow-up work.",
 242                "acceptance_criteria": "Validation is passed, failed, or blocked with a next action.",
 243                "evidence_needed": "record_milestone_validation entry and follow-up tasks if needed.",
 244                "features": [{"title": "Validate the checkpoint", "status": "planned", "output_contract": "decision"}],
 245            },
 246        ],
 247        "metadata": {"phase": "initial_plan"},
 248    }
 249
 250
 251def _initial_summary_for_profiles(profiles: list[str]) -> str:
 252    primary = profiles[0] if profiles else "general"
 253    if primary == "measured":
 254        return "I will start by defining the measurable baseline, then iterate on branches that can prove improvement."
 255    if primary == "deliverable":
 256        return "I will start by framing the deliverable, collecting evidence, and saving drafts that can be improved."
 257    if primary == "monitor":
 258        return "I will start by defining the watched signals, first check, cadence, and durable update format."
 259    if primary == "implementation":
 260        return "I will start by inspecting the current state, planning a small action, and validating the result."
 261    if primary == "research":
 262        return "I will start by mapping source branches, collecting evidence, and saving concise findings."
 263    return "I will turn this objective into a durable long-running job before starting tool work."
 264
 265
 266def _initial_tasks_for_profiles(profiles: list[str]) -> list[str]:
 267    tasks: list[str] = ["Clarify success criteria, constraints, and stop conditions."]
 268    primary = profiles[0] if profiles else "general"
 269    if primary == "measured":
 270        tasks.extend(
 271            [
 272                "Record the baseline metric and measurement method.",
 273                "Run the first measurable branch and record an experiment.",
 274                "Compare the result with the best known baseline and choose the next branch.",
 275            ]
 276        )
 277    elif primary == "deliverable":
 278        tasks.extend(
 279            [
 280                "Map the outline, audience, evidence gaps, and acceptance criteria.",
 281                "Collect evidence for the first section or deliverable unit.",
 282                "Save a durable draft or report checkpoint.",
 283            ]
 284        )
 285    elif primary == "monitor":
 286        tasks.extend(
 287            [
 288                "Define watched signals, check cadence, and alert conditions.",
 289                "Run the first status check and save the observation.",
 290                "Defer or continue based on the next useful check time.",
 291            ]
 292        )
 293    elif primary == "implementation":
 294        tasks.extend(
 295            [
 296                "Inspect current state and identify the smallest safe action.",
 297                "Apply one change or execute one action with observable output.",
 298                "Validate the result and record any follow-up branch.",
 299            ]
 300        )
 301    else:
 302        tasks.extend(
 303            [
 304                "Map the first research or execution branches.",
 305                "Collect evidence and save outputs as files.",
 306                "Reflect on what worked, update memory, and continue with the next branch.",
 307            ]
 308        )
 309    tasks.append("Publish a concise progress update and keep working on the next useful branch.")
 310    return tasks
 311
 312
 313def _initial_questions_for_profiles(profiles: list[str]) -> list[str]:
 314    questions = [
 315        "What result would make this job successful?",
 316        "Are there constraints, risks, or approaches I should avoid?",
 317    ]
 318    primary = profiles[0] if profiles else "general"
 319    if primary == "measured":
 320        questions.insert(1, "What metric should be treated as the primary measure of progress?")
 321    elif primary == "deliverable":
 322        questions.insert(1, "Who is the audience, and what quality bar should the deliverable meet?")
 323    elif primary == "monitor":
 324        questions.insert(1, "How often should I check, and what change should trigger a report?")
 325    elif primary == "implementation":
 326        questions.insert(1, "Which environment or files are in scope, and what requires approval?")
 327    else:
 328        questions.insert(1, "Which sources, artifacts, or signals should I trust most?")
 329    questions.append("Should this run aggressively in the background or wait for review between branches?")
 330    return questions
 331
 332
 333def _primary_execution_contract(profiles: list[str]) -> str:
 334    if "measured" in profiles:
 335        return "experiment"
 336    if "deliverable" in profiles:
 337        return "report"
 338    if "monitor" in profiles:
 339        return "monitor"
 340    if "implementation" in profiles:
 341        return "action"
 342    if "research" in profiles:
 343        return "research"
 344    return "artifact"
 345
 346
 347def format_initial_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
 348    tasks = plan.get("tasks") if isinstance(plan.get("tasks"), list) else []
 349    questions = plan.get("questions") if isinstance(plan.get("questions"), list) else []
 350    lines = [str(plan.get("summary") or "Initial plan created.")]
 351    if tasks:
 352        lines.append("Plan:")
 353        lines.extend(f"- {task}" for task in tasks)
 354    if questions:
 355        lines.append("Questions:")
 356        lines.extend(f"- {question}" for question in questions)
 357    lines.append("Reply with answers, or use the right-side Run control when this plan is good enough to start.")
 358    return "\n".join(lines)
nipux_cli/progress.py 130 lines 
   1"""Generic progress summaries for long-running jobs."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5from dataclasses import dataclass
   6from typing import Any
   7
   8
   9@dataclass(frozen=True)
  10class ProgressCheckpoint:
  11    message: str
  12    category: str
  13    counts: dict[str, int]
  14    deltas: dict[str, int]
  15    recent: str
  16
  17
  18LEDGER_KEYS = ("findings", "sources", "tasks", "experiments", "lessons", "milestones")
  19
  20
  21def build_progress_checkpoint(
  22    metadata: dict[str, Any],
  23    *,
  24    previous_counts: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
  25    step_no: int,
  26    tool_name: str | None,
  27    artifact_id: str = "",
  28    is_finding_output: bool = False,
  29) -> ProgressCheckpoint:
  30    """Create the operator-facing checkpoint text from durable ledger deltas."""
  31    counts = ledger_counts(metadata)
  32    previous = previous_counts or {}
  33    deltas = {key: counts[key] - _as_int(previous.get(key)) for key in LEDGER_KEYS}
  34    recent = recent_progress_bits(metadata)
  35    if is_finding_output:
  36        message = (
  37            f"Saved output {artifact_id}; ledgers now have {counts['findings']} findings, "
  38            f"{counts['sources']} sources, {counts['tasks']} tasks, and {counts['experiments']} experiments."
  39        )
  40        category = "finding"
  41    else:
  42        changed = ", ".join(f"+{value} {key}" for key, value in deltas.items() if value > 0)
  43        made_progress = bool(changed)
  44        if not changed:
  45            changed = "no new durable ledger entries"
  46        message = (
  47            f"Checkpoint step #{step_no}: {changed}. Totals: {counts['findings']} findings, "
  48            f"{counts['sources']} sources, {counts['tasks']} tasks, {counts['experiments']} experiments, "
  49            f"{counts['lessons']} lessons."
  50        )
  51        category = "progress" if made_progress else "activity"
  52    if recent:
  53        message = f"{message} Recent: {recent}."
  54    return ProgressCheckpoint(
  55        message=message,
  56        category=category,
  57        counts=counts,
  58        deltas=deltas,
  59        recent=recent,
  60    )
  61
  62
  63def ledger_counts(metadata: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, int]:
  64    roadmap = metadata.get("roadmap") if isinstance(metadata.get("roadmap"), dict) else {}
  65    milestones = roadmap.get("milestones") if isinstance(roadmap.get("milestones"), list) else []
  66    return {
  67        "findings": len(_metadata_list(metadata, "finding_ledger")),
  68        "sources": len(_metadata_list(metadata, "source_ledger")),
  69        "tasks": len(_metadata_list(metadata, "task_queue")),
  70        "experiments": len(_metadata_list(metadata, "experiment_ledger")),
  71        "lessons": len(_metadata_list(metadata, "lessons")),
  72        "milestones": len(milestones),
  73    }
  74
  75
  76def recent_progress_bits(metadata: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
  77    bits: list[str] = []
  78    findings = _metadata_list(metadata, "finding_ledger")
  79    if findings:
  80        finding = findings[-1]
  81        bits.append(f"finding={_clip_text(str(finding.get('name') or finding.get('title') or 'finding'), 80)}")
  82    active_tasks = [
  83        task
  84        for task in _metadata_list(metadata, "task_queue")
  85        if str(task.get("status") or "open").lower() in {"active", "open", "blocked"}
  86    ]
  87    if active_tasks:
  88        task = sorted(active_tasks, key=lambda entry: -_as_int(entry.get("priority")))[0]
  89        bits.append(f"task={_clip_text(str(task.get('title') or 'task'), 80)}")
  90    measured = [
  91        experiment
  92        for experiment in _metadata_list(metadata, "experiment_ledger")
  93        if experiment.get("metric_value") is not None
  94    ]
  95    if measured:
  96        experiment = measured[-1]
  97        metric = f"{experiment.get('metric_name') or 'metric'}={experiment.get('metric_value')}{experiment.get('metric_unit') or ''}"
  98        bits.append(f"measurement={_clip_text(metric, 80)}")
  99    roadmap = metadata.get("roadmap") if isinstance(metadata.get("roadmap"), dict) else {}
 100    milestones = roadmap.get("milestones") if isinstance(roadmap.get("milestones"), list) else []
 101    active_milestones = [
 102        milestone
 103        for milestone in milestones
 104        if isinstance(milestone, dict)
 105        and str(milestone.get("status") or "planned").lower() in {"active", "validating", "blocked"}
 106    ]
 107    if active_milestones:
 108        bits.append(f"milestone={_clip_text(str(active_milestones[-1].get('title') or 'milestone'), 80)}")
 109    return "; ".join(bits)
 110
 111
 112def _metadata_list(metadata: dict[str, Any], key: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
 113    value = metadata.get(key)
 114    if not isinstance(value, list):
 115        return []
 116    return [item for item in value if isinstance(item, dict)]
 117
 118
 119def _clip_text(value: str, limit: int) -> str:
 120    text = " ".join(value.split())
 121    if len(text) <= limit:
 122        return text
 123    return text[: max(0, limit - 1)].rstrip() + "..."
 124
 125
 126def _as_int(value: Any) -> int:
 127    try:
 128        return int(value)
 129    except (TypeError, ValueError):
 130        return 0
nipux_cli/scheduling.py 26 lines 
   1"""Shared scheduling helpers for deferred long-running work."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5from datetime import datetime, timezone
   6from typing import Any
   7
   8
   9def job_deferred_until(job: dict[str, Any], *, now: datetime | None = None) -> datetime | None:
  10    metadata = job.get("metadata") if isinstance(job.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
  11    raw_until = str(metadata.get("defer_until") or "").strip()
  12    if not raw_until:
  13        return None
  14    try:
  15        until = datetime.fromisoformat(raw_until.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
  16    except ValueError:
  17        return None
  18    if until.tzinfo is None:
  19        until = until.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
  20    until = until.astimezone(timezone.utc)
  21    now = (now or datetime.now(timezone.utc)).astimezone(timezone.utc)
  22    return until if until > now else None
  23
  24
  25def job_is_deferred(job: dict[str, Any], *, now: datetime | None = None) -> bool:
  26    return job_deferred_until(job, now=now) is not None
nipux_cli/settings.py 145 lines 
   1"""Inline config editing helpers for Nipux slash commands."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import os
   6from pathlib import Path
   7from typing import Any
   8
   9import yaml
  10
  11from nipux_cli.config import default_config_yaml, get_agent_home, load_config
  12from nipux_cli.tui_commands import SETTINGS_FIELD_TYPES
  13
  14
  15def config_field_value(field: str, config: Any | None = None) -> Any:
  16    config = load_config() if config is None else config
  17    values = {
  18        "model.name": config.model.model,
  19        "model.base_url": config.model.base_url,
  20        "model.api_key_env": config.model.api_key_env,
  21        "model.context_length": config.model.context_length,
  22        "model.request_timeout_seconds": config.model.request_timeout_seconds,
  23        "model.input_cost_per_million": config.model.input_cost_per_million,
  24        "model.output_cost_per_million": config.model.output_cost_per_million,
  25        "runtime.home": str(config.runtime.home),
  26        "runtime.max_step_seconds": config.runtime.max_step_seconds,
  27        "runtime.artifact_inline_char_limit": config.runtime.artifact_inline_char_limit,
  28        "runtime.daily_digest_enabled": config.runtime.daily_digest_enabled,
  29        "runtime.daily_digest_time": config.runtime.daily_digest_time,
  30    }
  31    return values.get(field, "")
  32
  33
  34def save_config_field(field: str, raw_value: str) -> Any:
  35    value = _coerce_config_value(field, raw_value)
  36    data = _load_config_yaml()
  37    section, key = field.split(".", 1)
  38    target = data.setdefault(section, {})
  39    if not isinstance(target, dict):
  40        target = {}
  41        data[section] = target
  42    target[key] = value
  43    _save_config_yaml(data)
  44    return value
  45
  46
  47def inline_setting_notice(field: str, raw_value: str) -> str:
  48    value = raw_value.strip()
  49    if not value:
  50        return f"kept {field}"
  51    if field == "secret:model.api_key":
  52        config = load_config()
  53        name = config.model.api_key_env
  54        _save_env_secret(name, value)
  55        return f"saved {name} in {_short_path(get_agent_home() / '.env')}"
  56    try:
  57        saved = save_config_field(field, value)
  58    except ValueError as exc:
  59        return f"{field}: {exc}"
  60    return f"saved {field} = {saved}"
  61
  62
  63def edit_target_label(field: str) -> str:
  64    if field == "secret:model.api_key":
  65        return "API key"
  66    return field
  67
  68
  69def edit_target_hint(field: str, config: Any | None = None) -> str:
  70    config = config or load_config()
  71    if field == "secret:model.api_key":
  72        state = "set" if config.model.api_key else "missing"
  73        return f"Editing API key ({state}). Enter saves, Esc cancels. Input is hidden."
  74    current = config_field_value(field, config)
  75    return f"Editing {field}. Current: {current}. Enter saves, Esc cancels, empty keeps current."
  76
  77
  78def edit_target_masks_input(field: str | None) -> bool:
  79    return field == "secret:model.api_key"
  80
  81
  82def _config_path() -> Path:
  83    return get_agent_home() / "config.yaml"
  84
  85
  86def _load_config_yaml() -> dict[str, Any]:
  87    path = _config_path()
  88    if not path.exists():
  89        loaded = yaml.safe_load(default_config_yaml()) or {}
  90        return loaded if isinstance(loaded, dict) else {}
  91    loaded = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) or {}
  92    return loaded if isinstance(loaded, dict) else {}
  93
  94
  95def _save_config_yaml(data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
  96    path = _config_path()
  97    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  98    path.write_text(yaml.safe_dump(data, sort_keys=False), encoding="utf-8")
  99
 100
 101def _save_env_secret(name: str, value: str) -> None:
 102    env_path = get_agent_home() / ".env"
 103    env_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 104    existing: dict[str, str] = {}
 105    if env_path.exists():
 106        for raw in env_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").splitlines():
 107            if "=" not in raw or raw.strip().startswith("#"):
 108                continue
 109            key, current = raw.split("=", 1)
 110            if key.strip():
 111                existing[key.strip()] = current.strip()
 112    existing[name] = value
 113    env_path.write_text("\n".join(f"{key}={current}" for key, current in existing.items()) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
 114    env_path.chmod(0o600)
 115    os.environ[name] = value
 116
 117
 118def _coerce_config_value(field: str, raw_value: str) -> Any:
 119    kind = SETTINGS_FIELD_TYPES.get(field, "str")
 120    value = raw_value.strip()
 121    if kind == "int":
 122        return int(value)
 123    if kind == "float":
 124        return float(value)
 125    if kind == "bool":
 126        lowered = value.lower()
 127        if lowered in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}:
 128            return True
 129        if lowered in {"0", "false", "no", "off"}:
 130            return False
 131        raise ValueError("use true or false")
 132    if kind == "path":
 133        return str(Path(value).expanduser())
 134    return value
 135
 136
 137def _short_path(path: Path | str, *, max_width: int = 80) -> str:
 138    text = str(path)
 139    home = str(Path.home())
 140    if text.startswith(home + os.sep):
 141        text = "~" + text[len(home) :]
 142    if len(text) <= max_width:
 143        return text
 144    keep = max(12, max_width - 4)
 145    return "..." + text[-keep:]
nipux_cli/settings_commands.py 47 lines 
   1"""Slash-command handlers for inline Nipux configuration."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import shlex
   6from contextlib import redirect_stdout
   7from io import StringIO
   8
   9from nipux_cli.config import load_config
  10from nipux_cli.settings import config_field_value, inline_setting_notice
  11from nipux_cli.tui_commands import CHAT_SETTING_COMMANDS
  12
  13
  14def handle_chat_setting_command(command: str, rest: list[str]) -> bool:
  15    if command in {"key", "api-key"}:
  16        if not rest:
  17            config = load_config()
  18            state = "set" if config.model.api_key else "missing"
  19            print(f"API key is {state} via {config.model.api_key_env}. Use /api-key KEY to save a new one.")
  20            return True
  21        print(inline_setting_notice("secret:model.api_key", " ".join(rest)))
  22        return True
  23    if command not in CHAT_SETTING_COMMANDS:
  24        return False
  25    field, placeholder = CHAT_SETTING_COMMANDS[command]
  26    if not rest:
  27        current = config_field_value(field)
  28        print(f"{field} = {current}")
  29        print(f"usage: /{command} {placeholder}")
  30        return True
  31    print(inline_setting_notice(field, " ".join(rest)))
  32    return True
  33
  34
  35def capture_setting_command(line: str) -> list[str]:
  36    try:
  37        parts = shlex.split(line[1:] if line.startswith("/") else line)
  38    except ValueError as exc:
  39        return [f"parse error: {exc}"]
  40    if not parts:
  41        return []
  42    stream = StringIO()
  43    with redirect_stdout(stream):
  44        if not handle_chat_setting_command(parts[0], parts[1:]):
  45            print(f"unknown config command: /{parts[0]}")
  46    lines = [" ".join(item.split()) for item in stream.getvalue().splitlines() if item.strip()]
  47    return lines[-12:] or ["done"]
nipux_cli/source_quality.py 32 lines 
   1"""Source quality checks for web and browser tools."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5ANTI_BOT_MARKERS = (
   6    "performing security verification",
   7    "cloudflare security challenge",
   8    "verifies you are not a bot",
   9    "verify you are not a bot",
  10    "enable javascript and cookies",
  11    "checking your browser before accessing",
  12    "just a moment...",
  13    "you have been blocked",
  14    "browsing and clicking at a speed much faster than expected",
  15    "there is a robot on the same network",
  16)
  17
  18
  19def anti_bot_reason(*parts: str) -> str | None:
  20    """Return a short reason if text looks like an anti-bot interstitial."""
  21
  22    text = " ".join(part for part in parts if part).lower()
  23    if not text:
  24        return None
  25    for marker in ANTI_BOT_MARKERS:
  26        if marker in text:
  27            if "cloudflare" in text:
  28                return "cloudflare anti-bot challenge"
  29            if "captcha" in text or "you have been blocked" in text:
  30                return "captcha/anti-bot block"
  31            return "anti-bot challenge"
  32    return None
nipux_cli/templates.py 67 lines 
   1"""Program templates for generic long-running jobs."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5
   6def program_for_job(*, kind: str, title: str, objective: str) -> str:
   7    kind = (kind or "generic").strip().lower()
   8    body = _TEMPLATES.get(kind, _generic_template)
   9    return body(title=title, objective=objective).strip() + "\n"
  10
  11
  12def _generic_template(*, title: str, objective: str) -> str:
  13    return f"""# {title}
  14
  15## Objective
  16
  17{objective}
  18
  19## Operating Rules
  20
  21- Work forever in bounded, resumable steps until the operator explicitly cancels or pauses the job.
  22- Treat useful results as checkpoints, not endings: save the result, create the next branch, and continue.
  23- Save important observations as artifacts.
  24- Use report_update for short progress notes or blocked-state notes.
  25- Use record_lesson when a source, mistake, operator preference, or strategy should affect future steps.
  26- Use record_source and record_findings when those tools are available so the job improves its ledgers over time.
  27- Use record_roadmap for broad work that needs milestones, feature groups, validation contracts, and roadmap-level checkpoints.
  28- Use record_milestone_validation to validate milestones from evidence and create follow-up tasks when validation fails or blocks.
  29- Use record_tasks to split broad objectives into durable branches with output contracts, acceptance criteria, required evidence, and stall behavior.
  30- Use record_experiment whenever a branch produces measured results, comparisons, benchmarks, scores, or optimization data.
  31- Use acknowledge_operator_context after incorporating or superseding active operator steering.
  32- Use browser and web tools first. Do not assume memory is exact unless it points to an artifact.
  33- Prefer quantity of attempts over one giant plan.
  34"""
  35
  36
  37def _research_paper_template(*, title: str, objective: str) -> str:
  38    return f"""# {title}
  39
  40## Objective
  41
  42{objective}
  43
  44## Research Rules
  45
  46- Save exact source URLs and extracted text snippets as artifacts.
  47- Keep a rolling citation map with claims, evidence, and open questions.
  48- Separate facts from hypotheses.
  49- Produce drafts only after evidence artifacts exist.
  50- Use report_update for brief progress, gap, or blocked-source notes.
  51- Use record_roadmap for the paper outline, evidence milestones, draft milestones, and validation checkpoints.
  52- Use record_milestone_validation when a section or draft milestone has enough evidence to judge.
  53- Use record_tasks to track source clusters, sections, and unresolved evidence gaps with output contracts and acceptance criteria.
  54- Use acknowledge_operator_context after incorporating or superseding active operator steering.
  55
  56## Step Loop
  57
  581. Search for one source cluster.
  592. Extract and save relevant evidence.
  603. Update the citation/evidence map.
  614. Write or improve one section when enough evidence exists.
  62"""
  63
  64
  65_TEMPLATES = {
  66    "research_paper": _research_paper_template,
  67}
nipux_cli/tools.py 1266 lines 
   1"""Static tool registry for the Nipux agent."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import json
   6import os
   7import re
   8import signal
   9import subprocess
  10import time
  11from dataclasses import dataclass
  12from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
  13from pathlib import Path
  14from typing import Any, Callable
  15
  16from nipux_cli.artifacts import ArtifactStore
  17from nipux_cli.config import AppConfig
  18from nipux_cli.db import AgentDB
  19from nipux_cli.metric_format import format_metric_value
  20from nipux_cli.digest import send_digest_email
  21
  22
  23@dataclass(frozen=True)
  24class ToolContext:
  25    config: AppConfig
  26    db: AgentDB
  27    artifacts: ArtifactStore
  28    job_id: str
  29    run_id: str | None = None
  30    step_id: str | None = None
  31    task_id: str | None = None
  32
  33
  34Handler = Callable[[dict[str, Any], ToolContext], str]
  35
  36EVIDENCE_OUTPUT_TERMS = {
  37    "audit",
  38    "checkpoint",
  39    "evidence",
  40    "extract",
  41    "extracted",
  42    "notes",
  43    "source",
  44    "sources",
  45}
  46DELIVERABLE_OUTPUT_TERMS = {
  47    "compiled",
  48    "deliverable",
  49    "draft",
  50    "final",
  51    "revision",
  52    "updated",
  53}
  54
  55
  56@dataclass(frozen=True)
  57class ToolSpec:
  58    name: str
  59    description: str
  60    parameters: dict[str, Any]
  61    handler: Handler
  62
  63    def as_openai_tool(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
  64        return {
  65            "type": "function",
  66            "function": {
  67                "name": self.name,
  68                "description": self.description,
  69                "parameters": self.parameters,
  70            },
  71        }
  72
  73
  74def _json(value: Any) -> str:
  75    return json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False)
  76
  77
  78def _write_artifact(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
  79    content = str(args.get("content") or "")
  80    if not content:
  81        return _json({"success": False, "error": "content is required"})
  82    stored = ctx.artifacts.write_text(
  83        job_id=ctx.job_id,
  84        run_id=ctx.run_id,
  85        step_id=ctx.step_id,
  86        content=content,
  87        title=args.get("title"),
  88        summary=args.get("summary"),
  89        artifact_type=str(args.get("type") or "text"),
  90        metadata=args.get("metadata") if isinstance(args.get("metadata"), dict) else None,
  91    )
  92    return _json({
  93        "success": True,
  94        "artifact_id": stored.id,
  95        "path": str(stored.path),
  96        "sha256": stored.sha256,
  97    })
  98
  99
 100def _read_artifact(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 101    artifact_ref = str(args.get("artifact_id") or args.get("path") or args.get("title") or args.get("ref") or "")
 102    if not artifact_ref:
 103        return _json({"success": False, "error": "artifact_id is required"})
 104    resolved = _resolve_artifact_ref(ctx, artifact_ref)
 105    if not resolved:
 106        return _json({"success": False, "error": f"artifact not found: {artifact_ref}"})
 107    try:
 108        content = ctx.artifacts.read_text(resolved["id"])
 109    except (OSError, KeyError, ValueError) as exc:
 110        return _json({"success": False, "artifact_id": resolved["id"], "error": str(exc)})
 111    return _json({"success": True, "artifact_id": resolved["id"], "title": resolved.get("title"), "path": resolved.get("path"), "content": content})
 112
 113
 114def _resolve_artifact_ref(ctx: ToolContext, artifact_ref: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
 115    ref = artifact_ref.strip().strip("'\"")
 116    if not ref:
 117        return None
 118    artifacts = ctx.db.list_artifacts(ctx.job_id, limit=250)
 119    for artifact in artifacts:
 120        if ref == artifact.get("id") or ref == str(artifact.get("path") or ""):
 121            return artifact
 122    if ref.isdigit():
 123        index = int(ref) - 1
 124        if 0 <= index < len(artifacts):
 125            return artifacts[index]
 126    lowered = ref.lower()
 127    for artifact in artifacts:
 128        title = str(artifact.get("title") or "").lower()
 129        if lowered == title:
 130            return artifact
 131    for artifact in artifacts:
 132        haystack = " ".join(str(artifact.get(key) or "") for key in ("title", "summary", "path")).lower()
 133        if lowered and lowered in haystack:
 134            return artifact
 135    return None
 136
 137
 138def _search_artifacts(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 139    query = str(args.get("query") or "")
 140    limit = int(args.get("limit") or 10)
 141    return _json({"success": True, "results": ctx.artifacts.search_text(job_id=ctx.job_id, query=query, limit=limit)})
 142
 143
 144def _write_file(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 145    raw_path = str(args.get("path") or "").strip()
 146    if not raw_path:
 147        return _json({"success": False, "error": "path is required"})
 148    if "content" not in args:
 149        return _json({"success": False, "error": "content is required"})
 150    mode = str(args.get("mode") or "overwrite").strip().lower()
 151    if mode not in {"overwrite", "append"}:
 152        return _json({"success": False, "error": f"invalid mode: {mode}"})
 153    path = Path(raw_path).expanduser()
 154    if not path.is_absolute():
 155        path = Path.cwd() / path
 156    if path.exists() and path.is_dir():
 157        return _json({"success": False, "error": f"path is a directory: {path}"})
 158    create_parents = bool(args.get("create_parents", True))
 159    if create_parents:
 160        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 161    content = str(args.get("content") or "")
 162    write_mode = "a" if mode == "append" else "w"
 163    with path.open(write_mode, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
 164        fh.write(content)
 165    return _json({
 166        "success": True,
 167        "path": str(path),
 168        "mode": mode,
 169        "bytes": path.stat().st_size,
 170    })
 171
 172
 173def _update_job_state(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 174    status = str(args.get("status") or "").strip().lower()
 175    if status in {"paused", "cancelled", "completed", "failed"}:
 176        note = str(args.get("note") or "")
 177        ctx.db.append_agent_update(
 178            ctx.job_id,
 179            f"Worker requested {status}; job remains running. {note}".strip(),
 180            category="progress" if status == "completed" else "blocked",
 181            metadata={"requested_status": status, "kept_running": True},
 182        )
 183        return _json({
 184            "success": True,
 185            "job_id": ctx.job_id,
 186            "status": "running",
 187            "requested_status": status,
 188            "kept_running": True,
 189            "guidance": (
 190                "Jobs are perpetual by default. Do not mark the job complete or failed. "
 191                "Save the current result, create follow-up tasks, report a checkpoint, and continue."
 192            ),
 193        })
 194    if status not in {"queued", "running"}:
 195        return _json({"success": False, "error": f"invalid status: {status}"})
 196    note = str(args.get("note") or "")
 197    patch = {"last_note": note} if note else None
 198    ctx.db.update_job_status(ctx.job_id, status, metadata_patch=patch)
 199    return _json({"success": True, "job_id": ctx.job_id, "status": status})
 200
 201
 202def _defer_job(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 203    until = _defer_until(args)
 204    reason = str(args.get("reason") or "").strip()
 205    next_action = str(args.get("next_action") or "").strip()
 206    patch = {
 207        "defer_until": until.isoformat(),
 208        "defer_reason": reason,
 209        "defer_next_action": next_action,
 210    }
 211    job = ctx.db.get_job(ctx.job_id)
 212    status = str(job.get("status") or "queued")
 213    if status not in {"queued", "running"}:
 214        status = "queued"
 215    ctx.db.update_job_status(ctx.job_id, status, metadata_patch=patch)
 216    message = f"Deferred until {until.isoformat()}"
 217    if reason:
 218        message += f": {reason}"
 219    if next_action:
 220        message += f" Next: {next_action}"
 221    ctx.db.append_agent_update(
 222        ctx.job_id,
 223        message,
 224        category="progress",
 225        metadata={"defer_until": until.isoformat(), "reason": reason, "next_action": next_action},
 226    )
 227    return _json({
 228        "success": True,
 229        "job_id": ctx.job_id,
 230        "status": status,
 231        "defer_until": until.isoformat(),
 232        "reason": reason,
 233        "next_action": next_action,
 234    })
 235
 236
 237def _defer_until(args: dict[str, Any]) -> datetime:
 238    raw_until = str(args.get("until") or "").strip()
 239    if raw_until:
 240        try:
 241            parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(raw_until.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
 242        except ValueError:
 243            parsed = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
 244        if parsed.tzinfo is None:
 245            parsed = parsed.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
 246        return parsed.astimezone(timezone.utc)
 247    seconds = args.get("seconds", args.get("delay_seconds", 300))
 248    try:
 249        delay = max(1.0, float(seconds))
 250    except (TypeError, ValueError):
 251        delay = 300.0
 252    return datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(seconds=delay)
 253
 254
 255def _report_update(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 256    message = str(args.get("message") or args.get("summary") or "").strip()
 257    if not message:
 258        return _json({"success": False, "error": "message is required"})
 259    category = str(args.get("category") or "progress").strip().lower()
 260    metadata = args.get("metadata") if isinstance(args.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 261    entry = ctx.db.append_agent_update(ctx.job_id, message, category=category, metadata=metadata)
 262    return _json({"success": True, "job_id": ctx.job_id, "update": entry})
 263
 264
 265def _record_lesson(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 266    lesson = str(args.get("lesson") or args.get("memory") or "").strip()
 267    if not lesson:
 268        return _json({"success": False, "error": "lesson is required"})
 269    category = str(args.get("category") or "memory").strip().lower()
 270    confidence_arg = args.get("confidence")
 271    confidence = float(confidence_arg) if isinstance(confidence_arg, (int, float)) else None
 272    metadata = args.get("metadata") if isinstance(args.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 273    entry = ctx.db.append_lesson(ctx.job_id, lesson, category=category, confidence=confidence, metadata=metadata)
 274    if _pending_measurement(ctx) and category in {"constraint", "mistake", "strategy", "memory"}:
 275        _resolve_measurement_obligation(
 276            ctx,
 277            status="explained",
 278            reason=lesson,
 279            via_tool="record_lesson",
 280        )
 281    return _json({"success": True, "job_id": ctx.job_id, "lesson": entry})
 282
 283
 284def _acknowledge_operator_context(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 285    raw_ids = args.get("message_ids")
 286    message_ids = [str(item) for item in raw_ids] if isinstance(raw_ids, list) else []
 287    summary = str(args.get("summary") or args.get("reason") or "").strip()
 288    status = str(args.get("status") or "acknowledged").strip().lower()
 289    result = ctx.db.acknowledge_operator_messages(
 290        ctx.job_id,
 291        message_ids=message_ids,
 292        summary=summary,
 293        status=status,
 294    )
 295    message = summary or f"Operator context {result.get('status')}."
 296    ctx.db.append_agent_update(
 297        ctx.job_id,
 298        message,
 299        category="progress",
 300        metadata={
 301            "operator_context_status": result.get("status"),
 302            "operator_message_count": result.get("count"),
 303            "operator_message_ids": [
 304                entry.get("event_id")
 305                for entry in result.get("messages", [])
 306                if isinstance(entry, dict) and entry.get("event_id")
 307            ],
 308        },
 309    )
 310    return _json({"success": True, "job_id": ctx.job_id, **result})
 311
 312
 313def _record_source(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 314    source = str(args.get("source") or args.get("url") or args.get("domain") or "").strip()
 315    if not source:
 316        return _json({"success": False, "error": "source is required"})
 317    warnings_raw = args.get("warnings")
 318    warnings = [str(item) for item in warnings_raw] if isinstance(warnings_raw, list) else []
 319    score_arg = args.get("usefulness_score")
 320    usefulness_score = float(score_arg) if isinstance(score_arg, (int, float)) else None
 321    yield_count = int(args.get("yield_count") or 0)
 322    fail_count_delta = int(args.get("fail_count_delta") or 0)
 323    metadata = args.get("metadata") if isinstance(args.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 324    entry = ctx.db.append_source_record(
 325        ctx.job_id,
 326        source,
 327        source_type=str(args.get("source_type") or ""),
 328        usefulness_score=usefulness_score,
 329        yield_count=yield_count,
 330        fail_count_delta=fail_count_delta,
 331        warnings=warnings,
 332        outcome=str(args.get("outcome") or ""),
 333        metadata=metadata,
 334    )
 335    return _json({"success": True, "job_id": ctx.job_id, "source": entry})
 336
 337
 338def _record_findings(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 339    raw_findings = args.get("findings")
 340    if isinstance(raw_findings, list):
 341        findings = [item for item in raw_findings if isinstance(item, dict)]
 342    else:
 343        findings = [args]
 344    if not findings:
 345        return _json({"success": False, "error": "findings are required"})
 346    evidence_artifact = str(args.get("evidence_artifact") or args.get("artifact_id") or "")
 347    stored = []
 348    added = 0
 349    updated = 0
 350    source_yields: dict[str, int] = {}
 351    for finding in findings[:50]:
 352        name = str(finding.get("name") or finding.get("title") or "").strip()
 353        if not name:
 354            continue
 355        source_url = str(finding.get("source_url") or finding.get("source") or args.get("source_url") or args.get("source") or "")
 356        score_arg = finding.get("score")
 357        score = float(score_arg) if isinstance(score_arg, (int, float)) else None
 358        entry = ctx.db.append_finding_record(
 359            ctx.job_id,
 360            name=name,
 361            url=str(finding.get("url") or ""),
 362            source_url=source_url,
 363            category=str(finding.get("category") or finding.get("type") or ""),
 364            location=str(finding.get("location") or ""),
 365            contact=str(finding.get("contact") or ""),
 366            reason=str(finding.get("reason") or finding.get("rationale") or ""),
 367            status=str(finding.get("status") or "new"),
 368            score=score,
 369            evidence_artifact=str(finding.get("evidence_artifact") or evidence_artifact),
 370            metadata=finding.get("metadata") if isinstance(finding.get("metadata"), dict) else {},
 371        )
 372        if entry.get("created"):
 373            added += 1
 374            if source_url:
 375                source_yields[source_url] = source_yields.get(source_url, 0) + 1
 376        else:
 377            updated += 1
 378        stored.append(entry)
 379    if not stored:
 380        return _json({"success": False, "error": "no valid finding with name/title was provided"})
 381    source_records = []
 382    for source_url, count in source_yields.items():
 383        score = round(min(1.0, 0.55 + min(count, 10) * 0.04), 2)
 384        source_records.append(
 385            ctx.db.append_source_record(
 386                ctx.job_id,
 387                source_url,
 388                source_type=str(args.get("source_type") or "finding_source"),
 389                usefulness_score=score,
 390                yield_count=count,
 391                outcome=f"record_findings yielded {count} new candidate(s)",
 392                metadata={"auto_from_record_findings": True, "evidence_artifact": evidence_artifact},
 393            )
 394        )
 395    ctx.db.append_agent_update(
 396        ctx.job_id,
 397        f"Finding ledger updated: {added} new, {updated} updated. Source ledger updated: {len(source_records)}.",
 398        category="finding",
 399        metadata={"added": added, "updated": updated, "sources_updated": len(source_records)},
 400    )
 401    return _json({
 402        "success": True,
 403        "job_id": ctx.job_id,
 404        "added": added,
 405        "updated": updated,
 406        "sources_updated": len(source_records),
 407        "sources": source_records,
 408        "findings": stored,
 409    })
 410
 411
 412def _record_tasks(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 413    raw_tasks = args.get("tasks")
 414    if isinstance(raw_tasks, list):
 415        tasks = [item for item in raw_tasks if isinstance(item, dict)]
 416    else:
 417        tasks = [args]
 418    if not tasks:
 419        return _json({"success": False, "error": "tasks are required"})
 420
 421    stored = []
 422    added = 0
 423    updated = 0
 424    for task in tasks[:50]:
 425        title = str(task.get("title") or task.get("name") or "").strip()
 426        if not title:
 427            continue
 428        status = str(task.get("status") or "open")
 429        output_contract = str(task.get("output_contract") or task.get("contract") or "")
 430        result_text = str(task.get("result") or task.get("outcome") or "")
 431        metadata = task.get("metadata") if isinstance(task.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 432        status, metadata = _validated_task_status(
 433            ctx,
 434            status=status,
 435            output_contract=output_contract,
 436            result=result_text,
 437            metadata=metadata,
 438        )
 439        priority_arg = task.get("priority")
 440        priority = int(priority_arg) if isinstance(priority_arg, (int, float)) else 0
 441        entry = ctx.db.append_task_record(
 442            ctx.job_id,
 443            title=title,
 444            status=status,
 445            priority=priority,
 446            goal=str(task.get("goal") or task.get("description") or ""),
 447            source_hint=str(task.get("source_hint") or task.get("source") or ""),
 448            result=result_text,
 449            parent=str(task.get("parent") or ""),
 450            output_contract=output_contract,
 451            acceptance_criteria=str(task.get("acceptance_criteria") or ""),
 452            evidence_needed=str(task.get("evidence_needed") or ""),
 453            stall_behavior=str(task.get("stall_behavior") or ""),
 454            metadata=metadata,
 455        )
 456        if entry.get("created"):
 457            added += 1
 458        else:
 459            updated += 1
 460        stored.append(entry)
 461    if not stored:
 462        return _json({"success": False, "error": "no valid task with title/name was provided"})
 463
 464    ctx.db.append_agent_update(
 465        ctx.job_id,
 466        f"Task queue updated: {added} new, {updated} updated.",
 467        category="plan",
 468        metadata={"added": added, "updated": updated},
 469    )
 470    if _pending_measurement(ctx):
 471        _resolve_measurement_obligation(
 472            ctx,
 473            status="deferred",
 474            reason="Created or updated task branch to obtain or handle the pending measurement.",
 475            via_tool="record_tasks",
 476        )
 477    return _json({"success": True, "job_id": ctx.job_id, "added": added, "updated": updated, "tasks": stored})
 478
 479
 480def _validated_task_status(
 481    ctx: ToolContext,
 482    *,
 483    status: str,
 484    output_contract: str,
 485    result: str,
 486    metadata: dict[str, Any],
 487) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
 488    normalized_status = status.strip().lower().replace(" ", "_") or "open"
 489    contract = output_contract.strip().lower().replace(" ", "_")
 490    if normalized_status != "done" or contract not in {"artifact", "report"}:
 491        return status, metadata
 492    if _recent_deliverable_evidence(ctx):
 493        return status, metadata
 494    updated = dict(metadata)
 495    updated["completion_validation"] = "missing_recent_deliverable_evidence"
 496    if result:
 497        updated["claimed_result"] = result
 498    return "active", updated
 499
 500
 501def _recent_deliverable_evidence(ctx: ToolContext, *, limit: int = 12) -> bool:
 502    for step in reversed(ctx.db.list_steps(job_id=ctx.job_id, limit=limit)):
 503        if step.get("id") == ctx.step_id:
 504            continue
 505        if step.get("status") != "completed":
 506            continue
 507        tool_name = str(step.get("tool_name") or "")
 508        input_data = step.get("input") if isinstance(step.get("input"), dict) else {}
 509        args = input_data.get("arguments") if isinstance(input_data.get("arguments"), dict) else {}
 510        if tool_name == "write_artifact" and _artifact_args_look_like_deliverable(args):
 511            return True
 512        if tool_name == "write_file":
 513            return True
 514        if tool_name == "shell_exec" and _shell_command_looks_like_write(str(args.get("command") or "")):
 515            return True
 516    return False
 517
 518
 519def _artifact_args_look_like_deliverable(args: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
 520    text = " ".join(str(args.get(key) or "") for key in ("title", "summary", "type")).lower()
 521    if not text:
 522        return False
 523    evidence_like = any(term in text for term in EVIDENCE_OUTPUT_TERMS)
 524    deliverable_like = any(term in text for term in DELIVERABLE_OUTPUT_TERMS)
 525    return deliverable_like and not evidence_like
 526
 527
 528def _shell_command_looks_like_write(command: str) -> bool:
 529    text = command.strip()
 530    if not text:
 531        return False
 532    write_patterns = [
 533        r"(?<!\d)>>?\s*[^&]",
 534        r"\b1>>?\s*[^&]",
 535        r"\btee\b",
 536        r"\bcat\s+>\b",
 537        r"\bpython[0-9.]*\b.*\bwrite_text\b",
 538        r"\bpython[0-9.]*\b.*\bopen\([^)]*,\s*['\"]w",
 539        r"\bsed\s+-i\b",
 540    ]
 541    return any(re.search(pattern, text, flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL) for pattern in write_patterns)
 542
 543
 544def _record_roadmap(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 545    title = str(args.get("title") or args.get("name") or "").strip()
 546    if not title:
 547        return _json({"success": False, "error": "title is required"})
 548    milestones_arg = args.get("milestones")
 549    milestones = [item for item in milestones_arg if isinstance(item, dict)] if isinstance(milestones_arg, list) else []
 550    roadmap = ctx.db.append_roadmap_record(
 551        ctx.job_id,
 552        title=title,
 553        status=str(args.get("status") or "planned"),
 554        objective=str(args.get("objective") or ""),
 555        scope=str(args.get("scope") or ""),
 556        current_milestone=str(args.get("current_milestone") or ""),
 557        validation_contract=str(args.get("validation_contract") or ""),
 558        milestones=milestones,
 559        metadata=args.get("metadata") if isinstance(args.get("metadata"), dict) else {},
 560    )
 561    ctx.db.append_agent_update(
 562        ctx.job_id,
 563        (
 564            f"Roadmap updated: {roadmap.get('status')} with "
 565            f"{len(roadmap.get('milestones') or [])} milestones."
 566        ),
 567        category="plan",
 568        metadata={
 569            "roadmap_title": roadmap.get("title"),
 570            "roadmap_status": roadmap.get("status"),
 571            "milestone_count": len(roadmap.get("milestones") or []),
 572            "current_milestone": roadmap.get("current_milestone"),
 573        },
 574    )
 575    return _json({"success": True, "job_id": ctx.job_id, "roadmap": roadmap})
 576
 577
 578def _record_milestone_validation(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 579    milestone = str(args.get("milestone") or args.get("milestone_title") or "").strip()
 580    if not milestone:
 581        return _json({"success": False, "error": "milestone is required"})
 582    raw_issues = args.get("issues")
 583    issues = [str(item) for item in raw_issues if str(item).strip()] if isinstance(raw_issues, list) else []
 584    validation = ctx.db.append_milestone_validation_record(
 585        ctx.job_id,
 586        milestone=milestone,
 587        validation_status=str(args.get("validation_status") or args.get("status") or "pending"),
 588        result=str(args.get("result") or args.get("summary") or ""),
 589        evidence=str(args.get("evidence") or args.get("evidence_artifact") or ""),
 590        issues=issues,
 591        next_action=str(args.get("next_action") or ""),
 592        metadata=args.get("metadata") if isinstance(args.get("metadata"), dict) else {},
 593    )
 594    follow_up_items = args.get("follow_up_tasks") if isinstance(args.get("follow_up_tasks"), list) else []
 595    follow_up_tasks = []
 596    for task in follow_up_items[:25]:
 597        if not isinstance(task, dict):
 598            continue
 599        title = str(task.get("title") or task.get("name") or "").strip()
 600        if not title:
 601            continue
 602        priority_arg = task.get("priority")
 603        priority = int(priority_arg) if isinstance(priority_arg, (int, float)) else 0
 604        follow_up_tasks.append(ctx.db.append_task_record(
 605            ctx.job_id,
 606            title=title,
 607            status=str(task.get("status") or "open"),
 608            priority=priority,
 609            goal=str(task.get("goal") or task.get("description") or ""),
 610            source_hint=str(task.get("source_hint") or task.get("source") or ""),
 611            result=str(task.get("result") or task.get("outcome") or ""),
 612            parent=str(task.get("parent") or milestone),
 613            output_contract=str(task.get("output_contract") or task.get("contract") or "action"),
 614            acceptance_criteria=str(task.get("acceptance_criteria") or ""),
 615            evidence_needed=str(task.get("evidence_needed") or ""),
 616            stall_behavior=str(task.get("stall_behavior") or ""),
 617            metadata=task.get("metadata") if isinstance(task.get("metadata"), dict) else {"source": "milestone_validation"},
 618        ))
 619    ctx.db.append_agent_update(
 620        ctx.job_id,
 621        (
 622            f"Milestone validation {validation.get('validation_status')}: "
 623            f"{validation.get('title') or milestone}; follow-up tasks {len(follow_up_tasks)}."
 624        ),
 625        category="plan",
 626        metadata={
 627            "milestone": validation.get("title") or milestone,
 628            "validation_status": validation.get("validation_status"),
 629            "follow_up_tasks": len(follow_up_tasks),
 630        },
 631    )
 632    return _json({
 633        "success": True,
 634        "job_id": ctx.job_id,
 635        "validation": validation,
 636        "follow_up_tasks": follow_up_tasks,
 637    })
 638
 639
 640def _record_experiment(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 641    title = str(args.get("title") or args.get("name") or "").strip()
 642    if not title:
 643        return _json({"success": False, "error": "title is required"})
 644    metric_value_arg = args.get("metric_value")
 645    metric_value = float(metric_value_arg) if isinstance(metric_value_arg, (int, float)) else None
 646    baseline_value_arg = args.get("baseline_value")
 647    baseline_value = float(baseline_value_arg) if isinstance(baseline_value_arg, (int, float)) else None
 648    record = ctx.db.append_experiment_record(
 649        ctx.job_id,
 650        title=title,
 651        hypothesis=str(args.get("hypothesis") or ""),
 652        status=str(args.get("status") or "planned"),
 653        metric_name=str(args.get("metric_name") or ""),
 654        metric_value=metric_value,
 655        metric_unit=str(args.get("metric_unit") or ""),
 656        higher_is_better=bool(args.get("higher_is_better", True)),
 657        baseline_value=baseline_value,
 658        config=args.get("config") if isinstance(args.get("config"), dict) else {},
 659        result=str(args.get("result") or args.get("outcome") or ""),
 660        evidence_artifact=str(args.get("evidence_artifact") or args.get("artifact_id") or ""),
 661        next_action=str(args.get("next_action") or ""),
 662        metadata=args.get("metadata") if isinstance(args.get("metadata"), dict) else {},
 663    )
 664    metric = ""
 665    if record.get("metric_value") is not None:
 666        metric = " " + format_metric_value(
 667            record.get("metric_name") or "metric",
 668            record.get("metric_value"),
 669            record.get("metric_unit") or "",
 670        )
 671    best = " best" if record.get("best_observed") else ""
 672    ctx.db.append_agent_update(
 673        ctx.job_id,
 674        f"Experiment {record.get('status')}: {record.get('title')}{metric}{best}.",
 675        category="progress",
 676        metadata={
 677            "experiment_key": record.get("key"),
 678            "metric_name": record.get("metric_name"),
 679            "metric_value": record.get("metric_value"),
 680            "best_observed": record.get("best_observed"),
 681            "delta_from_previous_best": record.get("delta_from_previous_best"),
 682        },
 683    )
 684    if record.get("metric_value") is not None or str(record.get("status") or "") in {"measured", "failed", "blocked", "skipped"}:
 685        _resolve_measurement_obligation(
 686            ctx,
 687            status="recorded",
 688            reason=f"Recorded experiment {record.get('title')}.",
 689            via_tool="record_experiment",
 690            experiment_key=str(record.get("key") or ""),
 691        )
 692    return _json({"success": True, "job_id": ctx.job_id, "experiment": record})
 693
 694
 695def _pending_measurement(ctx: ToolContext) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
 696    try:
 697        job = ctx.db.get_job(ctx.job_id)
 698    except KeyError:
 699        return None
 700    metadata = job.get("metadata") if isinstance(job.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 701    obligation = metadata.get("pending_measurement_obligation")
 702    if isinstance(obligation, dict) and not obligation.get("resolved_at"):
 703        return obligation
 704    return None
 705
 706
 707def _resolve_measurement_obligation(
 708    ctx: ToolContext,
 709    *,
 710    status: str,
 711    reason: str,
 712    via_tool: str,
 713    experiment_key: str = "",
 714) -> None:
 715    obligation = _pending_measurement(ctx)
 716    if not obligation:
 717        return
 718    resolved = dict(obligation)
 719    resolved.update({
 720        "resolved_at": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()),
 721        "resolution_status": status,
 722        "resolution_reason": reason[:1000],
 723        "resolution_tool": via_tool,
 724    })
 725    if experiment_key:
 726        resolved["experiment_key"] = experiment_key
 727    ctx.db.update_job_metadata(
 728        ctx.job_id,
 729        {
 730            "pending_measurement_obligation": {},
 731            "last_measurement_obligation": resolved,
 732        },
 733    )
 734    ctx.db.append_agent_update(
 735        ctx.job_id,
 736        f"Measurement obligation {status}: {reason[:220]}",
 737        category="progress" if status == "recorded" else "blocked",
 738        metadata={"measurement_obligation": resolved},
 739    )
 740
 741
 742def _send_digest_email(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 743    subject = str(args.get("subject") or "Agent digest")
 744    body = str(args.get("body") or "")
 745    if not body:
 746        return _json({"success": False, "error": "body is required"})
 747    result = send_digest_email(ctx.config.email, subject=subject, body=body, to_addr=args.get("to_addr"))
 748    stored = ctx.artifacts.write_text(
 749        job_id=ctx.job_id,
 750        run_id=ctx.run_id,
 751        step_id=ctx.step_id,
 752        content=body,
 753        title=subject,
 754        summary="Digest email body",
 755        artifact_type="digest",
 756        metadata={"email": result},
 757    )
 758    return _json({"success": True, "email": result, "artifact_id": stored.id, "path": str(stored.path)})
 759
 760
 761def _shell_exec(args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 762    command = str(args.get("command") or "").strip()
 763    if not command:
 764        return _json({"success": False, "error": "command is required"})
 765    cwd_raw = str(args.get("cwd") or "").strip()
 766    cwd = cwd_raw or None
 767    if cwd and not Path(cwd).expanduser().exists():
 768        return _json({"success": False, "error": f"cwd does not exist: {cwd}"})
 769    timeout_raw = args.get("timeout_seconds")
 770    timeout = float(timeout_raw) if isinstance(timeout_raw, (int, float)) else 60.0
 771    timeout = max(1.0, min(timeout, 900.0))
 772    max_chars_raw = args.get("max_output_chars")
 773    max_chars = int(max_chars_raw) if isinstance(max_chars_raw, (int, float)) else 12000
 774    max_chars = max(1000, min(max_chars, 50000))
 775    shell = "/bin/zsh" if Path("/bin/zsh").exists() else None
 776    env = dict(os.environ)
 777    env["NIPUX_JOB_ID"] = ctx.job_id
 778    if ctx.run_id:
 779        env["NIPUX_RUN_ID"] = ctx.run_id
 780    started = time.monotonic()
 781    try:
 782        process = subprocess.Popen(
 783            command,
 784            shell=True,
 785            executable=shell,
 786            cwd=str(Path(cwd).expanduser()) if cwd else None,
 787            env=env,
 788            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
 789            stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
 790            text=True,
 791            start_new_session=True,
 792        )
 793        stdout, stderr = process.communicate(timeout=timeout)
 794    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
 795        _terminate_process_group(process)
 796        try:
 797            stdout, stderr = process.communicate(timeout=2)
 798        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
 799            _kill_process_group(process)
 800            stdout, stderr = process.communicate()
 801        return _json({
 802            "success": False,
 803            "error": f"command timed out after {timeout:.1f}s",
 804            "timed_out": True,
 805            "command": command,
 806            "cwd": cwd or os.getcwd(),
 807            "timeout_seconds": timeout,
 808            "duration_seconds": round(time.monotonic() - started, 3),
 809            "returncode": None,
 810            "stdout": _truncate_output(stdout, max_chars),
 811            "stderr": _truncate_output(stderr, max_chars),
 812        })
 813    error = _shell_error(process.returncode, stdout, stderr)
 814    return _json({
 815        "success": process.returncode == 0,
 816        "error": error,
 817        "command": command,
 818        "cwd": cwd or os.getcwd(),
 819        "duration_seconds": round(time.monotonic() - started, 3),
 820        "returncode": process.returncode,
 821        "stdout": _truncate_output(stdout, max_chars),
 822        "stderr": _truncate_output(stderr, max_chars),
 823    })
 824
 825
 826def _shell_error(returncode: int | None, stdout: str, stderr: str) -> str:
 827    if returncode == 0:
 828        return ""
 829    combined = "\n".join(part.strip() for part in (stderr, stdout) if part and part.strip())
 830    lowered = combined.lower()
 831    if "sudo:" in lowered and ("password" in lowered or "terminal is required" in lowered):
 832        return "command requires interactive sudo/password; configure non-interactive privileges or choose a non-sudo path"
 833    if "permission denied" in lowered:
 834        return "command failed with permission denied"
 835    excerpt = " ".join(combined.split())[:500] if combined else "no output"
 836    return f"command exited with status {returncode}: {excerpt}"
 837
 838
 839def _terminate_process_group(process: subprocess.Popen[str]) -> None:
 840    try:
 841        os.killpg(process.pid, signal.SIGTERM)
 842    except ProcessLookupError:
 843        return
 844
 845
 846def _kill_process_group(process: subprocess.Popen[str]) -> None:
 847    try:
 848        os.killpg(process.pid, signal.SIGKILL)
 849    except ProcessLookupError:
 850        return
 851
 852
 853def _truncate_output(value: Any, max_chars: int) -> str:
 854    text = value.decode("utf-8", errors="replace") if isinstance(value, bytes) else str(value or "")
 855    if len(text) <= max_chars:
 856        return text
 857    omitted = len(text) - max_chars
 858    return text[:max_chars] + f"\n... truncated {omitted} chars ..."
 859
 860
 861def _browser_call(name: str, args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 862    from nipux_cli import browser
 863
 864    task_id = ctx.task_id or ctx.job_id
 865    if name == "browser_navigate":
 866        return _json(browser.navigate(ctx.config, task_id=task_id, url=str(args.get("url") or "")))
 867    if name == "browser_snapshot":
 868        return _json(browser.snapshot(ctx.config, task_id=task_id, full=bool(args.get("full", False))))
 869    if name == "browser_click":
 870        return _json(browser.click(ctx.config, task_id=task_id, ref=str(args.get("ref") or "")))
 871    if name == "browser_type":
 872        return _json(browser.fill(ctx.config, task_id=task_id, ref=str(args.get("ref") or ""), text=str(args.get("text") or "")))
 873    if name == "browser_scroll":
 874        return _json(browser.scroll(ctx.config, task_id=task_id, direction=str(args.get("direction") or "down")))
 875    if name == "browser_back":
 876        return _json(browser.back(ctx.config, task_id=task_id))
 877    if name == "browser_press":
 878        return _json(browser.press(ctx.config, task_id=task_id, key=str(args.get("key") or "")))
 879    if name == "browser_console":
 880        return _json(browser.console(ctx.config, task_id=task_id, clear=bool(args.get("clear", False)), expression=args.get("expression")))
 881    raise KeyError(name)
 882
 883
 884def _web_call(name: str, args: dict[str, Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> str:
 885    del ctx
 886    from nipux_cli.web import web_extract, web_search
 887
 888    if name == "web_search":
 889        return _json(web_search(str(args.get("query") or ""), limit=int(args.get("limit") or 5)))
 890    if name == "web_extract":
 891        urls = args.get("urls") if isinstance(args.get("urls"), list) else []
 892        return _json(web_extract(urls[:5]))
 893    raise KeyError(name)
 894
 895
 896def _browser_handler(name: str) -> Handler:
 897    return lambda args, ctx: _browser_call(name, args, ctx)
 898
 899
 900def _web_handler(name: str) -> Handler:
 901    return lambda args, ctx: _web_call(name, args, ctx)
 902
 903
 904BROWSER_SCHEMAS: list[ToolSpec] = [
 905    ToolSpec("browser_navigate", "Navigate to a URL and return a compact browser snapshot.", {
 906        "type": "object",
 907        "properties": {"url": {"type": "string"}},
 908        "required": ["url"],
 909    }, _browser_handler("browser_navigate")),
 910    ToolSpec("browser_snapshot", "Refresh the current page accessibility snapshot.", {
 911        "type": "object",
 912        "properties": {"full": {"type": "boolean", "default": False}},
 913        "required": [],
 914    }, _browser_handler("browser_snapshot")),
 915    ToolSpec("browser_click", "Click an element by snapshot ref, for example @e5.", {
 916        "type": "object",
 917        "properties": {"ref": {"type": "string"}},
 918        "required": ["ref"],
 919    }, _browser_handler("browser_click")),
 920    ToolSpec("browser_type", "Fill an input by snapshot ref.", {
 921        "type": "object",
 922        "properties": {"ref": {"type": "string"}, "text": {"type": "string"}},
 923        "required": ["ref", "text"],
