 100def chat_event_parts(event: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, str, str] | None:
 101    kind = str(event.get("event_type") or "")
 102    title = str(event.get("title") or "").strip()
 103    body = str(event.get("body") or "")
 104    clock = event_clock(event)
 105    if kind == "operator_message":
 106        return "YOU", body, clock
 107    if kind == "agent_message" and title == "chat":
 108        return "AGENT", body, clock
 109    return None
 110
 111
 112def append_chat_output(lines: list[str], label: str, body: Any, *, clock: str, width: int) -> None:
 113    label_text = _fit_ansi(_event_badge(label), 8)
 114    clock_text = _fit_ansi(_muted(clock), 5) if clock else " " * 5
 115    prefix = f"{clock_text} {label_text} "
 116    prefix_width = 15
 117    available = max(18, width - prefix_width)
 118    first = True
 119    for paragraph in _chat_message_paragraphs(body):
 120        wrapped = textwrap.wrap(paragraph, width=available) or [""]
 121        for part in wrapped:
 122            if first:
 123                lines.append(_fit_ansi(prefix + part, width))
 124                first = False
 125            else:
 126                lines.append(_fit_ansi(" " * prefix_width + part, width))
 127    if first:
 128        lines.append(_fit_ansi(prefix, width))
 129
 130
 131def chat_pane_lines(events: list[dict[str, Any]], notices: list[str], *, width: int, rows: int) -> list[str]:
 132    items: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = []
 133    for event in events:
 134        rendered = chat_event_parts(event)
 135        if not rendered:
 136            continue
 137        label, body, clock = rendered
 138        items.append((label, body, clock))
 139    for notice in notices:
 140        if notice.startswith("> "):
 141            items.append(("YOU", notice[2:], ""))
 142        else:
 143            items.append(("NIPUX", notice, ""))
 144    if not items:
 145        return chat_empty_state_lines(width=width, rows=rows)
 146    output_rows: list[str] = []
 147    for label, body, clock in items[-max(4, rows) :]:
 148        append_chat_output(output_rows, label, body, clock=clock, width=width)
 149    if len(output_rows) <= rows:
 150        return output_rows
 151    if rows <= 1:
 152        return output_rows[-rows:]
 153    hidden = len(output_rows) - rows + 1
 154    marker = _fit_ansi(_muted(f"... {hidden} chat lines hidden; use /history for full text."), width)
 155    return [marker, *output_rows[-(rows - 1) :]]
 156
 157
 158def chat_empty_state_lines(*, width: int, rows: int) -> list[str]:
 159    if width < 48:
 160        content = [
 161            _center_ansi(_bold(_accent("NIPUX")), width),
 162            "",
 163            _center_ansi(_muted("Type normally to talk."), width),
 164        ]
 165        return content[:rows]
 166    content = [
 167        *[_center_ansi(_style(line, "37;1"), width) for line in NIPUX_HERO],
 168        "",
 169        _center_ansi(_muted("A persistent agent workspace."), width),
 170        _center_ansi(_muted("Enter sends  ·  / opens commands  ·  arrows switch panels"), width),
 171    ]
 172    top_pad = max(0, (rows - len(content)) // 2 - 1)
 173    return ([""] * top_pad + content)[:rows]
 174
 175
 176def worker_activity_lines(events: list[dict[str, Any]], *, width: int, limit: int) -> list[str]:
 177    items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 178    for event in events:
 179        line = minimal_live_event_line(event, chars=max(16, width - 12))
 180        if not line:
 181            continue
 182        if items and items[-1].get("key") == line:
 183            items[-1]["count"] = int(items[-1].get("count") or 1) + 1
 184            continue
 185        items.append({"line": line, "count": 1, "key": line})
 186    rendered = []
 187    for item in items[-limit:]:
 188        line = str(item["line"])
 189        count = int(item.get("count") or 1)
 190        rendered.append(f"{live_badge(line)} {_one_line(live_display_text(line, count=count), max(16, width - 9))}")
 191    return rendered
 192
 193
 194def model_update_event_parts(event: dict[str, Any], *, width: int, compact: bool = True) -> tuple[str, str, str] | None:
 195    kind = str(event.get("event_type") or "")
 196    title = generic_display_text(event.get("title") or "")
 197    body = generic_display_text(event.get("body") or "")
 198    metadata = event.get("metadata") if isinstance(event.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 199    status = str(metadata.get("status") or "")
 200    clock = event_clock(event)
 201    chars = max(24, width - 16)
 202    if kind == "artifact":
 203        detail = event_title_body(title, body or str(metadata.get("summary") or ""), fallback="saved output")
 204        return "SAVE", _outcome_text(detail, chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 205    if kind == "finding":
 206        return "FIND", _outcome_text(event_title_body(title, body, fallback="finding"), chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 207    if kind == "source":
 208        return "SOURCE", _outcome_text(event_title_body(title, body, fallback="source"), chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 209    if kind == "experiment":
 210        metric = experiment_metric_text(metadata)
 211        detail = event_title_body(title, body, fallback="measurement")
 212        if metric and metric not in detail:
 213            detail = f"{detail} - {metric}"
 214        return "TEST", _outcome_text(detail, chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 215    if kind == "task":
 216        task_status = str(metadata.get("status") or "")
 217        detail = event_title_body(title, body, fallback="task")
 218        prefix = f"{task_status} " if task_status else ""
 219        return "TASK", _outcome_text(prefix + detail, chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 220    if kind == "roadmap":
 221        return "ROAD", _outcome_text(event_title_body(title, body, fallback="roadmap"), chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 222    if kind == "milestone_validation":
 223        validation = str(metadata.get("validation_status") or metadata.get("status") or "")
 224        detail = event_title_body(title, body, fallback="milestone")
 225        return "VALID", _outcome_text(f"{validation} {detail}".strip(), chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 226    if kind == "lesson":
 227        return "LEARN", _outcome_text(event_title_body(title, body, fallback="lesson"), chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 228    if kind == "reflection":
 229        return "PLAN", _outcome_text(brief_reflection_text(body or title), chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 230    if kind == "agent_message" and title.lower() in {"error", "blocked"}:
 231        detail = _chat_agent_message_text(title, body) or event_title_body(title, body, fallback="error")
 232        return "FAIL", _outcome_text(detail, chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 233    if kind == "agent_message" and title.lower() in {"progress", "update", "report", "plan", "planning"}:
 234        detail = _chat_agent_message_text(title, body) or event_title_body(title, body, fallback="update")
 235        return "UPDATE", _outcome_text(detail, chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 236    if kind == "tool_result" and status == "completed":
 237        tool = title
 238        if tool in {"web_search", "web_extract"}:
 239            return "DONE", _outcome_text(tool_live_summary(tool, metadata, body), chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 240        if tool == "shell_exec":
 241            command = str(event_tool_args(metadata).get("command") or "")
 242            target = shell_write_target(command)
 243            if target:
 244                return "FILE", _outcome_text(f"updated {_short_path(target, max_width=chars - 8)} via shell", chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 245        if tool == "write_file":
 246            output = metadata.get("output") if isinstance(metadata.get("output"), dict) else {}
 247            path = str(output.get("path") or event_tool_args(metadata).get("path") or "")
 248            return "FILE", _outcome_text(f"updated {_short_path(path, max_width=chars - 8)}", chars=chars, compact=compact), clock
 249    return None
 250
 251
 252def latest_durable_outcome_line(events: list[dict[str, Any]], *, width: int) -> str:
 253    fallback: tuple[str, str, str] | None = None
 254    for event in reversed(events):
 255        parsed = model_update_event_parts(event, width=width)
 256        if not parsed:
 257            continue
 258        label, text, _clock = parsed
 259        if label == "DONE":
 260            fallback = fallback or parsed
 261            continue
 262        prefix = f"{_muted('Outcome')} {_event_badge(label)} "
 263        return _fit_ansi(prefix + _one_line(text, max(12, width - len(_strip_ansi(prefix)))), width)
 264    if fallback:
 265        label, text, _clock = fallback
 266        prefix = f"{_muted('Outcome')} {_event_badge(label)} "
 267        return _fit_ansi(prefix + _one_line(text, max(12, width - len(_strip_ansi(prefix)))), width)
 268    return ""
 269
 270
 271def recent_model_update_lines(
 272    events: list[dict[str, Any]],
 273    *,
 274    width: int,
 275    limit: int,
 276    include_research: bool = False,
 277) -> list[str]:
 278    """Render recent durable worker outcomes for the compact status pane."""
 279    if limit <= 0:
 280        return []
 281    lines: list[str] = []
 282    seen: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
 283    for event in reversed(events):
 284        parsed = model_update_event_parts(event, width=max(width, 180))
 285        if not parsed:
 286            continue
 287        label, text, clock = parsed
 288        if label == "DONE" and not include_research:
 289            continue
 290        key = (label, text)
 291        if key in seen:
 292            continue
 293        seen.add(key)
 294        prefix = f"{_muted(clock)} {_event_badge(label)} " if clock else f"{_event_badge(label)} "
 295        available = max(12, width - len(_strip_ansi(prefix)))
 296        wrapped = textwrap.wrap(text, width=available) or [""]
 297        lines.append(_fit_ansi(prefix + wrapped[0], width))
 298        if len(lines) >= limit:
 299            return lines
 300        continuation_prefix = " " * len(_strip_ansi(prefix))
 301        for part in wrapped[1:2]:
 302            lines.append(_fit_ansi(continuation_prefix + part, width))
 303            if len(lines) >= limit:
 304                return lines
 305        if len(lines) >= limit:
 306            return lines
 307    return lines
 308
 309
 310def chat_updates_pane_lines(
 311    *,
 312    job: dict[str, Any],
 313    events: list[dict[str, Any]],
 314    width: int,
 315    rows: int,
 316) -> list[str]:
 317    lines = [
 318        f"{_muted('Page')}   {_page_indicator('updates', CHAT_RIGHT_PAGES)}",
 319        f"{_muted('Focus')}  {_bold(_one_line(job.get('title') or 'untitled', width - 8))}",
 320        "",
 321        _bold("Outcomes by hour"),
 322        _muted("Summaries of durable output, findings, measurements, decisions, and files."),
 323        "",
 324    ]
 325    update_lines = hourly_update_lines(events, width=width, limit=max(4, rows - len(lines)))
 326    if update_lines:
 327        lines.extend(update_lines)
 328    else:
 329        lines.append(_muted("No durable model updates yet. Tool calls are on Work."))
 330    return [_fit_ansi(line, width) for line in lines[:rows]]
 331
 332
 333def hourly_update_lines(events: list[dict[str, Any]], *, width: int, limit: int) -> list[str]:
 334    if limit <= 0:
 335        return []
 336    buckets: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
 337    order: list[str] = []
 338    for event in events:
 339        parsed = model_update_event_parts(event, width=max(width, 220), compact=False)
 340        if not parsed:
 341            continue
 342        label, text, clock = parsed
 343        hour = _event_hour(event)
 344        if hour not in buckets:
 345            buckets[hour] = {"counts": {}, "items": [], "clock": clock}
 346            order.append(hour)
 347        bucket = buckets[hour]
 348        counts = bucket["counts"]
 349        counts[label] = int(counts.get(label) or 0) + 1
 350        item = (label, text)
 351        if item not in bucket["items"]:
 352            bucket["items"].append(item)
 353    rendered: list[str] = []
 354    recent_hours = order[-max(1, min(len(order), limit)) :]
 355    per_bucket = max(2, min(8, (limit // max(1, len(recent_hours))) - 1))
 356    for hour in recent_hours:
 357        bucket = buckets[hour]
 358        counts = bucket["counts"]
 359        summary = hourly_outcome_summary(counts)
 360        rendered.append(_fit_ansi(f"{_muted(hour)} {_bold(summary or 'activity')}", width))
 361        primary_items = [item for item in bucket["items"] if item[0] in PRIMARY_OUTCOME_LABELS]
 362        visible_items = primary_items or bucket["items"]
 363        for label, text in visible_items[-per_bucket:]:
 364            prefix = f"  {_event_badge(label)} "
 365            available = max(16, width - len(_strip_ansi(prefix)))
 366            parts = textwrap.wrap(text, width=available) or [""]
 367            rendered.append(_fit_ansi(prefix + parts[0], width))
 368            for part in parts[1:]:
 369                rendered.append(_fit_ansi(" " * len(_strip_ansi(prefix)) + part, width))
 370                if len(rendered) >= limit:
 371                    return rendered[:limit]
 372        if len(rendered) >= limit:
 373            return rendered[:limit]
 374    return rendered[-limit:]
 375
 376
 377def _outcome_text(text: str, *, chars: int, compact: bool) -> str:
 378    clean = generic_display_text(text)
 379    if compact:
 380        return _one_line(clean, chars)
 381    return _one_line(clean, 900)
 382
 383
 384def hourly_outcome_summary(counts: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
 385    pieces: list[str] = []
 386    for label in sorted(counts):
 387        count = int(counts.get(label) or 0)
 388        if count <= 0:
 389            continue
 390        name = OUTCOME_SUMMARY_NAMES.get(label, label.lower())
 391        pieces.append(f"{count} {name}")
 392    return " ".join(pieces)
 393
 394
 395def minimal_live_event_line(event: dict[str, Any], *, chars: int = 92) -> str:
 396    kind = str(event.get("event_type") or "")
 397    title = str(event.get("title") or "").strip()
 398    body = generic_display_text(event.get("body") or "")
 399    metadata = event.get("metadata") if isinstance(event.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 400    status = str(metadata.get("status") or "")
 401    if kind == "operator_message":
 402        return ""
 403    if kind == "agent_message" and title == "chat":
 404        return ""
 405    if kind == "operator_context":
 406        return _one_line(f"operator {title or body}", chars)
 407    if kind == "tool_call":
 408        if title in LOW_SIGNAL_FRAME_TOOLS:
 409            return ""
 410        return _one_line("start " + tool_live_summary(title, metadata, body), chars)
 411    if kind == "tool_result":
 412        if title in LOW_SIGNAL_FRAME_TOOLS and status == "completed":
 413            return ""
 414        if title == "llm" and status == "failed":
 415            return ""
 416        prefix = "blocked" if status == "blocked" else "failed" if status == "failed" else "done"
 417        detail = friendly_error_text(body or title) if status in {"blocked", "failed"} else tool_live_summary(title, metadata, body)
 418        return _one_line(f"{prefix} {detail}", chars)
 419    if kind == "error":
 420        detail = friendly_error_text(body or title or "error")
 421        return _one_line(f"error {detail}", chars)
 422    if kind == "artifact":
 423        return _one_line(f"saved {title or body or 'output'}", chars)
 424    if kind == "finding":
 425        return _one_line(f"finding {title or body}", chars)
 426    if kind == "source":
 427        return _one_line(f"source {title or body}", chars)
 428    if kind == "task":
 429        return _one_line(f"task {title or body}", chars)
 430    if kind == "roadmap":
 431        return _one_line(f"roadmap {title or body}", chars)
 432    if kind == "milestone_validation":
 433        validation = str(metadata.get("validation_status") or metadata.get("status") or "")
 434        return _one_line(f"validate {validation} {title or body}".strip(), chars)
 435    if kind == "experiment":
 436        return _one_line(f"experiment {title or body}", chars)
 437    if kind == "lesson":
 438        detail = event_title_body(title, body, fallback="lesson")
 439        return _one_line(f"learned {detail}", chars)
 440    if kind == "reflection":
 441        return _one_line(f"reflect {brief_reflection_text(body or title)}", chars)
 442    if kind == "agent_message":
 443        if title not in {"chat", "progress", "update", "report"}:
 444            return ""
 445        return _one_line(f"update {body or title}", chars)
 446    if kind in {"daemon", "loop"}:
 447        return ""
 448    return ""
 449
 450
 451def live_badge(text: str) -> str:
 452    badge_text = re.sub(r"^x[0-9]+\s+", "", text)
 453    if badge_text.startswith("error") or badge_text.startswith("failed"):
 454        return _style("FAIL", "31")
 455    if badge_text.startswith("blocked"):
 456        return _style("BLOCK", "33")
 457    if badge_text.startswith("start"):
 458        return _style("run ", "36")
 459    if badge_text.startswith("done"):
 460        return _style("done", "32")
 461    if badge_text.startswith("saved"):
 462        return _style("save", "32")
 463    if badge_text.startswith("finding"):
 464        return _style("find", "32")
 465    if badge_text.startswith("source"):
 466        return _style("src ", "36")
 467    if badge_text.startswith("experiment"):
 468        return _style("test", "33")
 469    if badge_text.startswith("task"):
 470        return _style("task", "33")
 471    if badge_text.startswith("learned"):
 472        return _style("mem ", "36")
 473    if badge_text.startswith("reflect"):
 474        return _style("plan", "35")
 475    if badge_text.startswith("update"):
 476        return _style("note", "35")
 477    return _style("info", "2")
 478
 479
 480def live_display_text(text: str, *, count: int = 1) -> str:
 481    if count > 1 and (
 482        text.startswith("error")
 483        or text.startswith("failed")
 484        or text.startswith("blocked")
 485    ):
 486        return f"x{count} {text}"
 487    base = text
 488    for prefix in (
 489        "start ",
 490        "done ",
 491        "saved ",
 492        "finding ",
 493        "source ",
 494        "experiment ",
 495        "task ",
 496        "learned ",
 497        "reflect ",
 498        "update ",
 499    ):
 500        if text.startswith(prefix):
 501            base = text[len(prefix) :]
 502            break
 503    if count > 1:
 504        return f"{base} x{count}"
 505    return base
 506
 507
 508def tool_live_summary(tool: str, metadata: dict[str, Any], body: str) -> str:
 509    args = event_tool_args(metadata)
 510    clean_body = clean_step_summary(body)
 511    if tool == "web_search":
 512        query = str(args.get("query") or _regex_group(r"query='([^']+)'", clean_body) or "")
 513        return f"search {query}" if query else "search web"
 514    if tool == "web_extract":
 515        urls = args.get("urls") if isinstance(args.get("urls"), list) else []
 516        count = len(urls)
 517        fetched = _regex_group(r"fetched ([0-9]+/[0-9]+ pages)", clean_body)
 518        return f"extract {fetched or (str(count) + ' pages' if count else 'pages')}"
 519    if tool == "shell_exec":
 520        command = str(args.get("command") or _regex_group(r"cmd='([^']+)'", clean_body) or "")
 521        prefix = f"shell {_short_command(command)}" if command else "shell command"
 522        rc = metadata.get("output", {}).get("returncode") if isinstance(metadata.get("output"), dict) else None
 523        return f"{prefix} rc={rc}" if rc is not None else prefix
 524    if tool == "browser_navigate":
 525        url = str(args.get("url") or _regex_group(r"<([^>]+)>", clean_body) or "")
 526        return f"open {_short_url(url)}" if url else "open page"
 527    if tool == "browser_snapshot":
 528        return "snapshot page"
 529    if tool == "browser_click":
 530        ref = str(args.get("ref") or "")
 531        return f"click {ref}" if ref else "click page"
 532    if tool == "browser_scroll":
 533        return f"scroll {args.get('direction') or 'page'}"
 534    if tool == "write_artifact":
 535        return "save output"
 536    if tool == "write_file":
 537        args_path = str(args.get("path") or "")
 538        output = metadata.get("output") if isinstance(metadata.get("output"), dict) else {}
 539        path = str(output.get("path") or args_path)
 540        return f"update {_short_path(path, max_width=36)}" if path else "update file"
 541    if tool == "defer_job":
 542        seconds = args.get("seconds") or args.get("delay_seconds")
 543        until = args.get("until")
 544        if until:
 545            return f"wait until {until}"
 546        return f"wait {seconds}s" if seconds else "wait before next check"
 547    if tool == "record_lesson":
 548        return "learn memory"
 549    if tool == "record_source":
 550        return "score source"
 551    if tool == "record_findings":
 552        return "record findings"
 553    if tool == "record_tasks":
 554        return "update tasks"
 555    if tool == "record_roadmap":
 556        return "update roadmap"
 557    if tool == "record_milestone_validation":
 558        return "validate roadmap"
 559    if tool == "record_experiment":
 560        return "record experiment"
 561    if tool == "acknowledge_operator_context":
 562        return "ack operator"
 563    if tool == "report_update":
 564        return "report update"
 565    if tool == "read_artifact":
 566        return "read output"
 567    if tool == "search_artifacts":
 568        return "search outputs"
 569    return tool or clean_body or "step"
 570
 571
 572def event_tool_args(metadata: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
 573    input_data = metadata.get("input") if isinstance(metadata.get("input"), dict) else {}
 574    args = input_data.get("arguments") if isinstance(input_data.get("arguments"), dict) else {}
 575    return args
 576
 577
 578def shell_write_target(command: str) -> str:
 579    if not command.strip():
 580        return ""
 581    redirect = re.search(r"(?:^|\s)(?:1?>|>>)\s*([^\s;&|]+)", command)
 582    if redirect:
 583        target = redirect.group(1).strip("'\"")
 584        if target and not target.startswith("&"):
 585            return target
 586    try:
 587        parts = shlex.split(command)
 588    except ValueError:
 589        parts = command.split()
 590    for index, part in enumerate(parts):
 591        if part != "tee":
 592            continue
 593        for candidate in parts[index + 1 :]:
 594            if candidate.startswith("-"):
 595                continue
 596            return candidate
 597    return ""
 598
 599
 600def event_title_body(title: str, body: str, *, fallback: str) -> str:
 601    if title and body and title not in body:
 602        return f"{title} - {body}"
 603    return title or body or fallback
 604
 605
 606def experiment_metric_text(metadata: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
 607    value = metadata.get("metric_value")
 608    if value in (None, ""):
 609        return ""
 610    name = metadata.get("metric_name") or "metric"
 611    unit = metadata.get("metric_unit") or ""
 612    direction = metadata.get("result_direction") or metadata.get("decision") or ""
 613    return " ".join(part for part in [format_metric_value(name, value, unit), str(direction)] if part)
 614
 615
 616def event_clock(event: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
 617    compact = _compact_time(str(event.get("created_at") or ""))
 618    if len(compact) >= 16 and compact[10:11] == " ":
 619        return compact[11:16]
 620    return "" if compact == "?" else _one_line(compact, 5)
 621
 622
 623def _event_hour(event: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
 624    compact = _compact_time(str(event.get("created_at") or ""))
 625    if len(compact) >= 13 and compact[10:11] == " ":
 626        return f"{compact[:13]}:00"
 627    if len(compact) >= 2:
 628        return compact
 629    return "recent"
 630
 631
 632def friendly_error_text(text: str) -> str:
 633    lowered = text.lower()
 634    if "key limit exceeded" in lowered:
 635        return "Provider key limit exceeded. Update the key limit or switch models."
 636    if "authenticationerror" in lowered or "user not found" in lowered or "401" in lowered:
 637        return "Model authentication failed. Update the API key with /api-key, then try again."
 638    if "permissiondeniederror" in lowered or "403" in lowered:
 639        return "Provider permission denied. Check model access or key limits."
 640    return _one_line(clean_step_summary(text), 220)
 641
 642
 643def brief_reflection_text(text: str) -> str:
 644    clean = clean_step_summary(text)
 645    match = re.search(r"Reflection through step #?([0-9]+):\s*(.*?)(?:\. Best |\.\s*$|$)", clean)
 646    if match:
 647        counts = match.group(2)
 648        counts = counts.replace(", 0 active operator messages", "")
 649        counts = counts.replace(", 0 recent finding artifacts", "")
 650        return _one_line(f"reflected #{match.group(1)}: {counts}", 140)
 651    return _one_line(clean, 140)
 652
 653
 654def generic_display_text(value: Any) -> str:
 655    return " ".join(str(value).split())
 656
 657
 658def clean_step_summary(summary: Any) -> str:
 659    text = " ".join(str(summary).split())
 660    if text.startswith("write_artifact saved ") and " at /" in text:
 661        return text.split(" at /", 1)[0]
 662    return text
 663
 664
 665def _chat_message_paragraphs(value: Any) -> list[str]:
 666    text = str(value).replace("\r\n", "\n").replace("\r", "\n")
 667    text = re.sub(r"\*\*([^*]+)\*\*", r"\1", text)
 668    text = re.sub(r"`([^`]+)`", r"\1", text)
 669    text = re.sub(r"(?<!^)\s(?=(?:[0-9]+\.|[-*])\s+)", "\n", text)
 670    paragraphs: list[str] = []
 671    for raw in text.splitlines():
 672        line = " ".join(raw.strip().split())
 673        if line:
 674            paragraphs.append(line)
 675    return paragraphs or [""]
 676
 677
 678def _chat_agent_message_text(title: str, body: str) -> str:
 679    lowered = title.lower()
 680    if lowered == "chat":
 681        return body
 682    if lowered in {"plan", "planning"}:
 683        plan_body = body.split("Questions:", 1)[0]
 684        tasks = len(re.findall(r"(?:^|\s)- ", plan_body))
 685        if tasks:
 686            return f"Plan drafted with {tasks} items. Reply with changes or start work from the controls."
 687        return "Plan drafted. Reply with changes or start work from the controls."
 688    if lowered in {"progress", "update", "report"}:
 689        return _one_line(clean_step_summary(body), 220)
 690    return ""
 691
 692
 693def _regex_group(pattern: str, text: str) -> str:
 694    match = re.search(pattern, text)
 695    return match.group(1) if match else ""
 696
 697
 698def _short_url(url: str) -> str:
 699    if not url:
 700        return ""
 701    stripped = url.replace("https://", "").replace("http://", "")
 702    return stripped.split("/", 1)[0] or stripped
 703
 704
 705def _short_command(command: str) -> str:
 706    if not command:
 707        return ""
 708    try:
 709        parts = shlex.split(command)
 710    except ValueError:
 711        parts = command.split()
 712    if not parts:
 713        return ""
 714    if parts[0] == "ssh":
 715        host = next((part for part in parts[1:] if not part.startswith("-") and "=" not in part), "")
 716        remote = " ".join(parts[parts.index(host) + 1 :]) if host in parts else ""
 717        if remote:
 718            remote_parts = remote.split()
 719            remote_head = remote_parts[0] if remote_parts else "remote"
 720            return f"ssh {host} {remote_head}"
 721        return f"ssh {host}".strip()
 722    if parts[0] in {"python", "python3", "uv", "npm", "pnpm", "yarn", "node"} and len(parts) > 1:
 723        return " ".join(parts[:3])
 724    return " ".join(parts[:2])
 725
 726
 727def _short_path(path: Path | str, *, max_width: int = 80) -> str:
 728    text = str(path)
 729    home = str(Path.home())
 730    if text.startswith(home + os.sep):
 731        text = "~" + text[len(home) :]
 732    if len(text) <= max_width:
 733        return text
 734    keep = max(12, max_width - 4)
 735    return "..." + text[-keep:]
 736
 737
 738def _compact_time(value: str) -> str:
 739    text = value.replace("T", " ")
 740    if len(text) >= 16 and text[4:5] == "-" and text[13:14] == ":":
 741        return text[:16]
 742    return _one_line(text, 16)
nipux_cli/tui_input.py 80 lines 
   1"""Terminal input helpers for full-screen Nipux frames."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import os
   6import re
   7import select
   8import sys
   9import time
  10
  11
  12def read_terminal_char(fd: int) -> str:
  13    data = os.read(fd, 1)
  14    return data.decode("latin1", errors="ignore")
  15
  16
  17def read_escape_sequence(first: str, *, fd: int | None = None) -> str:
  18    fd = sys.stdin.fileno() if fd is None else fd
  19    sequence = first
  20    deadline = time.monotonic() + 0.12
  21    while len(sequence) < 96:
  22        timeout = max(0.0, min(0.04, deadline - time.monotonic()))
  23        if timeout <= 0:
  24            break
  25        readable, _, _ = select.select([fd], [], [], timeout)
  26        if not readable:
  27            break
  28        sequence += read_terminal_char(fd)
  29        if terminal_escape_complete(sequence):
  30            break
  31    return sequence
  32
  33
  34def terminal_escape_complete(sequence: str) -> bool:
  35    if sequence in {"\x1b[A", "\x1b[B", "\x1b[C", "\x1b[D", "\x1bOA", "\x1bOB", "\x1bOC", "\x1bOD"}:
  36        return True
  37    if re.match(r"^\x1b\[[0-9;?]*[ABCD]$", sequence):
  38        return True
  39    if re.match(r"^\x1b\[<\d+;\d+;\d+[mM]$", sequence):
  40        return True
  41    if sequence.startswith("\x1b[M") and len(sequence) >= 6:
  42        return True
  43    return False
  44
  45
  46def decode_terminal_escape(sequence: str) -> tuple[str, tuple[int, int] | None]:
  47    arrows = {
  48        "\x1b[A": "up",
  49        "\x1b[B": "down",
  50        "\x1b[C": "right",
  51        "\x1b[D": "left",
  52        "\x1bOA": "up",
  53        "\x1bOB": "down",
  54        "\x1bOC": "right",
  55        "\x1bOD": "left",
  56    }
  57    if sequence in arrows:
  58        return arrows[sequence], None
  59    csi_arrow = re.match(r"^\x1b\[[0-9;?]*([ABCD])$", sequence)
  60    if csi_arrow:
  61        return {"A": "up", "B": "down", "C": "right", "D": "left"}[csi_arrow.group(1)], None
  62    match = re.match(r"^\x1b\[<(\d+);(\d+);(\d+)([mM])$", sequence)
  63    if match and match.group(4) == "M":
  64        button = int(match.group(1))
  65        if button == 0:
  66            return "click", (int(match.group(2)), int(match.group(3)))
  67    if sequence.startswith("\x1b[M") and len(sequence) >= 6:
  68        button = ord(sequence[3]) - 32
  69        if button == 0:
  70            return "click", (ord(sequence[4]) - 32, ord(sequence[5]) - 32)
  71    return "unknown", None
  72
  73
  74def drain_pending_input(fd: int | None = None) -> None:
  75    fd = sys.stdin.fileno() if fd is None else fd
  76    while True:
  77        readable, _, _ = select.select([fd], [], [], 0)
  78        if not readable:
  79            return
  80        os.read(fd, 1)
nipux_cli/tui_layout.py 212 lines 
   1"""Reusable terminal layout primitives for Nipux frames."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5from typing import Any
   6
   7from nipux_cli.tui_style import (
   8    _accent,
   9    _bold,
  10    _fancy_ui,
  11    _fit_ansi,
  12    _muted,
  13    _one_line,
  14    _strip_ansi,
  15    _style,
  16)
  17
  18
  19def _top_bar(
  20    width: int,
  21    *,
  22    state: str,
  23    daemon: str,
  24    model: str,
  25    token_usage: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
  26    context_length: int = 0,
  27    base_url: str = "",
  28) -> list[str]:
  29    dots = f"{_style('●', '31')} {_style('●', '33')} {_style('●', '32')}  " if _fancy_ui() else ""
  30    title = f"{dots}{_bold(_accent('Nipux CLI'))} {_status_dot(state)}"
  31    daemon_compact = "running" if daemon.startswith("running") else "stopped"
  32    runtime = _pill("daemon", daemon_compact)
  33    usage_text = _token_usage_topline(token_usage or {}, context_length=context_length, model=model, base_url=base_url)
  34    model_line = f"{_muted('model')} {_style(_one_line(model, max(16, width // 3)), '36')}"
  35    first = _edge_line(title, runtime, width=width)
  36    second = _edge_line(model_line, usage_text, width=width)
  37    return [
  38        first,
  39        second,
  40        _muted("─" * width),
  41    ]
  42
  43
  44def _two_col_title(left_width: int, right_width: int, left: str, right: str) -> str:
  45    return _muted("╭─ ") + _fit_ansi(_bold(left), max(0, left_width - 3)) + _muted(" │ ") + _fit_ansi(_bold(right), right_width)
  46
  47
  48def _two_col_line(left: str, right: str, *, left_width: int, right_width: int) -> str:
  49    return _fit_ansi(left, left_width) + _muted(" │ ") + _fit_ansi(right, right_width)
  50
  51
  52def _edge_line(left: str, right: str, *, width: int) -> str:
  53    right_len = len(_strip_ansi(right))
  54    left_width = max(0, width - right_len - 2)
  55    left_text = _fit_ansi(left, left_width)
  56    gap = max(1, width - len(_strip_ansi(left_text)) - right_len)
  57    return _fit_ansi(left_text + " " * gap + right, width)
  58
  59
  60def _compose_bar(
  61    input_buffer: str,
  62    *,
  63    width: int,
  64    hint: str | None = None,
  65    suggestions: list[str] | None = None,
  66    prompt_label: str = "❯",
  67    mask_input: bool = False,
  68) -> list[str]:
  69    if mask_input:
  70        visible_input = "•" * min(len(input_buffer), max(8, width - 8))
  71    else:
  72        visible_input = input_buffer[-max(8, width - 8) :]
  73    hint = _muted(hint or "Enter sends  /jobs switches  /run starts  /help commands")
  74    label = _accent(prompt_label) if prompt_label == "❯" else _muted(prompt_label)
  75    prompt = f"{label} {visible_input}{_accent('▌')}"
  76    title = " Compose "
  77    lines = []
  78    if suggestions:
  79        lines.extend(suggestions)
  80    lines.extend(
  81        [
  82            _muted("╭─") + _bold(title) + _muted("─" * max(0, width - len(title) - 2)),
  83            _fit_ansi(hint, width),
  84            _fit_ansi(prompt, width),
  85        ]
  86    )
  87    return lines
  88
  89
  90def _metric_strip(items: list[tuple[str, Any]], *, width: int) -> str:
  91    parts = [f"{_muted(label)} {_bold(value)}" for label, value in items]
  92    text = "  ".join(parts)
  93    if len(_strip_ansi(text)) <= width:
  94        return text
  95    compact = [f"{label}:{value}" for label, value in items]
  96    return _one_line("  ".join(compact), width)
  97
  98
  99def _pill(label: str, value: Any) -> str:
 100    value_text = str(value)
 101    color = "36"
 102    lowered = value_text.lower()
 103    if any(term in lowered for term in ("running", "active", "advancing", "ok")):
 104        color = "32"
 105    elif any(term in lowered for term in ("paused", "idle", "queued", "planning")):
 106        color = "33"
 107    elif any(term in lowered for term in ("failed", "cancelled", "error", "stopped")):
 108        color = "31"
 109    return f"{_muted(label)} {_style(value_text, color)}"
 110
 111
 112def _token_usage_topline(
 113    usage: dict[str, Any],
 114    *,
 115    context_length: int,
 116    model: str,
 117    base_url: str,
 118) -> str:
 119    calls = _safe_int(usage.get("calls"))
 120    if calls <= 0:
 121        return (
 122            f"{_muted('ctx')} {_style('0', '36')}  "
 123            f"{_muted('out')} {_style('0', '36')}  "
 124            f"{_muted('tok')} {_style('0', '36')}  "
 125            f"{_muted('cost')} {_style('$0.00', '36')}"
 126        )
 127    latest_prompt = _safe_int(usage.get("latest_prompt_tokens"))
 128    completion = _safe_int(usage.get("completion_tokens"))
 129    total = _safe_int(usage.get("total_tokens")) or latest_prompt + completion
 130    ctx_text = _format_compact_count(latest_prompt)
 131    if context_length > 0:
 132        ctx_text = f"{ctx_text}/{_format_compact_count(context_length)}"
 133    cost_text = _format_usage_cost(usage, model=model, base_url=base_url)
 134    return (
 135        f"{_muted('ctx')} {_style(ctx_text, '36')}  "
 136        f"{_muted('out')} {_style(_format_compact_count(completion), '36')}  "
 137        f"{_muted('tok')} {_style(_format_compact_count(total), '36')}  "
 138        f"{_muted('cost')} {_style(cost_text, '36')}"
 139    )
 140
 141
 142def _model_cost_is_zero(*, model: str, base_url: str) -> bool:
 143    lowered_model = model.lower()
 144    lowered_url = base_url.lower()
 145    return (
 146        lowered_model.endswith(":free")
 147        or lowered_model in {"local-model", "fake", "test"}
 148        or "localhost" in lowered_url
 149        or "127.0.0.1" in lowered_url
 150    )
 151
 152
 153def _format_usage_cost(usage: dict[str, Any], *, model: str, base_url: str) -> str:
 154    if bool(usage.get("has_cost")):
 155        return f"${_safe_float(usage.get('cost')):.4f}"
 156    if _model_cost_is_zero(model=model, base_url=base_url):
 157        return "$0.00"
 158    input_rate = _safe_optional_float(usage.get("input_cost_per_million"))
 159    output_rate = _safe_optional_float(usage.get("output_cost_per_million"))
 160    if input_rate is not None and output_rate is not None:
 161        prompt = _safe_int(usage.get("prompt_tokens"))
 162        completion = _safe_int(usage.get("completion_tokens"))
 163        if prompt > 0 or completion > 0:
 164            estimated = (prompt / 1_000_000 * input_rate) + (completion / 1_000_000 * output_rate)
 165            return f"~${estimated:.4f}"
 166    if _safe_int(usage.get("estimated_calls")):
 167        return "pending"
 168    return "pending"
 169
 170
 171def _format_compact_count(value: Any) -> str:
 172    number = _safe_int(value)
 173    if number >= 1_000_000_000:
 174        return f"{number / 1_000_000_000:.1f}B"
 175    if number >= 1_000_000:
 176        return f"{number / 1_000_000:.1f}M"
 177    if number >= 1_000:
 178        return f"{number / 1_000:.1f}K"
 179    return str(number)
 180
 181
 182def _safe_int(value: Any) -> int:
 183    try:
 184        return int(float(value))
 185    except (TypeError, ValueError):
 186        return 0
 187
 188
 189def _safe_float(value: Any) -> float:
 190    try:
 191        return float(value)
 192    except (TypeError, ValueError):
 193        return 0.0
 194
 195
 196def _safe_optional_float(value: Any) -> float | None:
 197    if value in (None, ""):
 198        return None
 199    try:
 200        return float(value)
 201    except (TypeError, ValueError):
 202        return None
 203
 204
 205def _status_dot(state: str) -> str:
 206    if state in {"advancing", "running", "active"}:
 207        return _style("●", "32")
 208    if state in {"paused", "queued", "planning", "idle"}:
 209        return _style("●", "33")
 210    if state in {"failed", "cancelled"}:
 211        return _style("●", "31")
 212    return _style("●", "36")
nipux_cli/tui_status.py 302 lines 
   1"""Status and work-pane renderers for the Nipux terminal UI."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import textwrap
   6from typing import Any
   7
   8from nipux_cli.operator_context import active_prompt_operator_entries
   9from nipux_cli.scheduling import job_deferred_until
  10from nipux_cli.tui_events import (
  11    CHAT_RIGHT_PAGES,
  12    experiment_metric_text,
  13    latest_durable_outcome_line,
  14    recent_model_update_lines,
  15    worker_activity_lines,
  16)
  17from nipux_cli.tui_layout import _metric_strip
  18from nipux_cli.tui_style import (
  19    _accent,
  20    _bold,
  21    _event_badge,
  22    _fit_ansi,
  23    _muted,
  24    _one_line,
  25    _page_indicator,
  26    _status_badge,
  27)
  28
  29
  30def worker_label(job: dict[str, Any], daemon_running: bool) -> str:
  31    status = str(job.get("status") or "")
  32    if status == "planning":
  33        return "waiting"
  34    if status in {"paused", "completed", "cancelled", "failed"}:
  35        return status
  36    if job_deferred_until(job):
  37        return "waiting"
  38    return "active" if daemon_running and status in {"running", "queued"} else "idle"
  39
  40
  41def job_display_state(job: dict[str, Any], daemon_running: bool) -> str:
  42    status = str(job.get("status") or "")
  43    if status in {"running", "queued"}:
  44        if job_deferred_until(job):
  45            return "waiting"
  46        return "advancing" if daemon_running else "open"
  47    return status or "unknown"
  48
  49
  50def active_operator_messages(metadata: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
  51    messages = metadata.get("operator_messages") if isinstance(metadata.get("operator_messages"), list) else []
  52    return [
  53        entry
  54        for entry in messages
  55        if isinstance(entry, dict)
  56        and entry in active_prompt_operator_entries(messages)
  57        and str(entry.get("mode") or "steer") in {"steer", "follow_up"}
  58    ]
  59
  60
  61def right_pane_lines(
  62    *,
  63    job: dict[str, Any],
  64    jobs: list[dict[str, Any]],
  65    job_artifacts: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]],
  66    job_id: str,
  67    daemon_running: bool,
  68    state: str,
  69    worker: str,
  70    daemon_text: str,
  71    model: str,
  72    goal_text: str,
  73    latest_text: str,
  74    metrics: list[tuple[str, Any]],
  75    events: list[dict[str, Any]],
  76    width: int,
  77    rows: int,
  78    right_view: str = "status",
  79) -> list[str]:
  80    del model
  81    info_lines = _chat_workspace_lines(
  82        right_view=right_view,
  83        job=job,
  84        state=state,
  85        worker=worker,
  86        daemon_text=daemon_text,
  87        goal_text=goal_text,
  88        latest_text=latest_text,
  89        metrics=metrics,
  90        width=width,
  91    )
  92    metadata = job.get("metadata") if isinstance(job.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
  93    active_operator = active_operator_messages(metadata)
  94    pending_measurement = (
  95        metadata.get("pending_measurement_obligation")
  96        if isinstance(metadata.get("pending_measurement_obligation"), dict)
  97        else {}
  98    )
  99    if active_operator:
 100        info_lines.append(f"{_muted('Operator')} {len(active_operator)} active")
 101        info_lines.append(f"{_muted('Context')} {_one_line(active_operator[-1].get('message') or '', width - 8)}")
 102    if pending_measurement:
 103        info_lines.append(f"{_muted('Measure')} pending step #{pending_measurement.get('source_step_no') or '?'}")
 104    defer_line = _defer_status_line(job, width=width)
 105    if defer_line:
 106        info_lines.append(defer_line)
 107    latest_outcome = latest_durable_outcome_line(events, width=width)
 108    if latest_outcome:
 109        info_lines.append(latest_outcome)
 110    info_lines.append("")
 111    info_lines.append(_bold("Jobs"))
 112    info_lines.extend(
 113        frame_jobs_lines(
 114            jobs[:5],
 115            focused_job_id=job_id,
 116            daemon_running=daemon_running,
 117            width=width,
 118            job_artifacts=job_artifacts,
 119            show_outputs=True,
 120        )
 121    )
 122    info_lines.append("")
 123    info_lines.append(_bold("Recent outcomes"))
 124    outcome_lines = recent_model_update_lines(events, width=width, limit=max(3, rows - len(info_lines)))
 125    if outcome_lines:
 126        info_lines.extend(outcome_lines)
 127    else:
 128        current_outputs = job_artifacts.get(job_id) or []
 129        if current_outputs:
 130            for artifact in current_outputs[:4]:
 131                title = _one_line(str(artifact.get("title") or artifact.get("id") or "output"), max(10, width - 8))
 132                info_lines.append(_fit_ansi(f"{_event_badge('SAVE')} {title}", width))
 133        else:
 134            info_lines.append(_muted("No durable outcomes yet."))
 135    return info_lines[:rows]
 136
 137
 138def chat_work_pane_lines(
 139    *,
 140    job: dict[str, Any],
 141    events: list[dict[str, Any]],
 142    tasks: list[dict[str, Any]],
 143    experiments: list[dict[str, Any]],
 144    width: int,
 145    rows: int,
 146) -> list[str]:
 147    lines = [
 148        f"{_muted('Page')}   {_page_indicator('work', CHAT_RIGHT_PAGES)}",
 149        f"{_muted('Focus')}  {_bold(_one_line(job.get('title') or 'untitled', width - 8))}",
 150        "",
 151        _bold("Tool / console"),
 152    ]
 153    tool_lines = worker_activity_lines(events, width=width, limit=max(4, rows // 2))
 154    if tool_lines:
 155        lines.extend(tool_lines)
 156    else:
 157        lines.append(_muted("No recent tool calls."))
 158    remaining = max(0, rows - len(lines))
 159    if remaining > 4:
 160        lines.append("")
 161        lines.append(_bold("Tasks"))
 162        for task in _rank_visible_tasks(tasks)[: max(1, remaining // 2)]:
 163            status = str(task.get("status") or "open")
 164            title = _one_line(str(task.get("title") or "task"), max(10, width - 15))
 165            lines.append(_fit_ansi(f"{_status_badge(status)} {title}", width))
 166    remaining = max(0, rows - len(lines))
 167    if remaining > 3 and experiments:
 168        lines.append("")
 169        lines.append(_bold("Measurements"))
 170        for experiment in experiments[-max(1, remaining - 2) :]:
 171            metric = experiment_metric_text(experiment)
 172            title = _one_line(str(experiment.get("title") or "experiment"), max(10, width - 16))
 173            suffix = f" {_muted(metric)}" if metric else ""
 174            lines.append(_fit_ansi(f"{_event_badge('TEST')} {title}{suffix}", width))
 175    return [_fit_ansi(line, width) for line in lines[:rows]]
 176
 177
 178def frame_jobs_lines(
 179    jobs: list[dict[str, Any]],
 180    *,
 181    focused_job_id: str,
 182    daemon_running: bool,
 183    width: int,
 184    job_artifacts: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] | None = None,
 185    show_outputs: bool = False,
 186) -> list[str]:
 187    rendered = []
 188    for index, item in enumerate(jobs[:5], start=1):
 189        item_id = str(item.get("id") or "")
 190        marker = _accent("●") if item_id == focused_job_id else _muted("○")
 191        title_width = max(14, min(30, width - 34))
 192        title = _one_line(str(item.get("title") or item.get("id") or "job"), title_width)
 193        state = _status_badge(job_display_state(item, daemon_running))
 194        worker = _status_badge(worker_label(item, daemon_running))
 195        kind = _one_line(item.get("kind") or "", max(0, width - title_width - 33))
 196        rendered.append(
 197            _fit_ansi(
 198                f"{marker} {index:<2} {_fit_ansi(title, title_width)} "
 199                f"{_fit_ansi(state, 10)} {_fit_ansi(worker, 10)} {kind}",
 200                width,
 201            )
 202        )
 203        outputs = (job_artifacts or {}).get(item_id) or []
 204        if show_outputs and outputs:
 205            latest = outputs[0]
 206            output_title = _one_line(str(latest.get("title") or latest.get("id") or "saved output"), max(8, width - 15))
 207            rendered.append(_fit_ansi(f"   {_event_badge('SAVE')} {output_title}", width))
 208    return rendered
 209
 210
 211def _defer_status_line(job: dict[str, Any], *, width: int) -> str:
 212    until = job_deferred_until(job)
 213    if not until:
 214        return ""
 215    metadata = job.get("metadata") if isinstance(job.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 216    reason = str(metadata.get("defer_reason") or metadata.get("defer_next_action") or "").strip()
 217    time_text = until.astimezone().strftime("%b %d %H:%M")
 218    detail = f"next check {time_text}"
 219    if reason:
 220        detail += f" - {reason}"
 221    return _fit_ansi(f"{_muted('Wait')}   {_one_line(detail, max(12, width - 7))}", width)
 222
 223
 224def _rank_visible_tasks(tasks: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
 225    status_order = {"active": 0, "open": 1, "blocked": 2, "validating": 3, "done": 4, "skipped": 5}
 226    return sorted(
 227        [task for task in tasks if isinstance(task, dict)],
 228        key=lambda task: (
 229            status_order.get(str(task.get("status") or "open"), 9),
 230            -int(task.get("priority") or 0),
 231            str(task.get("title") or ""),
 232        ),
 233    )
 234
 235
 236def _chat_workspace_lines(
 237    *,
 238    right_view: str,
 239    job: dict[str, Any],
 240    state: str,
 241    worker: str,
 242    daemon_text: str,
 243    goal_text: str,
 244    latest_text: str,
 245    metrics: list[tuple[str, Any]],
 246    width: int,
 247) -> list[str]:
 248    goal_lines = textwrap.wrap(goal_text, width=max(20, width - 8))[:2] or [""]
 249    while len(goal_lines) < 2:
 250        goal_lines.append("")
 251    title = _one_line(str(job.get("title") or "untitled"), max(10, width))
 252    lines = [
 253        f"{_muted('Page')}   {_page_indicator(right_view, CHAT_RIGHT_PAGES)}",
 254        _bold(title),
 255        f"{_status_badge(state)} {_muted('worker')} {_status_badge(worker)}  {_muted(_one_line(daemon_text, max(8, width - 28)))}",
 256        f"{_muted('Goal')}   {goal_lines[0]}",
 257        f"{_muted('       ')}{goal_lines[1]}",
 258        f"{_muted('Latest')} {_one_line(latest_text, width - 8)}",
 259    ]
 260    task_line = _current_task_line(job, width=width)
 261    if task_line:
 262        lines.append(task_line)
 263    lines.extend(_metrics_grid_lines(metrics, width=width))
 264    return lines
 265
 266
 267def _current_task_line(job: dict[str, Any], *, width: int) -> str:
 268    metadata = job.get("metadata") if isinstance(job.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
 269    tasks = metadata.get("task_queue") if isinstance(metadata.get("task_queue"), list) else []
 270    visible = [
 271        task
 272        for task in tasks
 273        if isinstance(task, dict)
 274        and str(task.get("status") or "open") in {"active", "open", "blocked"}
 275    ]
 276    if not visible:
 277        return ""
 278    ranked = _rank_visible_tasks(visible)
 279    task = ranked[0]
 280    status = str(task.get("status") or "open")
 281    title = _one_line(str(task.get("title") or "task"), max(12, width - 16))
 282    return _fit_ansi(f"{_muted('Task')}   {_status_badge(status)} {title}", width)
 283
 284
 285def _metrics_grid_lines(metrics: list[tuple[str, Any]], *, width: int) -> list[str]:
 286    wanted = ["actions", "outputs", "findings", "sources", "tasks", "experiments", "memory"]
 287    lookup = {label: value for label, value in metrics}
 288    items = [(label, lookup[label]) for label in wanted if label in lookup]
 289    if width < 40:
 290        return [_metric_strip(items, width=width)]
 291    lines: list[str] = []
 292    col_width = max(16, (width - 2) // 2)
 293    for index in range(0, len(items), 2):
 294        left = _metric_cell(items[index], width=col_width)
 295        right = _metric_cell(items[index + 1], width=col_width) if index + 1 < len(items) else ""
 296        lines.append(_fit_ansi(left + "  " + right, width))
 297    return lines
 298
 299
 300def _metric_cell(item: tuple[str, Any], *, width: int) -> str:
 301    label, value = item
 302    return _fit_ansi(f"{_muted(label)} {_bold(value)}", width)
nipux_cli/tui_style.py 136 lines 
   1"""Small terminal styling helpers shared by the CLI frame renderers."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import os
   6import re
   7import sys
   8from typing import Any
   9
  10
  11def _fancy_ui() -> bool:
  12    return (
  13        sys.stdout.isatty()
  14        and os.environ.get("NO_COLOR") is None
  15        and os.environ.get("NIPUX_PLAIN") is None
  16        and os.environ.get("TERM", "") not in {"", "dumb"}
  17    )
  18
  19
  20def _style(text: Any, code: str) -> str:
  21    value = str(text)
  22    if not _fancy_ui():
  23        return value
  24    return f"\033[{code}m{value}\033[0m"
  25
  26
  27def _accent(text: Any) -> str:
  28    return _style(text, "36")
  29
  30
  31def _muted(text: Any) -> str:
  32    return _style(text, "2")
  33
  34
  35def _bold(text: Any) -> str:
  36    return _style(text, "1")
  37
  38
  39def _one_line(value: Any, width: int) -> str:
  40    text = " ".join(str(value).split())
  41    if len(text) <= width:
  42        return text
  43    return text[: max(0, width - 3)] + "..."
  44
  45
  46def _strip_ansi(text: str) -> str:
  47    return re.sub(r"\x1b\[[0-9;]*m", "", text)
  48
  49
  50def _fit_ansi(text: Any, width: int) -> str:
  51    width = max(0, int(width))
  52    content = str(text)
  53    visible = _strip_ansi(content)
  54    if len(visible) > width:
  55        content = _one_line(visible, width)
  56        visible = content
  57    return content + " " * max(0, width - len(visible))
  58
  59
  60def _center_ansi(text: str, width: int) -> str:
  61    text_width = len(_strip_ansi(text))
  62    if text_width >= width:
  63        return _fit_ansi(text, width)
  64    left_pad = max(0, (width - text_width) // 2)
  65    return _fit_ansi(" " * left_pad + text, width)
  66
  67
  68def _themed_lines(lines: list[str], *, width: int) -> list[str]:
  69    if not _fancy_ui():
  70        return [_fit_ansi(line, width) for line in lines]
  71    bg = "\033[48;5;235m\033[38;5;252m"
  72    reset = "\033[0m"
  73    return [bg + _fit_ansi(line, width).replace(reset, reset + bg) + reset for line in lines]
  74
  75
  76def _page_indicator(active: str, pages: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> str:
  77    parts: list[str] = []
  78    for key, label in pages:
  79        if key == active:
  80            parts.append(f"{_accent('●')} {_bold(label)}")
  81        else:
  82            parts.append(f"{_muted('○')} {_muted(label)}")
  83    return "  ".join(parts)
  84
  85
  86def _status_badge(value: Any) -> str:
  87    text = str(value)
  88    color = {
  89        "active": "32",
  90        "advancing": "32",
  91        "running": "32",
  92        "queued": "33",
  93        "planning": "35",
  94        "waiting": "35",
  95        "open": "33",
  96        "idle": "33",
  97        "paused": "33",
  98        "cancelled": "31",
  99        "failed": "31",
 100        "completed": "36",
 101    }.get(text, "37")
 102    return _style(text, color)
 103
 104
 105def _event_badge(label: str) -> str:
 106    padded = f"{label:<8}"
 107    color = {
 108        "AGENT": "36",
 109        "USER": "35",
 110        "FOLLOW": "35",
 111        "YOU": "35",
 112        "NIPUX": "36",
 113        "RUN": "34",
 114        "TOOL": "34",
 115        "DONE": "32",
 116        "FILE": "32",
 117        "SAVE": "32",
 118        "OUTPUT": "32",
 119        "FIND": "32",
 120        "SOURCE": "36",
 121        "TASK": "33",
 122        "ROAD": "35",
 123        "VALID": "35",
 124        "TEST": "33",
 125        "UPDATE": "36",
 126        "ACK": "36",
 127        "FAIL": "31",
 128        "LEARN": "36",
 129        "PLAN": "36",
 130        "DIGEST": "36",
 131        "MEMORY": "36",
 132        "SYSTEM": "2",
 133        "BLOCK": "33",
 134        "ERROR": "31",
 135    }.get(label, "37")
 136    return _style(padded, color)
nipux_cli/updates.py 72 lines 
   1"""Readable durable progress reports for jobs."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import shlex
   6from typing import Any
   7
   8from nipux_cli.config import AppConfig
   9from nipux_cli.daemon import daemon_lock_status
  10from nipux_cli.db import AgentDB
  11from nipux_cli.tui_events import hourly_update_lines
  12from nipux_cli.tui_status import job_display_state
  13from nipux_cli.tui_style import _one_line
  14
  15
  16def render_updates_report(
  17    db: AgentDB,
  18    config: AppConfig,
  19    job_id: str,
  20    *,
  21    limit: int = 5,
  22    chars: int = 180,
  23    paths: bool = False,
  24) -> list[str]:
  25    job = db.get_job(job_id)
  26    artifacts = db.list_artifacts(job_id, limit=limit)
  27    events = db.list_timeline_events(job_id, limit=max(250, limit * 80))
  28    metadata = job.get("metadata") if isinstance(job.get("metadata"), dict) else {}
  29    operator_messages = _metadata_list(metadata, "operator_messages")
  30    agent_updates = _metadata_list(metadata, "agent_updates")
  31    lessons = _metadata_list(metadata, "lessons")
  32    daemon = daemon_lock_status(config.runtime.home / "agentd.lock")
  33    lines = [
  34        f"updates {job['title']} | state {job_display_state(job, bool(daemon['running']))}",
  35        "=" * 80,
  36    ]
  37    if operator_messages:
  38        latest = operator_messages[-1]
  39        lines.append(f"last steering: {_one_line(latest.get('message') or '', chars)}")
  40    lines.append("outcomes by hour:")
  41    outcome_lines = hourly_update_lines(events, width=max(72, chars), limit=max(8, limit * 4))
  42    if outcome_lines:
  43        lines.extend(f"  {line}" for line in outcome_lines)
  44    else:
  45        lines.append("  none yet")
  46    if agent_updates:
  47        lines.extend(["", "latest agent notes:"])
  48        for update in agent_updates[-min(limit, 5) :]:
  49            category = update.get("category") or "progress"
  50            lines.append(f"  {category}: {_one_line(update.get('message') or '', chars)}")
  51    if lessons:
  52        lines.extend(["", "latest lessons:"])
  53        for lesson in lessons[-min(limit, 5) :]:
  54            category = lesson.get("category") or "memory"
  55            lines.append(f"  {category}: {_one_line(lesson.get('lesson') or '', chars)}")
  56    lines.extend(["", "latest saved outputs:"])
  57    if not artifacts:
  58        lines.append("  none yet")
  59    for artifact in artifacts:
  60        title = artifact.get("title") or artifact["id"]
  61        summary = f" - {_one_line(artifact['summary'], chars)}" if artifact.get("summary") else ""
  62        lines.append(f"  {artifact['created_at']} {title}{summary}")
  63        lines.append(f"    view: artifact {shlex.quote(title)}")
  64        if paths:
  65            lines.append(f"    {artifact['path']}")
  66    lines.extend(["", "raw tool stream: activity"])
  67    return lines
  68
  69
  70def _metadata_list(metadata: dict[str, Any], key: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
  71    values = metadata.get(key)
  72    return [value for value in values if isinstance(value, dict)] if isinstance(values, list) else []
nipux_cli/usage.py 57 lines 
   1"""Formatting helpers for model token and cost usage."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5from typing import Any
   6
   7from nipux_cli.tui_layout import _format_compact_count, _format_usage_cost
   8
   9
  10def format_usage_report(
  11    *,
  12    title: str,
  13    usage: dict[str, Any],
  14    context_length: int,
  15    model: str,
  16    base_url: str,
  17) -> list[str]:
  18    calls = _safe_int(usage.get("calls"))
  19    prompt = _safe_int(usage.get("prompt_tokens"))
  20    completion = _safe_int(usage.get("completion_tokens"))
  21    total = _safe_int(usage.get("total_tokens")) or prompt + completion
  22    latest_prompt = _safe_int(usage.get("latest_prompt_tokens"))
  23    latest_completion = _safe_int(usage.get("latest_completion_tokens"))
  24    latest_total = _safe_int(usage.get("latest_total_tokens")) or latest_prompt + latest_completion
  25    estimated = _safe_int(usage.get("estimated_calls"))
  26    cached = _safe_int(usage.get("cached_tokens"))
  27    reasoning = _safe_int(usage.get("reasoning_tokens"))
  28    cost = _format_usage_cost(usage, model=model, base_url=base_url)
  29    context_text = _format_compact_count(latest_prompt)
  30    if context_length > 0:
  31        context_text = f"{context_text}/{_format_compact_count(context_length)}"
  32    lines = [
  33        f"usage {title}",
  34        "=" * 80,
  35        f"model: {model}",
  36        f"calls: {calls} | estimated: {estimated}",
  37        f"tokens: total={_format_compact_count(total)} prompt={_format_compact_count(prompt)} output={_format_compact_count(completion)}",
  38        f"latest: ctx={context_text} output={_format_compact_count(latest_completion)} total={_format_compact_count(latest_total)}",
  39        f"details: cached={_format_compact_count(cached)} reasoning={_format_compact_count(reasoning)} cost={cost}",
  40    ]
  41    if calls <= 0:
  42        lines.append("no model usage has been recorded for this job yet")
  43    elif estimated:
  44        lines.append("some usage is estimated because the provider did not return complete token/cost metadata")
  45    elif not bool(usage.get("has_cost")):
  46        lines.append(
  47            "cost is pending unless the provider returns cost metadata, configured token rates are set, "
  48            "or the model is local/free"
  49        )
  50    return lines
  51
  52
  53def _safe_int(value: Any) -> int:
  54    try:
  55        return int(float(value))
  56    except (TypeError, ValueError):
  57        return 0
nipux_cli/web.py 121 lines 
   1"""Small web search/extract helpers without external web tool dependencies."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import html
   6import re
   7import urllib.parse
   8import urllib.request
   9from html.parser import HTMLParser
  10from typing import Any
  11
  12from nipux_cli.source_quality import anti_bot_reason
  13
  14
  15class _TextExtractor(HTMLParser):
  16    def __init__(self):
  17        super().__init__()
  18        self.parts: list[str] = []
  19        self.skip_depth = 0
  20
  21    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs):
  22        del attrs
  23        if tag in {"script", "style", "noscript"}:
  24            self.skip_depth += 1
  25        if tag in {"p", "br", "div", "section", "article", "li", "h1", "h2", "h3"}:
  26            self.parts.append("\n")
  27
  28    def handle_endtag(self, tag: str):
  29        if tag in {"script", "style", "noscript"} and self.skip_depth:
  30            self.skip_depth -= 1
  31        if tag in {"p", "div", "section", "article", "li"}:
  32            self.parts.append("\n")
  33
  34    def handle_data(self, data: str):
  35        if not self.skip_depth:
  36            text = data.strip()
  37            if text:
  38                self.parts.append(text)
  39
  40    def text(self) -> str:
  41        raw = " ".join(self.parts)
  42        raw = re.sub(r"[ \t\r\f\v]+", " ", raw)
  43        raw = re.sub(r"\n\s+", "\n", raw)
  44        raw = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", raw)
  45        return html.unescape(raw).strip()
  46
  47
  48def _request(url: str, *, timeout: int = 20, max_bytes: int = 2_000_000) -> tuple[str, str]:
  49    request = urllib.request.Request(
  50        url,
  51        headers={
  52            "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; nipux/0.1; +https://github.com/nipuxx/agent-cli)",
  53            "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,text/plain;q=0.8,*/*;q=0.5",
  54        },
  55    )
  56    with urllib.request.urlopen(request, timeout=timeout) as response:
  57        content_type = response.headers.get("content-type", "")
  58        body = response.read(max_bytes + 1)
  59    if len(body) > max_bytes:
  60        raise ValueError(f"response exceeded {max_bytes} bytes")
  61    charset = "utf-8"
  62    match = re.search(r"charset=([\w.-]+)", content_type, re.I)
  63    if match:
  64        charset = match.group(1)
  65    return body.decode(charset, errors="replace"), content_type
  66
  67
  68def _strip_html(markup: str) -> str:
  69    parser = _TextExtractor()
  70    parser.feed(markup)
  71    return parser.text()
  72
  73
  74def _duckduckgo_link(raw: str) -> str:
  75    parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(html.unescape(raw))
  76    query = urllib.parse.parse_qs(parsed.query)
  77    if "uddg" in query and query["uddg"]:
  78        return query["uddg"][0]
  79    return html.unescape(raw)
  80
  81
  82def web_search(query: str, *, limit: int = 5) -> dict[str, Any]:
  83    url = "https://duckduckgo.com/html/?" + urllib.parse.urlencode({"q": query})
  84    markup, _ = _request(url)
  85    pattern = re.compile(
  86        r'<a[^>]+class="result__a"[^>]+href="(?P<href>[^"]+)"[^>]*>(?P<title>.*?)</a>',
  87        re.I | re.S,
  88    )
  89    results = []
  90    for match in pattern.finditer(markup):
  91        title = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", match.group("title"))
  92        results.append({"title": html.unescape(title).strip(), "url": _duckduckgo_link(match.group("href"))})
  93        if len(results) >= limit:
  94            break
  95    return {"success": True, "query": query, "results": results}
  96
  97
  98def web_extract(urls: list[str], *, limit_chars: int = 12_000) -> dict[str, Any]:
  99    pages = []
 100    for url in urls[:5]:
 101        try:
 102            body, content_type = _request(url)
 103            text = body if "text/plain" in content_type else _strip_html(body)
 104            reason = anti_bot_reason(url, text[:2000])
 105            page = {
 106                "url": url,
 107                "content_type": content_type,
 108                "text": text[:limit_chars],
 109                "truncated": len(text) > limit_chars,
 110            }
 111            if reason:
 112                page["source_warning"] = reason
 113                page["warnings"] = [{
 114                    "type": "anti_bot",
 115                    "message": reason,
 116                    "guidance": "This page may require normal human browser verification. Do not bypass protections.",
 117                }]
 118            pages.append(page)
 119        except Exception as exc:
 120            pages.append({"url": url, "error": str(exc)})
 121    return {"success": True, "pages": pages}
nipux_cli/worker.py 3324 lines 
   1"""Bounded worker loop for one restartable agent step."""
   2
   3from __future__ import annotations
   4
   5import json
   6import re
   7from dataclasses import dataclass
   8from datetime import datetime, timezone
   9from typing import Any
  10from urllib.parse import urlparse
  11
  12from nipux_cli.artifacts import ArtifactStore
  13from nipux_cli.config import AppConfig, load_config
  14from nipux_cli.compression import refresh_memory_index
  15from nipux_cli.db import AgentDB
  16from nipux_cli.llm import LLMResponse, LLMResponseError, OpenAIChatLLM, StepLLM
  17from nipux_cli.metric_format import format_metric_value
  18from nipux_cli.operator_context import (
  19    active_prompt_operator_entries,
  20    inactive_prompt_operator_ids,
  21    operator_entry_is_prompt_relevant,
  22)
  23from nipux_cli.progress import build_progress_checkpoint
  24from nipux_cli.source_quality import anti_bot_reason
  25from nipux_cli.tools import DEFAULT_REGISTRY, ToolContext, ToolRegistry
  26
  27
  28REFLECTION_INTERVAL_STEPS = 12
  29WORKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION = "2026-05-01-contract-first-v1"
  30
  31SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a long-running local work agent.
  32
  33Operate as a bounded worker, not a chat assistant. Choose one useful next step,
  34call one of the available tools, and persist important evidence as artifacts.
  35Do not claim the whole job is complete. A strong result is only a checkpoint:
  36save it, report it, add the next tasks, and continue improving or broadening.
  37
  38Use a contract-first durable cycle. Read the objective, operator context,
  39roadmap, active task, and recent evidence; choose the next action that satisfies
  40the active output contract; produce or measure concrete evidence; update the
  41right ledger; report the checkpoint; then open or continue the next branch.
  42Research is only one possible contract. For action, experiment, monitor, report,
  43or file-deliverable work, prefer execution, measurement, validation, or writing
  44over more background collection. Keep moving forever until the operator pauses
  45or cancels the job.
  46The worker must not mark jobs completed or failed; use record_tasks,
  47record_lesson, report_update, and artifacts to describe checkpoints, blockers,
  48and next branches while the job stays runnable.
  49
  50Avoid loops. Do not repeat the same search query or the same exact tool call.
  51If search results already exist, move forward by extracting source pages,
  52opening a useful site in the browser, or saving a finding/evidence artifact.
  53If a page has already been extracted and contains useful evidence, save that
  54evidence with write_artifact before doing more searching or browsing.
  55Only click or type browser refs from the most recent successful browser snapshot
  56or navigation result. If a click/type fails with an unknown ref, use the fresh
  57recovery snapshot or call browser_snapshot before retrying.
  58If a source shows Cloudflare, login, paywall, or anti-bot verification, keep it
  59visible in the trace. Do not bypass protections. Continue with normal visible
  60browser actions when possible, persist what you have, or use alternate public
  61sources if stuck.
  62If a browser page says blocked, CAPTCHA, bot check, login required, paywall, or
  63anti-bot, treat that page as a failed/low-yield source for the current job. Do
  64not write an artifact that claims usable evidence exists unless the evidence is
  65actually visible. Record the source outcome or pivot to another public source.
  66Use report_update for short operator-readable progress notes when you need to
  67say what you found or why you are blocked. Do not use report_update instead of
  68write_artifact when you have durable evidence, findings, or report content to save.
  69Use write_file when the objective requires a concrete file deliverable, source
  70file, document, config, dataset, or other workspace output. If a measured
  71experiment says the next action is to write, merge, update, compile, or insert
  72content, prefer write_file or an execution command that actually changes the
  73target over more read-only inspection.
  74Use defer_job when the next useful step is to wait for an external process,
  75scheduled check, cooldown, long-running command, or monitor interval. Do not
  76simulate waiting with repeated searches, reports, or shell probes.
  77Use record_lesson when you learn something that should change future behavior:
  78bad source patterns, task-specific success criteria, repeated mistakes, operator
  79preferences, or a better strategy. Keep lessons short and reusable.
  80Use record_source when a source is high-yield, low-yield, blocked, repetitive,
  81or otherwise useful to score for future behavior.
  82Use record_findings after finding durable candidates, facts, opportunities,
  83experiments, files, bugs, sources, or other reusable outputs. Dedupe against the
  84finding ledger and artifacts before saving.
  85Use record_tasks to maintain a durable queue of objective-neutral branches:
  86open work, active branch, blocked branch, completed branch, and skipped branch.
  87Each task should include an output_contract (research, artifact, experiment,
  88action, monitor, decision, or report), acceptance criteria, evidence needed,
  89and stall behavior so progress is judged by evidence, not activity volume.
  90When the job is broad or starts looping, split it into tasks and move to the
  91highest-priority open task rather than staying on one source or tactic forever.
  92Use record_roadmap for broad, multi-phase, or ambiguous objectives that need a
  93higher-level orchestration plan. A roadmap is generic: milestones group related
  94features or work units; each milestone has acceptance criteria, evidence needed,
  95and a validation contract. Use record_milestone_validation at milestone checkpoints
  96to pass, fail, block, or create follow-up tasks from validation gaps. Keep the
  97roadmap compact and update it from durable evidence, not from activity count.
  98Use record_experiment for measurable trials, benchmarks, comparisons,
  99optimization attempts, or hypothesis tests. A saved note, source, or artifact is
 100not enough progress for a measurable objective: record the exact configuration,
 101metric, result, whether higher or lower is better, and the next experiment. Keep
 102improving against the best observed result instead of declaring victory after a
 103single measurement.
 104Use shell_exec for command-line work, repository inspection, diagnostics,
 105benchmarks, repeatable experiments, and other command execution that the
 106objective requires. Prefer small read-only probes before changing anything, use
 107explicit timeouts, and save important command output with write_artifact before
 108continuing. Do not run destructive or high-risk cyber commands.
 109read_artifact only reads saved Nipux artifacts. Use shell_exec for repository,
 110workspace, project, or filesystem files that are not saved artifacts.
 111write_file writes workspace/local files directly; write_artifact writes Nipux's
 112separate saved-output store. Use the right one for the operator-facing result.
 113Operator messages are durable context from the human operator. Messages marked
 114steer are active constraints until acknowledged or superseded. Messages marked
 115follow_up are lower-priority queued work; keep them in the task queue and act on
 116them after the current active branch has a durable checkpoint. Messages marked
 117note are durable preferences. Use acknowledge_operator_context only after you
 118have incorporated or intentionally superseded a steer/follow_up message.
 119"""
 120
 121INFORMATION_GATHERING_TOOLS = {
 122    "browser_back",
 123    "browser_click",
 124    "browser_console",
 125    "browser_navigate",
 126    "browser_press",

 190}

 392
 393
 394@dataclass(frozen=True)
 395class StepExecution:
 396    job_id: str
 397    run_id: str
 398    step_id: str
 399    tool_name: str | None
 400    status: str
 401    result: dict[str, Any]
 402
 403
 404def build_messages(
 405    job: dict[str, Any],
 406    recent_steps: list[dict[str, Any]],
 407    memory_entries: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
 408    program_text: str = "",
 409    timeline_events: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
 410    active_operator_messages: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
 411    include_unclaimed_operator_messages: bool = True,
 412) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
 413    step_lines = []
 414    for step in recent_steps[-RECENT_STATE_STEPS:]:
 415        step_lines.append(_clip_text(_format_step_for_prompt(step), 720))
 416    state = _clip_text("\n".join(step_lines), RECENT_STATE_PROMPT_CHARS) if step_lines else "No prior steps."
 417    memory_lines = []
 418    for entry in _memory_entries_for_prompt(memory_entries or []):
 419        refs = ", ".join((entry.get("artifact_refs") or [])[:8])
 420        suffix = f"\nArtifact refs: {refs}" if refs else ""
 421        memory_lines.append(
 422            _clip_text(f"### {entry.get('key') or 'memory'}\n{entry.get('summary') or ''}{suffix}", MEMORY_ENTRY_PROMPT_CHARS)
 423        )
 424    memory_text = _clip_text("\n\n".join(memory_lines), MEMORY_PROMPT_CHARS) if memory_lines else "No compact memory yet."
 425    program = _clip_text(program_text.strip(), PROGRAM_PROMPT_CHARS) if program_text else "No program.md saved yet."
 426    operator_messages = _operator_messages_for_prompt(
 427        job,
 428        active_messages=active_operator_messages or [],
 429        include_unclaimed=include_unclaimed_operator_messages,
 430    )
 431    measurement_obligation = _measurement_obligation_for_prompt(job)
 432    measured_progress_guard = _measured_progress_guard_for_prompt(job, recent_steps)
 433    progress_accounting_guard = _progress_accounting_for_prompt(recent_steps)
 434    activity_stagnation = _activity_stagnation_for_prompt(job)
 435    lessons = _lessons_for_prompt(job)
 436    roadmap = _roadmap_for_prompt(job)
 437    tasks = _tasks_for_prompt(job)
 438    ledgers = _ledgers_for_prompt(job)
 439    experiments = _experiments_for_prompt(job)
 440    reflections = _reflections_for_prompt(job)
 441    timeline = _timeline_for_prompt(timeline_events or [])
 442    next_constraint = _next_action_constraint(job, recent_steps)
 443    content = _render_worker_prompt(
 444        job,
 445        sections=[
 446            (
 447                "Workspace",
 448                "\n".join([
 449                    "- shell_exec runs on the machine hosting this Nipux worker, in the current worker directory unless the command changes it",
 450                    "- saved artifacts are separate Nipux outputs; read_artifact is only for those saved outputs",
 451                    "- use shell_exec for workspace/project files unless the file is a saved artifact",
 452                ]),
 453            ),
 454            ("Operator context", operator_messages),
 455            ("Pending measurement obligation", measurement_obligation),
 456            ("Measured progress guard", measured_progress_guard),
 457            ("Progress accounting guard", progress_accounting_guard),
 458            ("Activity stagnation", activity_stagnation),
 459            ("Program", program),
 460            ("Lessons learned", lessons),
 461            ("Roadmap", roadmap),
 462            ("Task queue", tasks),
 463            ("Ledgers", ledgers),
 464            ("Experiment ledger", experiments),
 465            ("Reflections", reflections),
 466            ("Compact memory", memory_text),
 467            ("Recent visible timeline", timeline),
 468            ("Recent state", state),
 469            ("Next-action constraint", next_constraint),
 470        ],
 471    )
 472    return [
 473        {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
 474        {"role": "user", "content": content},
 475    ]
 476
 477
 478def _memory_entries_for_prompt(memory_entries: list[dict[str, Any]], *, limit: int = 2) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
 479    entries = [entry for entry in memory_entries if isinstance(entry, dict)]
 480    rolling = next((entry for entry in entries if entry.get("key") == "rolling_state"), None)
 481    selected: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 482    if rolling:
 483        selected.append(rolling)
 484    for entry in entries:
 485        if len(selected) >= limit:
 486            break
 487        if rolling is not None and entry is rolling:
 488            continue
 489        selected.append(entry)
 490    return selected[:limit]
 491
 492
 493def _render_worker_prompt(job: dict[str, Any], *, sections: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> str:
 494    objective = _clip_text(job.get("objective") or "", 2_000)
 495    header = f"Job: {job['title']}\nKind: {job['kind']}\nObjective:\n{objective}"
 496    instruction = (
 497        "Take exactly one bounded next action. If recent state contains search results, do not search the same query again. "
 498        "If recent state contains extracted page evidence, write an artifact before doing more search or browsing."
 499    )
 500    scale = 1.0
 501    while True:
 502        parts = [header]
 503        for title, body in sections:
